text.KJV: And it happened, after that Alexander son of Philip, the Macedonian, who came out of the land of Chettiim, had smitten Darius king of the Persians and Medes, that he reigned in his stead, the first over Greece,
text.NABRE: I. Crisis and Response Chapter 1 From Alexander to Antiochus. After Alexander the Macedonian, Philip’s son, who came from the land of Kittim, had defeated Darius, king of the Persians and Medes, he became king in his place, having first ruled in Greece.
text.NRSV: Alexander the Great 1
text.DRB: And it came to pass, after that Alexander Philip's son the Macedonian, that first reigned in Greece, being gone out of the land of Cethim, struck Darius king of the Persians and the Medes:
he made many battles, and obtained the munition of all, & slew the kings of the earth:
text.KJV: And made many wars, and won many strong holds, and slew the kings of the earth,
text.NABRE: He fought many battles, captured fortresses, and put the kings of the earth to death.
text.NRSV: He fought many battles, conquered strongholds, and put to death the kings of the earth.

text.DRB: and he passed through even to the ends of the earth: and took the spoils of the multitude of the Gentiles: & the earth was silent in his sight.
text.KJV: And went through to the ends of the earth, and took spoils of many nations, insomuch that the earth was quiet before him; whereupon he was exalted and his heart was lifted up.
text.NABRE: He advanced to the ends of the earth, gathering plunder from many nations; the earth fell silent before him, and his heart became proud and arrogant.
text.NRSV: He advanced to the ends of the earth, and plundered many nations. When the earth became quiet before him, he was exalted, and his heart was lifted up.

text.DRB: And he gathered power, and an army exceeding strong: and his heart was exalted and elevated:
text.KJV: And he gathered a mighty strong host and ruled over countries, and nations, and kings, who became tributaries unto him.
text.NABRE: He collected a very strong army and won dominion over provinces, nations, and rulers, and they paid him tribute.
text.NRSV: He gathered a very strong army and ruled over countries, nations, and princes, and they became tributary to him.

text.DRB: and he obtained the regions of the Gentiles, & the tyrants: and they were made tributaries to him.
text.KJV: And after these things he fell sick, and perceived that he should die.
text.NABRE: But after all this he took to his bed, realizing that he was going to die.
text.NRSV: After this he fell sick and perceived that he was dying.

text.DRB: And after these things, he fell down in his bed, and he knew that he should die.
text.KJV: Wherefore he called his servants, such as were honourable, and had been brought up with him from his youth, and parted his kingdom among them, while he was yet alive.
text.NABRE: So he summoned his noblest officers, who had been brought up with him from his youth, and divided his kingdom among them while he was still alive.
text.NRSV: So he summoned his most honoured officers, who had been brought up with him from youth, and divided his kingdom among them while he was still alive.

text.KJV: So Alexander reigned twelves years, and then died.
text.NABRE: Alexander had reigned twelve years when he died.
text.NRSV: And after Alexander had reigned for twelve years, he died.
text.DRB: And he called his servants the Nobles, that were brought up with him from his youth: & he divided his kingdom to them, when he yet lived.
And Alexander reigned twelve years, & he died.
text.KJV: And his servants bare rule every one in his place.
text.NABRE: So his officers took over his kingdom, each in his own territory,
text.NRSV: Then his officers began to rule, each in his own place.

text.DRB: And his servants possessed the kingdom, every one in his place:
text.KJV: And after his death they all put crowns upon themselves; so did their sons after them many years: and evils were multiplied in the earth.
text.NABRE: and after his death they all put on diadems, and so did their sons after them for many years, multiplying evils on the earth.
text.NRSV: They all put on crowns after his death, and so did their descendants after them for many years; and they caused many evils on the earth.

text.KJV: And there came out of them a wicked root Antiochus surnamed Epiphanes, son of Antiochus the king, who had been an hostage at Rome, and he reigned in the hundred and thirty and seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.
text.NABRE: There sprang from these a sinful offshoot, Antiochus Epiphanes, son of King Antiochus, once a hostage at Rome. He became king in the one hundred and thirty-seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.
text.NRSV: Antiochus Epiphanes and Renegade Jews From them came forth a sinful root, Antiochus Epiphanes, son of King Antiochus; he had been a hostage in Rome. He began to reign in the one hundred and thirty-seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.
text.DRB: and they did all put crowns on them after his death, & their sons after them many years, & evils were multiplied in the earth.

text.KJV: In those days went there out of Israel wicked men, who persuaded many, saying, Let us go and make a covenant with the heathen that are round about us: for since we departed from them we have had much sorrow.
text.NABRE: Lawless Jews. In those days there appeared in Israel transgressors of the law who seduced many, saying: “Let us go and make a covenant with the Gentiles all around us; since we separated from them, many evils have come upon us.”
text.NRSV: In those days certain renegades came out from Israel and misled many, saying, ‘Let us go and make a covenant with the Gentiles around us, for since we separated from them many disasters have come upon us.’
text.DRB: And there came forth of them a sinful root Antiochus Illustre, the son of king Antiochus, that had been at Rome an hostage: & he reigned in the hundredth & seven & thirtieth year of the kingdom of the Greeks.

In those days there went forth of Israel wicked children, & persuaded many, saying: Let us go, and make a covenant with the Gentiles, that are about us: because since we departed from them many evils have found us.
text.KJV: So this device pleased them well.
text.NABRE: The proposal was agreeable;
text.NRSV: This proposal pleased them,

text.DRB: And the talk seemed good in their eyes.
text.KJV: Then certain of the people were so forward herein, that they went to the king, who gave them licence to do after the ordinances of the heathen:
text.NABRE: some from among the people promptly went to the king, and he authorized them to introduce the ordinances of the Gentiles.
text.NRSV: and some of the people eagerly went to the king, who authorized them to observe the ordinances of the Gentiles.

text.DRB: And some of the people determined, & went to the king: & he gave them leave to do the justice of the Gentiles.
text.KJV: Whereupon they built a place of exercise at Jerusalem according to the customs of the heathen:
text.NABRE: Thereupon they built a gymnasium in Jerusalem according to the Gentile custom.
text.NRSV: So they built a gymnasium in Jerusalem, according to Gentile custom,

text.DRB: And they built a school in Jerusalem, according to the laws of the Nations:
text.KJV: And made themselves uncircumcised, and forsook the holy covenant, and joined themselves to the heathen, and were sold to do mischief.
text.NABRE: They disguised their circumcision and abandoned the holy covenant; they allied themselves with the Gentiles and sold themselves to wrongdoing.
text.NRSV: and removed the marks of circumcision, and abandoned the holy covenant. They joined with the Gentiles and sold themselves to do evil.

text.DRB: and they made to themselves prepuces, and revolted from the holy testament, and were joined to the Nations, and were sold to do evil.
text.KJV: Now when the kingdom was established before Antiochus, he thought to reign over Egypt that he might have the dominion of two realms.
text.NABRE: Antiochus in Egypt. When his kingdom seemed secure, Antiochus undertook to become king of the land of Egypt and to rule over both kingdoms.
text.NRSV: Antiochus in Egypt When Antiochus saw that his kingdom was established, he determined to become king of the land of Egypt, in order that he might reign over both kingdoms.

text.DRB: And the kingdom was prepared in the sight of Antiochus, and he began to reign in the land of Ægypt, that he might reign over two kingdoms.
text.KJV: Wherefore he entered into Egypt with a great multitude, with chariots, and elephants, and horsemen, and a great navy,
text.NABRE: He invaded Egypt with a strong force, with chariots, elephants and cavalry, and with a large fleet,
text.NRSV: So he invaded Egypt with a strong force, with chariots and elephants and cavalry and with a large fleet.

text.DRB: And he entered into Ægypt with great multitude, with chariots, and elephants, & horsemen, and a copious multitude of ships:
text.KJV: And made war against Ptolemee king of Egypt: but Ptolemee was afraid of him, and fled; and many were wounded to death.
text.NABRE: to make war on Ptolemy, king of Egypt. Ptolemy was frightened at his presence and fled, and many were wounded and fell dead.
text.NRSV: He engaged King Ptolemy of Egypt in battle, and Ptolemy turned and fled before him, and many were wounded and fell.

text.DRB: And he made war against Ptolomee the king of Ægypt, & Ptolomee was afraid at his presence, and fled, and many fell wounded.
text.KJV: Thus they got the strong cities in the land of Egypt and he took the spoils thereof.
text.NABRE: The fortified cities in the land of Egypt were captured, and Antiochus plundered the land of Egypt.
text.NRSV: They captured the fortified cities in the land of Egypt, and he plundered the land of Egypt.

text.KJV: And after that Antiochus had smitten Egypt, he returned again in the hundred forty and third year, and went up against Israel and Jerusalem with a great multitude,
text.NABRE: Robbery of the Temple. After Antiochus had defeated Egypt in the one hundred and forty-third year, he returned and went up against Israel and against Jerusalem with a strong force.
text.NRSV: Persecution of the Jews After subduing Egypt, Antiochus returned in the one hundred and forty-third year. He went up against Israel and came to Jerusalem with a strong force.
text.DRB: And he took the fenced cities in the land of Ægypt: and he took the spoils of the land of Ægypt.

text.KJV: And entered proudly into the sanctuary, and took away the golden altar, and the candlestick of light, and all the vessels thereof,
text.NABRE: He insolently entered the sanctuary and took away the golden altar, the lampstand for the light with all its utensils,
text.NRSV: He arrogantly entered the sanctuary and took the golden altar, the lampstand for the light, and all its utensils.
text.DRB: And Antiochus turned, after he struck Ægypt in the hundredth and three and fortieth year: and he went up to Israel,
and went up to Jerusalem with a great multitude.
text.KJV: And the table of the shewbread, and the pouring vessels, and the vials. and the censers of gold, and the veil, and the crown, and the golden ornaments that were before the temple, all which he pulled off.
text.NABRE: the offering table, the cups and bowls, the golden censers, and the curtain. The cornices and the golden ornament on the facade of the temple—he stripped it all off.
text.NRSV: He took also the table for the bread of the Presence, the cups for drink-offerings, the bowls, the golden censers, the curtain, the crowns, and the gold decoration on the front of the temple; he stripped it all off.

text.DRB: And he entered into the sanctification with pride, & took the golden altar, & the candlestick of light, & all the vessels thereof, & the table of proposition, and the libatories, and the phials, and the little mortars of gold, and the veil, & the crowns, and the golden ornament, that was in the face of the temple: & he brake all into pieces.
text.KJV: He took also the silver and the gold, and the precious vessels: also he took the hidden treasures which he found.
text.NABRE: And he took away the silver and gold and the precious vessels; he also took all the hidden treasures he could find.
text.NRSV: He took the silver and the gold, and the costly vessels; he took also the hidden treasures that he found.

text.DRB: And he took the silver and gold, & the desiderable vessels: and he took the hidden treasures which he found: & carrying away he departed into his own land.
text.KJV: And when he had taken all away, he went into his own land, having made a great massacre, and spoken very proudly.
text.NABRE: Taking all this, he went back to his own country. He shed much blood and spoke with great arrogance.
text.NRSV: Taking them all, he went into his own land. He shed much blood, and spoke with great arrogance.

text.DRB: And he made a slaughter of men, and spake in great pride.
text.KJV: Therefore there was a great mourning in Israel, in every place where they were;
text.NABRE: And there was great mourning throughout all Israel,
text.NRSV: Israel mourned deeply in every community,

text.DRB: And great lamentation was made in Israel, and in every place of theirs:
text.KJV: So that the princes and elders mourned, the virgins and young men were made feeble, and the beauty of women was changed.
text.NABRE: and the rulers and the elders groaned. Young women and men languished, and the beauty of the women faded.
text.NRSV: young women and young men became faint, the beauty of the women faded.

text.DRB: and the princes, and the ancients mourned, and the young men, and the virgins were weakened, & the beautifulness of the women was changed.
text.KJV: Every bridegroom took up lamentation, and she that sat in the marriage chamber was in heaviness,
text.NABRE: Every bridegroom took up lamentation, while the bride sitting in her chamber mourned,
text.NRSV: Every bridegroom took up the lament; she who sat in the bridal chamber was mourning.

text.DRB: Every husband took lamentation: and the women that sat in the marriage bed mourned:
text.KJV: The land also was moved for the inhabitants thereof, and all the house of Jacob was covered with confusion.
text.NABRE: And the land quaked on account of its inhabitants, and all the house of Jacob was clothed with shame.
text.NRSV: Even the land trembled for its inhabitants, and all the house of Jacob was clothed with shame.

text.DRB: and the land was moved upon the inhabitants therein, & all the house of Jacob did put on confusion.
text.KJV: And after two years fully expired the king sent his chief collector of tribute unto the cities of Juda, who came unto Jerusalem with a great multitude,
text.NABRE: Attack and Occupation. Two years later, the king sent the Mysian commander to the cities of Judah, and he came to Jerusalem with a strong force.
text.NRSV: The Occupation of Jerusalem Two years later the king sent to the cities of Judah a chief collector of tribute, and he came to Jerusalem with a large force.

text.DRB: And after two years of days the king sent a Prince of tributes into the cities of Juda, and he came to Jerusalem with a great multitude.
text.KJV: And spake peaceable words unto them, but all was deceit: for when they had given him credence, he fell suddenly upon the city, and smote it very sore, and destroyed much people of Israel.
text.NABRE: He spoke to them deceitfully in peaceful terms, and they believed him. Then he attacked the city suddenly, in a great onslaught, and destroyed many of the people in Israel.
text.NRSV: Deceitfully he spoke peaceable words to them, and they believed him; but he suddenly fell upon the city, dealt it a severe blow, and destroyed many people of Israel.

text.DRB: And he spake unto them peaceable words in guile: and they believed him.
text.KJV: And when he had taken the spoils of the city, he set it on fire, and pulled down the houses and walls thereof on every side.
text.NABRE: He plundered the city and set fire to it, demolished its houses and its surrounding walls.
text.NRSV: He plundered the city, burned it with fire, and tore down its houses and its surrounding walls.

text.DRB: And he fell upon the city suddenly, and struck it with a great plague, & destroyed much people in Israel.
text.KJV: But the women and children took they captive, and possessed the cattle.
text.NABRE: And they took captive the women and children, and seized the animals.
text.NRSV: They took captive the women and children, and seized the livestock.

text.DRB: And he took the spoils of the city, and burnt it with fire, and destroyed the houses thereof, and the walls thereof round about:
text.KJV: Then builded they the city of David with a great and strong wall, and with mighty towers, and made it a strong hold for them.
text.NABRE: Then they built up the City of David with a high, strong wall and strong towers, and it became their citadel.
text.NRSV: Then they fortified the city of David with a great strong wall and strong towers, and it became their citadel.

text.DRB: and they led the women captive, and the children, and the cattle they possessed.
text.KJV: And they put therein a sinful nation, wicked men, and fortified themselves therein.
text.NABRE: There they installed a sinful race, transgressors of the law, who fortified themselves inside it.
text.NRSV: They stationed there a sinful people, men who were renegades. These strengthened their position;

text.DRB: And they built the city of David with a great wall, and a strong, and with firm towers, and it was made a castle for them:
text.KJV: They stored it also with armour and victuals, and when they had gathered together the spoils of Jerusalem, they laid them up there, and so they became a sore snare:
text.NABRE: They stored up weapons and provisions, depositing there the plunder they had collected from Jerusalem, and they became a great snare.
text.NRSV: they stored up arms and food, and collecting the spoils of Jerusalem they stored them there, and became a great menace,

text.DRB: and they placed there a sinful nation, wicked men, & they waxed strong therein: And they laid armour, and victuals, and gathered together the spoils of Jerusalem:
text.KJV: For it was a place to lie in wait against the sanctuary, and an evil adversary to Israel.
text.NABRE: The citadel became an ambush against the sanctuary, and a wicked adversary to Israel at all times.
text.NRSV: for the citadel became an ambush against the sanctuary, an evil adversary of Israel at all times.

text.DRB: and laid them up there: & they became a great snare.
text.KJV: Thus they shed innocent blood on every side of the sanctuary, and defiled it:
text.NABRE: They shed innocent blood around the sanctuary; they defiled the sanctuary.
text.NRSV: On every side of the sanctuary they shed innocent blood; they even defiled the sanctuary.

text.DRB: And this was made for an embushment of the sanctification, and to be an ill devil in Israel.
text.KJV: Insomuch that the inhabitants of Jerusalem fled because of them: whereupon the city was made an habitation of strangers, and became strange to those that were born in her; and her own children left her.
text.NABRE: Because of them the inhabitants of Jerusalem fled away, she became the abode of strangers. She became a stranger to her own offspring, and her children forsook her.
text.NRSV: Because of them the residents of Jerusalem fled; she became a dwelling of strangers; she became strange to her offspring, and her children forsook her.

text.DRB: And they shed innocent blood round about the sanctification, and contaminated the sanctification.
text.KJV: Her sanctuary was laid waste like a wilderness, her feasts were turned into mourning, her sabbaths into reproach her honour into contempt.
text.NABRE: Her sanctuary became desolate as a wilderness; her feasts were turned into mourning, Her sabbaths to shame, her honor to contempt.
text.NRSV: Her sanctuary became desolate like a desert; her feasts were turned into mourning, her sabbaths into a reproach, her honour into contempt.

text.DRB: And the inhabitants of Jerusalem fled by reason of them, and it became the habitation of strangers, and she became stranger to her own seed, and her children forsook her.
text.KJV: As had been her glory, so was her dishonour increased, and her excellency was turned into mourning.
text.NABRE: As her glory had been, so great was her dishonor: her exaltation was turned into mourning.
text.NRSV: Her dishonour now grew as great as her glory; her exaltation was turned into mourning.

text.KJV: Moreover king Antiochus wrote to his whole kingdom, that all should be one people,
text.NABRE: Religious Persecution. Then the king wrote to his whole kingdom that all should be one people,
text.NRSV: Installation of Gentile Cults Then the king wrote to his whole kingdom that all should be one people,
text.DRB: Her sanctification was desolate as a wilderness, her festival days were turned into mourning, her sabbaths into reproach, her honours into naught.

text.KJV: And every one should leave his laws: so all the heathen agreed according to the commandment of the king.
text.NABRE: and abandon their particular customs. All the Gentiles conformed to the command of the king,
text.NRSV: and that all should give up their particular customs.
text.DRB: According to her glory was her ignominy multiplied, and her highness was turned into mourning.
And king Antiochus wrote to all his kingdom, that all the people should be one: and every one should leave his own law.
text.KJV: Yea, many also of the Israelites consented to his religion, and sacrificed unto idols, and profaned the sabbath.
text.NABRE: and many Israelites delighted in his religion; they sacrificed to idols and profaned the sabbath.
text.NRSV: All the Gentiles accepted the command of the king. Many even from Israel gladly adopted his religion; they sacrificed to idols and profaned the sabbath.

text.DRB: And all Nations consented according to the word of king Antiochus,
text.KJV: For the king had sent letters by messengers unto Jerusalem and the cities of Juda that they should follow the strange laws of the land,
text.NABRE: The king sent letters by messenger to Jerusalem and to the cities of Judah, ordering them to follow customs foreign to their land;
text.NRSV: And the king sent letters by messengers to Jerusalem and the towns of Judah; he directed them to follow customs strange to the land,

text.DRB: & many of Israel consented to his service, & they sacrificed to idols, & defiled the sabbath.
text.KJV: And forbid burnt offerings, and sacrifice, and drink offerings, in the temple; and that they should profane the sabbaths and festival days:
text.NABRE: to prohibit burnt offerings, sacrifices, and libations in the sanctuary, to profane the sabbaths and feast days,
text.NRSV: to forbid burnt-offerings and sacrifices and drink-offerings in the sanctuary, to profane sabbaths and festivals,

text.DRB: And the king sent books by the hands of messengers into Jerusalem, & into all the cities of Juda: that they should follow the law of the Nations of the earth,
text.KJV: And pollute the sanctuary and holy people:
text.NABRE: to desecrate the sanctuary and the sacred ministers,
text.NRSV: to defile the sanctuary and the priests,

text.DRB: and should prohibit holocausts & sacrifices, & placations to be made in the temple of God,
text.KJV: Set up altars, and groves, and chapels of idols, and sacrifice swine’s flesh, and unclean beasts:
text.NABRE: to build pagan altars and temples and shrines, to sacrifice swine and unclean animals,
text.NRSV: to build altars and sacred precincts and shrines for idols, to sacrifice swine and other unclean animals,

text.DRB: and should prohibit the sabbath to be celebrated, & the solemn days.
text.KJV: That they should also leave their children uncircumcised, and make their souls abominable with all manner of uncleanness and profanation:
text.NABRE: to leave their sons uncircumcised, and to defile themselves with every kind of impurity and abomination;
text.NRSV: and to leave their sons uncircumcised. They were to make themselves abominable by everything unclean and profane,

text.DRB: And he commanded the holy places to be defiled, & the holy people of Israel.
text.KJV: To the end they might forget the law, and change all the ordinances.
text.NABRE: so that they might forget the law and change all its ordinances.
text.NRSV: so that they would forget the law and change all the ordinances.

text.DRB: And he commanded altars to be built, and temples, and idols, & swine's flesh to be immolated, and common beasts,
text.KJV: And whosoever would not do according to the commandment of the king, he said, he should die.
text.NABRE: Whoever refused to act according to the command of the king was to be put to death.
text.NRSV: He added, ‘And whoever does not obey the command of the king shall die.’

text.DRB: and to leave their children uncircumcised, and their souls to be contaminated in all uncleannesses, and abominations, so that they should forget the law, and should change all the justifications of God.
text.KJV: In the selfsame manner wrote he to his whole kingdom, and appointed overseers over all the people, commanding the cities of Juda to sacrifice, city by city.
text.NABRE: In words such as these he wrote to his whole kingdom. He appointed inspectors over all the people, and he ordered the cities of Judah to offer sacrifices, each city in turn.
text.NRSV: In such words he wrote to his whole kingdom. He appointed inspectors over all the people and commanded the towns of Judah to offer sacrifice, town by town.

text.DRB: And whosoever had not done according to the word of king Antiochus, they should die.
text.KJV: Then many of the people were gathered unto them, to wit every one that forsook the law; and so they committed evils in the land;
text.NABRE: Many of the people, those who abandoned the law, joined them and committed evil in the land.
text.NRSV: Many of the people, everyone who forsook the law, joined them, and they did evil in the land;

text.DRB: According to all these words wrote he to all his kingdom: & he appointed princes over the people, that should force these things to be done.
text.KJV: And drove the Israelites into secret places, even wheresoever they could flee for succour.
text.NABRE: They drove Israel into hiding, wherever places of refuge could be found.
text.NRSV: they drove Israel into hiding in every place of refuge they had.

text.KJV: Now the fifteenth day of the month Casleu, in the hundred forty and fifth year, they set up the abomination of desolation upon the altar, and builded idol altars throughout the cities of Juda on every side;
text.NABRE: On the fifteenth day of the month Kislev, in the year one hundred and forty-five, the king erected the desolating abomination upon the altar of burnt offerings, and in the surrounding cities of Judah they built pagan altars.
text.NRSV: Now on the fifteenth day of Chislev, in the one hundred and forty-fifth year, they erected a desolating sacrilege on the altar of burnt-offering. They also built altars in the surrounding towns of Judah,
text.DRB: And they commanded the cities of Juda to sacrifice.
And many of the people were gathered to them, they that had forsaken the law of our Lord: and they did evils upon the land:
text.KJV: And burnt incense at the doors of their houses, and in the streets.
text.NABRE: They also burned incense at the doors of houses and in the streets.
text.NRSV: and offered incense at the doors of the houses and in the streets.

text.DRB: and they chased forth the people of Israel in hidden corners, and in the secret places of fugitives.
text.KJV: And when they had rent in pieces the books of the law which they found, they burnt them with fire.
text.NABRE: Any scrolls of the law that they found they tore up and burned.
text.NRSV: The books of the law that they found they tore to pieces and burned with fire.

text.DRB: The fifteenth day of the month Casleu, the hundredth five and fortieth year king Antiochus built the abominable idol of desolation upon the altar of God, and throughout all the cities of Juda round about they builded altars:
text.KJV: And whosoever was found with any the book of the testament, or if any committed to the law, the king’s commandment was, that they should put him to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever was found with a scroll of the covenant, and whoever observed the law, was condemned to death by royal decree.
text.NRSV: Anyone found possessing the book of the covenant, or anyone who adhered to the law, was condemned to death by decree of the king.

text.DRB: and before the gates of houses, and in the streets they burnt frankincense, and sacrificed.
text.KJV: Thus did they by their authority unto the Israelites every month, to as many as were found in the cities.
text.NABRE: So they used their power against Israel, against those who were caught, each month, in the cities.
text.NRSV: They kept using violence against Israel, against those who were found month after month in the towns.

text.DRB: and the books of the law of God they burnt with fire, cutting them:
text.KJV: Now the five and twentieth day of the month they did sacrifice upon the idol altar, which was upon the altar of God.
text.NABRE: On the twenty-fifth day of each month they sacrificed on the pagan altar that was over the altar of burnt offerings.
text.NRSV: On the twenty-fifth day of the month they offered sacrifice on the altar that was on top of the altar of burnt-offering.

text.DRB: and with whomsoever were found the books of the testament of our Lord, and whomsoever observed the law of our Lord, they murdered him, according to the edict of the king.
text.KJV: At which time according to the commandment they put to death certain women, that had caused their children to be circumcised.
text.NABRE: In keeping with the decree, they put to death women who had their children circumcised,
text.NRSV: In accordance with the decree, they put to death the women who had their children circumcised,

text.DRB: In their power did they these things to the people of Israel, that was found in every month & month in the cities:
text.KJV: And they hanged the infants about their necks, and rifled their houses, and slew them that had circumcised them.
text.NABRE: and they hung their babies from their necks; their families also and those who had circumcised them were killed.
text.NRSV: and their families and those who circumcised them; and they hung the infants from their mothers’ necks.

text.KJV: Howbeit many in Israel were fully resolved and confirmed in themselves not to eat any unclean thing.
text.NABRE: But many in Israel were determined and resolved in their hearts not to eat anything unclean;
text.NRSV: But many in Israel stood firm and were resolved in their hearts not to eat unclean food.
text.DRB: And the five and twentieth day of the month they sacrificed upon the altar that was against the altar.

text.KJV: Wherefore the rather to die, that they might not be defiled with meats, and that they might not profane the holy covenant: so then they died.
text.NABRE: they preferred to die rather than to be defiled with food or to profane the holy covenant; and they did die.
text.NRSV: They chose to die rather than to be defiled by food or to profane the holy covenant; and they did die.
text.DRB: And the women that circumcised their children, were murdered according to the commandment of king Antiochus,
and they hang up the children by the necks throughout all their houses, and those that had circumcised them, they murdered.
text.KJV: And there was very great wrath upon Israel.
text.NABRE: And very great wrath came upon Israel.
text.NRSV: Very great wrath came upon Israel.

text.DRB: And many of the people of Israel determined with themselves, that they would not eat the unclean things: and they chose rather to die, than to be defiled with unclean meats:
and they would not break the holy law of God, and they were murdered,
and there was made great wrath upon the people exceedingly.

text.KJV: In those days arose Mattathias the son of John, the son of Simeon, a priest of the sons of Joarib, from Jerusalem, and dwelt in Modin.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Mattathias and His Sons. In those days Mattathias, son of John, son of Simeon, a priest of the family of Joarib, left Jerusalem and settled in Modein.
text.NRSV: Mattathias and His Sons 2
text.DRB: In those days arose Mathathias the son of John, the son of Simeon, Priest of the sons of Joarib from Jerusalem, & he sat in the mountain of Modin:

text.KJV: And he had five sons, Joannan, called Caddis:
text.NABRE: He had five sons: John, who was called Gaddi;
text.NRSV: He had five sons, John surnamed Gaddi,
text.DRB: and he had five sons, John who was surnamed Gaddis:

text.KJV: Simon; called Thassi:
text.NABRE: Simon, who was called Thassi;
text.NRSV: Simon called Thassi,
text.DRB: and Simon, who was surnamed Thasi:

text.KJV: Judas, who was called Maccabeus:
text.NABRE: Judas, who was called Maccabeus;
text.NRSV: Judas called Maccabeus,
text.DRB: and Judas, who was called Machabeus:

text.KJV: Eleazar, called Avaran: and Jonathan, whose surname was Apphus.
text.NABRE: Eleazar, who was called Avaran; and Jonathan, who was called Apphus.
text.NRSV: Eleazar called Avaran, and Jonathan called Apphus.
text.DRB: and Eleazar, who was surnamed Abaron: and Jonathan, who was surnamed Apphus.
These saw the evils, that were done in the people of Juda, and in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And when he saw the blasphemies that were committed in Juda and Jerusalem,
text.NABRE: When he saw the sacrileges that were being committed in Judah and in Jerusalem,
text.NRSV: He saw the blasphemies being committed in Judah and Jerusalem,

text.DRB: And Mathathias said: Woe is me, wherefore was I born to see the affliction of my people, and the affliction of the holy city, and to sit there, when it is given in the hands of the enemies?
text.KJV: He said, Woe is me! wherefore was I born to see this misery of my people, and of the holy city, and to dwell there, when it was delivered into the hand of the enemy, and the sanctuary into the hand of strangers?
text.NABRE: he said: “Woe is me! Why was I born to see the ruin of my people, the ruin of the holy city— To dwell there as it was given into the hands of enemies, the sanctuary into the hands of strangers?
text.NRSV: and said, ‘Alas! Why was I born to see this, the ruin of my people, the ruin of the holy city, and to live there when it was given over to the enemy, the sanctuary given over to aliens?

text.DRB: The holy places are come into the hand of strangers: the temple thereof as an ignoble man.
text.KJV: Her temple is become as a man without glory.
text.NABRE: Her temple has become like a man disgraced,
text.NRSV: Her temple has become like a person without honour;

text.DRB: The vessels of her glory are carried away captive: her old men are murdered in the streets, and her young men are fallen by the sword of the enemies.
text.KJV: Her glorious vessels are carried away into captivity, her infants are slain in the streets, her young men with the sword of the enemy.
text.NABRE: her glorious vessels carried off as spoils, Her infants murdered in her streets, her youths by the sword of the enemy.
text.NRSV: Her infants have been killed in her streets, her youths by the sword of the foe.

text.DRB: What nation hath not inherited her kingdom, and hath not obtained her spoils?
text.KJV: What nation hath not had a part in her kingdom and gotten of her spoils?
text.NABRE: What nation has not taken its share of her realm, and laid its hand on her spoils?
text.NRSV: What nation has not inherited her palaces and has not seized her spoils?

text.DRB: All her beauty is taken away. She that was free, is made a servant.
text.KJV: All her ornaments are taken away; of a free woman she is become a bondslave.
text.NABRE: All her adornment has been taken away. Once free, she has become a slave.
text.NRSV: All her adornment has been taken away; no longer free, she has become a slave.

text.DRB: And lo our holies, and our beauty, and our glory is desolate, and the Nations have defiled them.
text.KJV: And, behold, our sanctuary, even our beauty and our glory, is laid waste, and the Gentiles have profaned it.
text.NABRE: We see our sanctuary laid waste, our beauty, our glory. The Gentiles have defiled them!
text.NRSV: And see, our holy place, our beauty, and our glory have been laid waste; the Gentiles have profaned them.

text.DRB: Whereto then is it for us yet to live?
text.KJV: To what end therefore shall we live any longer?
text.NABRE: Why are we still alive?”

text.DRB: And Mathathias rent his garments, and his sons: and they covered themselves with hair-cloths, and lamented exceedingly.
text.KJV: Then Mattathias and his sons rent their clothes, and put on sackcloth, and mourned very sore.
text.NABRE: Then Mattathias and his sons tore their garments, put on sackcloth, and mourned bitterly.
text.NRSV: Then Mattathias and his sons tore their clothes, put on sackcloth, and mourned greatly.

text.KJV: In the mean while the king’s officers, such as compelled the people to revolt, came into the city Modin, to make them sacrifice.
text.NABRE: Pagan Worship Refused and Resisted. The officers of the king in charge of enforcing the apostasy came to the city of Modein to make them sacrifice.
text.NRSV: Pagan Worship Refused The king’s officers who were enforcing the apostasy came to the town of Modein to make them offer sacrifice.
text.DRB: And there came thither they that were sent from king Antiochus, to compel them that were fled into the city of Modin, to immolate and to burn frankincense, & to depart from the law of God.
And many of the people of Israel consenting came to them: but Mathathias, & his sons stood constantly.
text.KJV: And when many of Israel came unto them, Mattathias also and his sons came together.
text.NABRE: Many of Israel joined them, but Mattathias and his sons drew together.
text.NRSV: Many from Israel came to them; and Mattathias and his sons were assembled.

text.DRB: And they that were sent from Antiochus answering said to Mathathias: Thou art the prince, and most honourable, and great in this city, and adorned with sons, and brethren.
text.KJV: Then answered the king’s officers, and said to Mattathias on this wise, Thou art a ruler, and an honourable and great man in this city, and strengthened with sons and brethren:
text.NABRE: Then the officers of the king addressed Mattathias: “You are a leader, an honorable and great man in this city, supported by sons and kindred.
text.NRSV: Then the king’s officers spoke to Mattathias as follows: ‘You are a leader, honoured and great in this town, and supported by sons and brothers.

text.DRB: Therefore come thou first, and do the king's commandment, as all Nations have done, and the men of Juda, and they that are remaining in Jerusalem: & thou shalt be & thy sons among the king's friends, and amplified with gold, and silver, and many gifts.
text.KJV: Now therefore come thou first, and fulfil the king’s commandment, like as all the heathen have done, yea, and the men of Juda also, and such as remain at Jerusalem: so shalt thou and thy house be in the number of the king’s friends, and thou and thy children shall be honoured with silver and gold, and many rewards.
text.NABRE: Come now, be the first to obey the king’s command, as all the Gentiles and Judeans and those who are left in Jerusalem have done. Then you and your sons shall be numbered among the King’s Friends, and you and your sons shall be honored with silver and gold and many gifts.”
text.NRSV: Now be the first to come and do what the king commands, as all the Gentiles and the people of Judah and those that are left in Jerusalem have done. Then you and your sons will be numbered among the Friends of the king, and you and your sons will be honoured with silver and gold and many gifts.’

text.KJV: Then Mattathias answered and spake with a loud voice, Though all the nations that are under the king’s dominion obey him, and fall away every one from the religion of their fathers, and give consent to his commandments:
text.NABRE: But Mattathias answered in a loud voice: “Although all the Gentiles in the king’s realm obey him, so that they forsake the religion of their ancestors and consent to the king’s orders,
text.NRSV: But Mattathias answered and said in a loud voice: ‘Even if all the nations that live under the rule of the king obey him, and have chosen to obey his commandments, every one of them abandoning the religion of their ancestors,
text.DRB: And Mathathias answered, & said with a loud voice: Although all Nations obey king Antiochus, that every man revolt from the service of the law of his fathers, and consent to his commandments:

text.KJV: Yet will I and my sons and my brethren walk in the covenant of our fathers.
text.NABRE: yet I and my sons and my kindred will keep to the covenant of our ancestors.
text.NRSV: I and my sons and my brothers will continue to live by the covenant of our ancestors.
text.DRB: I and my sons, & my brethren will obey the law of our fathers.
God be merciful unto us: it is not profitable for us to forsake the law, and the justices of God:
text.KJV: God forbid that we should forsake the law and the ordinances.
text.NABRE: Heaven forbid that we should forsake the law and the commandments.
text.NRSV: Far be it from us to desert the law and the ordinances.

text.DRB: we will not hear the words of king Antiochus, neither will we sacrifice, transgressing the commandments of our law, to go another way.
text.KJV: We will not hearken to the king’s words, to go from our religion, either on the right hand, or the left.
text.NABRE: We will not obey the words of the king by departing from our religion in the slightest degree.”
text.NRSV: We will not obey the king’s words by turning aside from our religion to the right hand or to the left.’

text.DRB: And as he ceased to speak these words, there came a certain Jew in the eyes of all to sacrifice to the idols upon the altar in the city of Modin, according to the king's commandment.
text.KJV: Now when he had left speaking these words, there came one of the Jews in the sight of all to sacrifice on the altar which was at Modin, according to the king’s commandment.
text.NABRE: As he finished saying these words, a certain Jew came forward in the sight of all to offer sacrifice on the altar in Modein according to the king’s order.
text.NRSV: When he had finished speaking these words, a Jew came forward in the sight of all to offer sacrifice on the altar in Modein, according to the king’s command.

text.KJV: Which thing when Mattathias saw, he was inflamed with zeal, and his reins trembled, neither could he forbear to shew his anger according to judgment: wherefore he ran, and slew him upon the altar.
text.NABRE: When Mattathias saw him, he was filled with zeal; his heart was moved and his just fury was aroused; he sprang forward and killed him upon the altar.
text.NRSV: When Mattathias saw it, he burned with zeal and his heart was stirred. He gave vent to righteous anger; he ran and killed him on the altar.
text.DRB: And Mathathias saw, & was sorry, and his reins trembled, and his fury was kindled according to the judgement of the law, and flying upon him he slew him upon the altar:
yea and the man, whom king Antiochus had sent, which compelled them to immolate, he slew in that very time, & destroyed the altar,
text.KJV: Also the king’s commissioner, who compelled men to sacrifice, he killed at that time, and the altar he pulled down.
text.NABRE: At the same time, he also killed the messenger of the king who was forcing them to sacrifice, and he tore down the altar.
text.NRSV: At the same time he killed the king’s officer who was forcing them to sacrifice, and he tore down the altar.

text.DRB: and zealed the law, as did Phinees to Zamri the son of Salomi.
text.KJV: Thus dealt he zealously for the law of God like as Phinees did unto Zambri the son of Salom.
text.NABRE: Thus he showed his zeal for the law, just as Phinehas did with Zimri, son of Salu.
text.NRSV: Thus he burned with zeal for the law, just as Phinehas did against Zimri son of Salu.

text.KJV: And Mattathias cried throughout the city with a loud voice, saying, Whosoever is zealous of the law, and maintaineth the covenant, let him follow me.
text.NABRE: Then Mattathias cried out in the city, “Let everyone who is zealous for the law and who stands by the covenant follow me!”
text.NRSV: Then Mattathias cried out in the town with a loud voice, saying: ‘Let everyone who is zealous for the law and supports the covenant come out with me!’
text.DRB: And Mathathias cried out with a loud voice in the city, saying: Every one that hath zeal of the law, establishing his testament, let him come forth after me.
And he fled himself, & his sons into the mountains, and left all things whatsoever they had in the city.
text.KJV: So he and his sons fled into the mountains, and left all that ever they had in the city.
text.NABRE: Then he and his sons fled to the mountains, leaving behind in the city all their possessions.
text.NRSV: Then he and his sons fled to the hills and left all that they had in the town.

text.DRB: Then came down many seeking judgement, and justice, into the desert:
text.KJV: Then many that sought after justice and judgment went down into the wilderness, to dwell there:
text.NABRE: At that time many who sought righteousness and justice went out into the wilderness to settle there,
text.NRSV: At that time many who were seeking righteousness and justice went down to the wilderness to live there,

text.DRB: and they sat there themselves, and their children, and their wives, & their cattle: because the evils overflowed upon them.
text.KJV: Both they, and their children, and their wives; and their cattle; because afflictions increased sore upon them.
text.NABRE: they and their children, their wives and their animals, because misfortunes pressed so hard on them.
text.NRSV: they, their sons, their wives, and their livestock, because troubles pressed heavily upon them.

text.DRB: And it was reported to the king's men, and to the army that was in Jerusalem in the city of David, that certain men which dissipated the king's commandment, were departed into secret places in the desert, & many were gone after them.
text.KJV: Now when it was told the king’s servants, and the host that was at Jerusalem, in the city of David, that certain men, who had broken the king’s commandment, were gone down into the secret places in the wilderness,
text.NABRE: It was reported to the officers and soldiers of the king who were in the City of David, in Jerusalem, that those who had flouted the king’s order had gone out to secret refuges in the wilderness.
text.NRSV: And it was reported to the king’s officers, and to the troops in Jerusalem the city of David, that those who had rejected the king’s command had gone down to the hiding-places in the wilderness.

text.DRB: And forthwith they went forwards towards them, & set battle against them in the day of the Sabbaths,
text.KJV: They pursued after them a great number, and having overtaken them, they camped against them, and made war against them on the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: Many hurried out after them, and having caught up with them, camped opposite and prepared to attack them on the sabbath.
text.NRSV: Many pursued them, and overtook them; they encamped opposite them and prepared for battle against them on the sabbath day.

text.DRB: & they said to them: Do you resist now also as yet? Come forth: and do according to the word of king Antiochus, and you shall live.
text.KJV: And they said unto them, Let that which ye have done hitherto suffice; come forth, and do according to the commandment of the king, and ye shall live.
text.NABRE: The pursuers said to them, “Enough of this! Come out and obey the king’s command, and you will live.”
text.NRSV: They said to them, ‘Enough of this! Come out and do what the king commands, and you will live.’

text.DRB: And they said: We will not come forth, neither will we do the king's word, to pollute the day of the Sabbaths.
text.KJV: But they said, We will not come forth, neither will we do the king’s commandment, to profane the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: But they replied, “We will not come out, nor will we obey the king’s command to profane the sabbath.”
text.NRSV: But they said, ‘We will not come out, nor will we do what the king commands and so profane the sabbath day.’

text.DRB: And they hastened battle against them.
text.KJV: So then they gave them the battle with all speed.
text.NABRE: Then the enemy attacked them at once.
text.NRSV: Then the enemy quickly attacked them.

text.DRB: And they answered them not, neither did they cast a stone at them, nor stopped the secret places,
text.KJV: Howbeit they answered them not, neither cast they a stone at them, nor stopped the places where they lay hid;
text.NABRE: But they did not retaliate; they neither threw stones, nor blocked up their secret refuges.
text.NRSV: But they did not answer them or hurl a stone at them or block up their hiding-places,

text.DRB: saying: Let us die all in our simplicity: and heaven & earth shall be witnesses, upon us that you unjustly destroy us.
text.KJV: But said, Let us die all in our innocency: heaven and earth will testify for us, that ye put us to death wrongfully.
text.NABRE: They said, “Let us all die in innocence; heaven and earth are our witnesses that you destroy us unjustly.”
text.NRSV: for they said, ‘Let us all die in our innocence; heaven and earth testify for us that you are killing us unjustly.’

text.DRB: And they gave them battle on the Sabbaths: & there died they, & their wives, & their children, & their cattle even to a thousand souls of men.
text.KJV: So they rose up against them in battle on the sabbath, and they slew them, with their wives and children and their cattle, to the number of a thousand people.
text.NABRE: So the officers and soldiers attacked them on the sabbath, and they died with their wives, their children and their animals, to the number of a thousand persons.
text.NRSV: So they attacked them on the sabbath, and they died, with their wives and children and livestock, to the number of a thousand people.

text.DRB: And Mathathias understood it and his friends, and they had lamentation upon them exceedingly.
text.KJV: Now when Mattathias and his friends understood hereof, they mourned for them right sore.
text.NABRE: When Mattathias and his friends heard of it, they mourned deeply for them.
text.NRSV: When Mattathias and his friends learned of it, they mourned for them deeply.

text.DRB: And every man said to his neighbour: If we shall all do as our brethren have done, and shall not fight against the heathen for our lives, and our justifications: now will they quickly destroy us from the earth.
text.KJV: And one of them said to another, If we all do as our brethren have done, and fight not for our lives and laws against the heathen, they will now quickly root us out of the earth.
text.NABRE: They said to one another, “If we all do as our kindred have done, and do not fight against the Gentiles for our lives and our laws, they will soon destroy us from the earth.”
text.NRSV: And all said to their neighbours: ‘If we all do as our kindred have done and refuse to fight with the Gentiles for our lives and for our ordinances, they will quickly destroy us from the earth.’

text.DRB: And they thought in that day, saying: Every man whosoever shall come unto us in battle on the day of the Sabbaths, let us fight against him: & we will not all die, as our brethren died in secret places.
text.KJV: At that time therefore they decreed, saying, Whosoever shall come to make battle with us on the sabbath day, we will fight against him; neither will we die all, as our brethren that were murdered in the secret places.
text.NABRE: So on that day they came to this decision: “Let us fight against anyone who attacks us on the sabbath, so that we may not all die as our kindred died in their secret refuges.”
text.NRSV: So they made this decision that day: ‘Let us fight against anyone who comes to attack us on the sabbath day; let us not all die as our kindred died in their hiding-places.’

text.DRB: Then was there gathered to them the synagogue of the Assideans strong of force out of Israel, every voluntary in the law:
text.KJV: Then came there unto him a company of Assideans who were mighty men of Israel, even all such as were voluntarily devoted unto the law.
text.NABRE: Then they were joined by a group of Hasideans, mighty warriors of Israel, all of them devoted to the law.
text.NRSV: Counter-Attack Then there united with them a company of Hasideans, mighty warriors of Israel, all who offered themselves willingly for the law.

text.DRB: and all that fled from the evils, were added to them, and were made a strength to them.
text.KJV: Also all they that fled for persecution joined themselves unto them, and were a stay unto them.
text.NABRE: And all those who were fleeing from the persecutions joined them and supported them.
text.NRSV: And all who became fugitives to escape their troubles joined them and reinforced them.

text.DRB: And they gathered an army, & struck the sinners in their wrath, and the wicked men in their indignation: & the rest fled to the nations to escape.
text.KJV: So they joined their forces, and smote sinful men in their anger, and wicked men in their wrath: but the rest fled to the heathen for succour.
text.NABRE: They gathered an army and struck down sinners in their wrath and the lawless in their anger, and the survivors fled to the Gentiles for safety.
text.NRSV: They organized an army, and struck down sinners in their anger and renegades in their wrath; the survivors fled to the Gentiles for safety.

text.DRB: And Mathathias went round about, & his friends, and they destroyed the altars:
text.KJV: Then Mattathias and his friends went round about, and pulled down the altars:
text.NABRE: Mattathias and his friends went about and tore down the pagan altars;
text.NRSV: And Mattathias and his friends went around and tore down the altars;

text.DRB: & they circumcised the uncircumcised children, as many as they found in the coasts of Israel: and in strength.
text.KJV: And what children soever they found within the coast of Israel uncircumcised, those they circumcised valiantly.
text.NABRE: they also forcibly circumcised any uncircumcised boys whom they found in the territory of Israel.
text.NRSV: they forcibly circumcised all the uncircumcised boys that they found within the borders of Israel.

text.DRB: And they persecuted the children of pride, and the work prospered in their hands:
text.KJV: They pursued also after the proud men, and the work prospered in their hand.
text.NABRE: They put to flight the arrogant, and the work prospered in their hands.
text.NRSV: They hunted down the arrogant, and the work prospered in their hands.

text.DRB: and they obtained the law out of the hands of the nations, and out of the hands of the kings: and they gave not the horn to the sinner.
text.KJV: So they recovered the law out of the hand of the Gentiles, and out of the hand of kings, neither suffered they the sinner to triumph.
text.NABRE: They saved the law from the hands of the Gentiles and of the kings and did not let the sinner triumph.
text.NRSV: They rescued the law out of the hands of the Gentiles and kings, and they never let the sinner gain the upper hand.

text.KJV: Now when the time drew near that Mattathias should die, he said unto his sons, Now hath pride and rebuke gotten strength, and the time of destruction, and the wrath of indignation:
text.NABRE: Farewell of Mattathias. When the time came for Mattathias to die, he said to his sons: “Arrogance and scorn have now grown strong; it is a time of disaster and violent wrath.
text.NRSV: The Last Words of Mattathias Now the days drew near for Mattathias to die, and he said to his sons: ‘Arrogance and scorn have now become strong; it is a time of ruin and furious anger.
text.DRB: And the days of Mathathias approached to die, & he said to his sons: Now is pride strengthened, & chastisement, & the time of subversion, & the wrath of indignation:
Now therefore o my sons, be ye emulators of the law, & give your lives for the testament of your fathers.
text.KJV: Now therefore, my sons, be ye zealous for the law, and give your lives for the covenant of your fathers.
text.NABRE: Therefore, my children, be zealous for the law and give your lives for the covenant of our ancestors.
text.NRSV: Now, my children, show zeal for the law, and give your lives for the covenant of our ancestors.

text.DRB: And remember the works of the fathers, which they have done in their generations, & you shall receive great glory, and an eternal name.
text.KJV: Call to remembrance what acts our fathers did in their time; so shall ye receive great honour and an everlasting name.
text.NABRE: “Remember the deeds that our ancestors did in their times, and you shall win great honor and an everlasting name.
text.NRSV: ‘Remember the deeds of the ancestors, which they did in their generations; and you will receive great honour and an everlasting name.

text.DRB: Abraham was he not in tentation found faithful, and it was reputed to him unto justice?
text.KJV: Was not Abraham found faithful in temptation, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness?
text.NABRE: Was not Abraham found faithful in trial, and it was credited to him as righteousness?
text.NRSV: Was not Abraham found faithful when tested, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness?

text.DRB: Joseph in the time of his distress kept the commandment, & he was made Lord of Ægypt.
text.KJV: Joseph in the time of his distress kept the commandment and was made lord of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Joseph, when in distress, kept the commandment, and he became master of Egypt.
text.NRSV: Joseph in the time of his distress kept the commandment, and became lord of Egypt.

text.DRB: Phinees our father, fervent in the zeal of God, received the testament of an everlasting Priesthood.
text.KJV: Phinees our father in being zealous and fervent obtained the covenant of an everlasting priesthood.
text.NABRE: Phinehas our ancestor, for his burning zeal, received the covenant of an everlasting priesthood.
text.NRSV: Phinehas our ancestor, because he was deeply zealous, received the covenant of everlasting priesthood.

text.DRB: Jesus whilst he accomplished the word, was made the Duke in Israel.
text.KJV: Jesus for fulfilling the word was made a judge in Israel.
text.NABRE: Joshua, for executing his commission, became a judge in Israel.
text.NRSV: Joshua, because he fulfilled the command, became a judge in Israel.

text.DRB: Caleb whilst he testifieth in the Church, received an inheritance.
text.KJV: Caleb for bearing witness before the congregation received the heritage of the land.
text.NABRE: Caleb, for bearing witness before the assembly, received an inheritance in the land.
text.NRSV: Caleb, because he testified in the assembly, received an inheritance in the land.

text.DRB: David in his mercy obtained the seat of the kingdom for ever.
text.KJV: David for being merciful possessed the throne of an everlasting kingdom.
text.NABRE: David, for his loyalty, received as a heritage a throne of eternal kingship.
text.NRSV: David, because he was merciful, inherited the throne of the kingdom for ever.

text.DRB: Elias whilst he zealeth the zeal of the law, was received into heaven.
text.KJV: Elias for being zealous and fervent for the law was taken up into heaven.
text.NABRE: Elijah, for his burning zeal for the law, was taken up to heaven.
text.NRSV: Elijah, because of great zeal for the law, was taken up into heaven.

text.DRB: Ananias and Azarias & Misael believing, were delivered out of the flame.
text.KJV: Ananias, Azarias, and Misael, by believing were saved out of the flame.
text.NABRE: Hananiah, Azariah and Mishael, for their faith, were saved from the fire.
text.NRSV: Hananiah, Azariah, and Mishael believed and were saved from the flame.

text.DRB: Daniel in his simplicity was delivered out of the lions' mouth.
text.KJV: Daniel for his innocency was delivered from the mouth of lions.
text.NABRE: Daniel, for his innocence, was delivered from the mouths of lions.
text.NRSV: Daniel, because of his innocence, was delivered from the mouth of the lions.

text.DRB: And so think ye through generation & generation: that all that hope in him, are not weakened.
text.KJV: And thus consider ye throughout all ages, that none that put their trust in him shall be overcome.
text.NABRE: And so, consider this from generation to generation, that none who hope in Heaven shall fail in strength.
text.NRSV: ‘And so observe, from generation to generation, that none of those who put their trust in him will lack strength.

text.DRB: And of the words of the sinful man be not afraid: because his glory is dung, and worm:
text.KJV: Fear not then the words of a sinful man: for his glory shall be dung and worms.
text.NABRE: Do not fear the words of sinners, for their glory ends in corruption and worms.
text.NRSV: Do not fear the words of sinners, for their splendour will turn into dung and worms.

text.DRB: today he is extolled, and tomorrow he shall not be found: because he is turned into his earth, & his cogitation is perished.
text.KJV: To day he shall be lifted up and to morrow he shall not be found, because he is returned into his dust, and his thought is come to nothing.
text.NABRE: Today exalted, tomorrow not to be found, they have returned to dust, their schemes have perished.
text.NRSV: Today they will be exalted, but tomorrow they will not be found, because they will have returned to the dust, and their plans will have perished.

text.DRB: You therefore my sons take courage, and do manfully in the law: because in it you shall be glorious.
text.KJV: Wherefore, ye my sons, be valiant and shew yourselves men in the behalf of the law; for by it shall ye obtain glory.
text.NABRE: Children! be courageous and strong in keeping the law, for by it you shall be honored.
text.NRSV: My children, be courageous and grow strong in the law, for by it you will gain honour.

text.DRB: And behold Simon your brother, I know that he is a man of counsel: hear ye him always, and he shall be a father to you.
text.KJV: And behold, I know that your brother Simon is a man of counsel, give ear unto him alway: he shall be a father unto you.
text.NABRE: “Here is your brother Simeon who I know is a wise counselor; listen to him always, and he will be a father to you.
text.NRSV: ‘Here is your brother Simeon who, I know, is wise in counsel; always listen to him; he shall be your father.

text.KJV: As for Judas Maccabeus, he hath been mighty and strong, even from his youth up: let him be your captain, and fight the battle of the people.
text.NABRE: And Judas Maccabeus, a mighty warrior from his youth, shall be the leader of your army and wage the war against the nations.
text.NRSV: Judas Maccabeus has been a mighty warrior from his youth; he shall command the army for you and fight the battle against the peoples.
text.DRB: And Judas Machabeus valiant of strength from his youth, let him be to you the prince of warfare, and he shall manage the battle of the people.
And you shall bring to you all the doers of the law: and revenge ye the revenge of your people.
text.KJV: Take also unto you all those that observe the law, and avenge ye the wrong of your people.
text.NABRE: Gather about you all who observe the law, and avenge your people.
text.NRSV: You shall rally around you all who observe the law, and avenge the wrong done to your people.

text.DRB: Repay retribution to the Gentiles, & attend to the precept of the law.
text.KJV: Recompense fully the heathen, and take heed to the commandments of the law.
text.NABRE: Pay back the Gentiles what they deserve, and observe the precepts of the law.”
text.NRSV: Pay back the Gentiles in full, and obey the commands of the law.’

text.DRB: And he blessed them, and was laid to his fathers.
text.KJV: So he blessed them, and was gathered to his fathers.
text.NABRE: Then he blessed them, and he was gathered to his ancestors.
text.NRSV: Then he blessed them, and was gathered to his ancestors.

text.KJV: And he died in the hundred forty and sixth year, and his sons buried him in the sepulchres of his fathers at Modin, and all Israel made great lamentation for him.
text.NABRE: He died in the year one hundred and forty-six, and was buried in the tombs of his ancestors in Modein, and all Israel mourned him greatly.
text.NRSV: He died in the one hundred and forty-sixth year and was buried in the tomb of his ancestors at Modein. And all Israel mourned for him with great lamentation.
text.DRB: And he died the hundredth & six and fortieth year, and he was buried by his sons in the sepulchres of his fathers in Modin, and all Israel lamented him with great lamentation.

text.KJV: Then his son Judas, called Maccabeus, rose up in his stead.
text.NABRE: II. Leadership of Judas Maccabeus Chapter 3 Judas and His Early Victories. Then his son Judas, who was called Maccabeus, took his place.
text.NRSV: The Early Victories of Judas 3
text.DRB: And Judas, that was called Machabeus his son arose in his stead:
and all his brethren helped him, & all that had joined themselves to his father, and they fought the battle of Israel with joy.
text.KJV: And all his brethren helped him, and so did all they that held with his father, and they fought with cheerfulness the battle of Israel.
text.NABRE: All his brothers and all who had joined his father supported him, and they gladly carried on Israel’s war.
text.NRSV: All his brothers and all who had joined his father helped him; they gladly fought for Israel.

text.KJV: So he gat his people great honour, and put on a breastplate as a giant, and girt his warlike harness about him, and he made battles, protecting the host with his sword.
text.NABRE: He spread abroad the glory of his people, and put on his breastplate like a giant. He armed himself with weapons of war; he fought battles and protected the camp with his sword.
text.NRSV: He extended the glory of his people. Like a giant he put on his breastplate; he bound on his armour of war and waged battles, protecting the camp by his sword.
text.DRB: And he dilated glory to his people, & put on him a brigantine as a giant, and girded about him his warly armour in battles, and protected the camp with his sword.
He became like a lion in his acts, and as a lion's whelp roaring in hunting.
text.KJV: In his acts he was like a lion, and like a lion’s whelp roaring for his prey.
text.NABRE: In his deeds he was like a lion, like a young lion roaring for prey.
text.NRSV: He was like a lion in his deeds, like a lion’s cub roaring for prey.

text.DRB: And he persecuted the wicked inquiring them out, & such as troubled his people, them he burnt with fire:
text.KJV: For He pursued the wicked, and sought them out, and burnt up those that vexed his people.
text.NABRE: He pursued the lawless, hunting them out, and those who troubled his people he destroyed by fire.
text.NRSV: He searched out and pursued those who broke the law; he burned those who troubled his people.

text.DRB: and his enemies were repelled for fear of him, all the workers of iniquity were troubled: and salvation was directed in his hand.
text.KJV: Wherefore the wicked shrunk for fear of him, and all the workers of iniquity were troubled, because salvation prospered in his hand.
text.NABRE: The lawless were cowed by fear of him, and all evildoers were dismayed. By his hand deliverance was happily achieved,
text.NRSV: Lawbreakers shrank back for fear of him; all the evildoers were confounded; and deliverance prospered by his hand.

text.DRB: And he exasperated many kings, & rejoiced Jacob in his works, & for ever his memory is in benediction.
text.KJV: He grieved also many kings, and made Jacob glad with his acts, and his memorial is blessed for ever.
text.NABRE: and he afflicted many kings. He gave joy to Jacob by his deeds, and his memory is blessed forever.
text.NRSV: He embittered many kings, but he made Jacob glad by his deeds, and his memory is blessed for ever.

text.DRB: And he walked through the cities of Juda, & destroyed the impious out of them, & turned away wrath from Israel.
text.KJV: Moreover he went through the cities of Juda, destroying the ungodly out of them, and turning away wrath from Israel:
text.NABRE: He went about the cities of Judah destroying the renegades there. He turned away wrath from Israel,
text.NRSV: He went through the cities of Judah; he destroyed the ungodly out of the land; thus he turned away wrath from Israel.

text.DRB: And he was renowned even to the uttermost part of the earth, and he gathered them that perished.
text.KJV: So that he was renowned unto the utmost part of the earth, and he received unto him such as were ready to perish.
text.NABRE: was renowned to the ends of the earth; and gathered together those who were perishing.
text.NRSV: He was renowned to the ends of the earth; he gathered in those who were perishing.

text.DRB: And Apollonius gathered together the Gentiles, and from Samaria a power much and great, to make battle against Israel.
text.KJV: Then Apollonius gathered the Gentiles together, and a great host out of Samaria, to fight against Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Apollonius gathered together the Gentiles, along with a large army from Samaria, to fight against Israel.
text.NRSV: Apollonius now gathered together Gentiles and a large force from Samaria to fight against Israel.

text.DRB: And Judas understood it, and went forth to meet him: and he struck, and killed him: and there fell many wounded, & the rest fled away.
text.KJV: Which thing when Judas perceived, he went forth to meet him, and so he smote him, and slew him: many also fell down slain, but the rest fled.
text.NABRE: When Judas learned of it, he went out to meet him and struck and killed him. Many fell wounded, and the rest fled.
text.NRSV: When Judas learned of it, he went out to meet him, and he defeated and killed him. Many were wounded and fell, and the rest fled.

text.DRB: And he took the spoils of them, and the sword of Apollonius Judas took away, and he fought with it all his days.
text.KJV: Wherefore Judas took their spoils, and Apollonius’ sword also, and therewith he fought all his life long.
text.NABRE: They took their spoils, and Judas took the sword of Apollonius and fought with it the rest of his life.
text.NRSV: Then they seized their spoils; and Judas took the sword of Apollonius, and used it in battle for the rest of his life.

text.DRB: And Seron captain of the army of Syria heard that Judas gathered a congregation of the faithful, and an assembly with him,
text.KJV: Now when Seron, a prince of the army of Syria, heard say that Judas had gathered unto him a multitude and company of the faithful to go out with him to war;
text.NABRE: But Seron, commander of the Syrian army, heard that Judas had mustered an assembly of faithful men ready for war.
text.NRSV: When Seron, the commander of the Syrian army, heard that Judas had gathered a large company, including a body of faithful soldiers who stayed with him and went out to battle,

text.DRB: and he said: I will make me a name, and will be glorified in kingdom, and will overthrow Judas, and those that are with him that despised the word of the king.
text.KJV: He said, I will get me a name and honour in the kingdom; for I will go fight with Judas and them that are with him, who despise the king’s commandment.
text.NABRE: So he said, “I will make a name for myself and win honor in the kingdom. I will wage war against Judas and his followers, who have despised the king’s command.”
text.NRSV: he said, ‘I will make a name for myself and win honour in the kingdom. I will make war on Judas and his companions, who scorn the king’s command.’

text.DRB: And he prepared himself: and there went up with him a camp of the impious strong helpers, to do vengeance upon the children of Israel.
text.KJV: So he made him ready to go up, and there went with him a mighty host of the ungodly to help him, and to be avenged of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: And again a large company of renegades advanced with him to help him take revenge on the Israelites.
text.NRSV: Once again a strong army of godless men went up with him to help him, to take vengeance on the Israelites.

text.DRB: And they approached even as far as Bethoron: & Judas went forth to meet him with few.
text.KJV: And when he came near to the going up of Bethhoron, Judas went forth to meet him with a small company:
text.NABRE: When he reached the ascent of Beth-horon, Judas went out to meet him with a few men.
text.NRSV: When he approached the ascent of Beth-horon, Judas went out to meet him with a small company.

text.DRB: But as they saw the army coming to meet them, they said to Judas: How shall we a few be able to fight against so great a multitude & so strong, and we are wearied with fasting today?
text.KJV: Who, when they saw the host coming to meet them, said unto Judas, How shall we be able, being so few, to fight against so great a multitude and so strong, seeing we are ready to faint with fasting all this day?
text.NABRE: But when they saw the army coming against them, they said to Judas: “How can we, few as we are, fight such a strong host as this? Besides, we are weak since we have not eaten today.”
text.NRSV: But when they saw the army coming to meet them, they said to Judas, ‘How can we, few as we are, fight against so great and so strong a multitude? And we are faint, for we have eaten nothing today.’

text.KJV: Unto whom Judas answered, It is no hard matter for many to be shut up in the hands of a few; and with the God of heaven it is all one, to deliver with a great multitude, or a small company:
text.NABRE: But Judas said: “Many are easily hemmed in by a few; in the sight of Heaven there is no difference between deliverance by many or by few;
text.NRSV: Judas replied, ‘It is easy for many to be hemmed in by few, for in the sight of Heaven there is no difference between saving by many or by few.
text.DRB: And Judas said: It is an easy matter for many to be inclosed in the hand of few, & there is no difference in the sight of the God of heaven to deliver in many, & in few.

text.KJV: For the victory of battle standeth not in the multitude of an host; but strength cometh from heaven.
text.NABRE: for victory in war does not depend upon the size of the army, but on strength that comes from Heaven.
text.NRSV: It is not on the size of the army that victory in battle depends, but strength comes from Heaven.
text.DRB: Because not in the multitude of the army is the victory of battle, but from heaven is the strength.
They come to us in an obstinate multitude, & in pride, to destroy us, & our wives, & our children, and to spoil us.
text.KJV: They come against us in much pride and iniquity to destroy us, and our wives and children, and to spoil us:
text.NABRE: With great presumption and lawlessness they come against us to destroy us and our wives and children and to despoil us;
text.NRSV: They come against us in great insolence and lawlessness to destroy us and our wives and our children, and to despoil us;

text.DRB: But we will fight for our lives, & our laws:
text.KJV: But we fight for our lives and our laws.
text.NABRE: but we are fighting for our lives and our laws.
text.NRSV: but we fight for our lives and our laws.

text.DRB: & our Lord himself will destroy them before our face, but you fear them not.
text.KJV: Wherefore the Lord himself will overthrow them before our face: and as for you, be ye not afraid of them.
text.NABRE: He will crush them before us; so do not fear them.”
text.NRSV: He himself will crush them before us; as for you, do not be afraid of them.’

text.DRB: And as he ceased to speak, he flew upon them suddenly: & Seron was destroyed, & his host in his sight:
text.KJV: Now as soon as he had left off speaking, he leapt suddenly upon them, and so Seron and his host was overthrown before him.
text.NABRE: When he finished speaking, he rushed suddenly upon Seron and his army, who were crushed before him.
text.NRSV: When he finished speaking, he rushed suddenly against Seron and his army, and they were crushed before him.

text.DRB: & he pursued him in the descent of Bethoron even to the plain, & there fell of them eight hundred men, & the rest fled into the land of the Philisthiims.
text.KJV: And they pursued them from the going down of Bethhoron unto the plain, where were slain about eight hundred men of them; and the residue fled into the land of the Philistines.
text.NABRE: He pursued Seron down the descent of Beth-horon into the plain. About eight hundred of their men fell, and the rest fled to the land of the Philistines.
text.NRSV: They pursued them down the descent of Beth-horon to the plain; eight hundred of them fell, and the rest fled into the land of the Philistines.

text.DRB: And the fear of Judas & of his brethren, & the dread fell upon all the nations round about them.
text.KJV: Then began the fear of Judas and his brethren, and an exceeding great dread, to fall upon the nations round about them:
text.NABRE: Then Judas and his brothers began to be feared, and dread fell upon the Gentiles about them.
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his brothers began to be feared, and terror fell on the Gentiles all around them.

text.DRB: And his name came to the king, & all nations told of the battles of Judas.
text.KJV: Insomuch as his fame came unto the king, and all nations talked of the battles of Judas.
text.NABRE: His fame reached the king, and the Gentiles talked about the battles of Judas.
text.NRSV: His fame reached the king, and the Gentiles talked of the battles of Judas.

text.DRB: And as king Antiochus heard these words he was wrath in his mind: & he sent, & gathered the army of all his kingdom, a camp exceeding strong:
text.KJV: Now when king Antiochus heard these things, he was full of indignation: wherefore he sent and gathered together all the forces of his realm, even a very strong army.
text.NABRE: The King’s Strategy. When King Antiochus heard these reports, he was filled with rage; so he ordered that all the forces of his kingdom be gathered, a very strong army.
text.NRSV: The Policy of Antiochus When King Antiochus heard these reports, he was greatly angered; and he sent and gathered all the forces of his kingdom, a very strong army.

text.DRB: and he opened his treasury, and gave wages to the army for a year: and he commanded them, that they should be ready at all assays.
text.KJV: He opened also his treasure, and gave his soldiers pay for a year, commanding them to be ready whensoever he should need them.
text.NABRE: He opened his treasury, gave his soldiers a year’s pay, and commanded them to be prepared for anything.
text.NRSV: He opened his coffers and gave a year’s pay to his forces, and ordered them to be ready for any need.

text.DRB: And he saw that money failed out of his treasures, & the tributes of the region small, because of the dissension, and the plague, that he had made in the land, to take away the ordinances, that were from the first days:
text.KJV: Nevertheless, when he saw that the money of his treasures failed and that the tributes in the country were small, because of the dissension and plague, which he had brought upon the land in taking away the laws which had been of old time;
