text.NABRE: But then he saw that this exhausted the money in his treasury; moreover the tribute from the province was small because of the dissension and distress he had brought upon the land by abolishing the laws which had been in effect from of old.
text.NRSV: Then he saw that the money in the treasury was exhausted, and that the revenues from the country were small because of the dissension and disaster that he had caused in the land by abolishing the laws that had existed from the earliest days.

text.DRB: and he feared lest he should not have as once and twice, for costs & gifts, which he had given before with a large hand: & he had abounded above the kings, that had been before him.
text.KJV: He feared that he should not be able to bear the charges any longer, nor to have such gifts to give so liberally as he did before: for he had abounded above the kings that were before him.
text.NABRE: He feared that, as had happened once or twice, he would not have enough for his expenses and for the gifts that he was accustomed to give with a lavish hand—more so than all previous kings.
text.NRSV: He feared that he might not have such funds as he had before for his expenses and for the gifts that he used to give more lavishly than preceding kings.

text.DRB: And he was exceedingly astonished in mind, and purposed to go into Persis, and to take the tributes of the regions, and to gather much silver.
text.KJV: Wherefore, being greatly perplexed in his mind, he determined to go into Persia, there to take the tributes of the countries, and to gather much money.
text.NABRE: Greatly perplexed, he decided to go to Persia and levy tribute on those provinces, and so raise a large sum of money.
text.NRSV: He was greatly perplexed in mind; then he determined to go to Persia and collect the revenues from those regions and raise a large fund.

text.DRB: And he left Lysias a noble man of the blood royal, over the king's affairs, from the river Euphrates even to the river of Ægypt:
text.KJV: So he left Lysias, a nobleman, and one of the blood royal, to oversee the affairs of the king from the river Euphrates unto the borders of Egypt:
text.NABRE: He left Lysias, a noble of royal descent, in charge of the king’s affairs from the Euphrates River to the frontier of Egypt,
text.NRSV: He left Lysias, a distinguished man of royal lineage, in charge of the king’s affairs from the river Euphrates to the borders of Egypt.

text.DRB: and that he should bring up Antiochus his son, till he returned.
text.KJV: And to bring up his son Antiochus, until he came again.
text.NABRE: and commissioned him to take care of his son Antiochus until his return.
text.NRSV: Lysias was also to take care of his son Antiochus until he returned.

text.DRB: And he delivered to him half the army, and Elephants: and he gave him commandment concerning all things that he would, and concerning the inhabitants of Jurie, and Jerusalem:
text.KJV: Moreover he delivered unto him the half of his forces, and the elephants, and gave him charge of all things that he would have done, as also concerning them that dwelt in Juda and Jerusalem:
text.NABRE: He entrusted to him half of his forces, and the elephants, and gave him instructions concerning everything he wanted done. As for the inhabitants of Judea and Jerusalem,
text.NRSV: And he turned over to Lysias half of his forces and the elephants, and gave him orders about all that he wanted done. As for the residents of Judea and Jerusalem,

text.DRB: and that he should send an army to them, to destroy and root out the power of Israel, and the remnant of Jerusalem, and to take away the memory of them out of the place,
text.KJV: To wit, that he should send an army against them, to destroy and root out the strength of Israel, and the remnant of Jerusalem, and to take away their memorial from that place;
text.NABRE: Lysias was to send an army against them to crush and destroy the power of Israel and the remnant of Jerusalem and efface their memory from the place.
text.NRSV: Lysias was to send a force against them to wipe out and destroy the strength of Israel and the remnant of Jerusalem; he was to banish the memory of them from the place,

text.DRB: and that he should appoint inhabitants in all their coasts, children strangers, & should by lot distribute their land.
text.KJV: And that he should place strangers in all their quarters, and divide their land by lot.
text.NABRE: He was to settle foreigners in all their territory and distribute their land by lot.
text.NRSV: settle aliens in all their territory, and distribute their land by lot.

text.DRB: And the king took the part of the army that remained, & went forth from Antioch the city of his kingdom in the year an hundreth and seven and forty: and he passed over the river Euphrates, & walked through the higher countries.
text.KJV: So the king took the half of the forces that remained, and departed from Antioch, his royal city, the hundred forty and seventh year; and having passed the river Euphrates, he went through the high countries.
text.NABRE: The king took the remaining half of the army and set out from Antioch, his capital, in the year one hundred and forty-seven; he crossed the Euphrates River and went through the provinces beyond.
text.NRSV: Then the king took the remaining half of his forces and left Antioch his capital in the one hundred and forty-seventh year. He crossed the Euphrates river and went through the upper provinces.

text.KJV: Then Lysias chose Ptolemee the son of Dorymenes, Nicanor, and Gorgias, mighty men of the king’s friends:
text.NABRE: Preparations for Battle. Lysias chose Ptolemy, son of Dorymenes, and Nicanor and Gorgias, powerful men among the King’s Friends,
text.NRSV: Preparations for Battle Lysias chose Ptolemy son of Dorymenes, and Nicanor and Gorgias, able men among the Friends of the king,
text.DRB: And Lysias chose Ptolomee the son of Doryminus, and Nicanor, and Gorgias, mighty men of the king's friends.

text.KJV: And with them he sent forty thousand footmen, and seven thousand horsemen, to go into the land of Juda, and to destroy it, as the king commanded.
text.NABRE: and with them he sent forty thousand foot soldiers and seven thousand cavalry to invade and ravage the land of Judah according to the king’s orders.
text.NRSV: and sent with them forty thousand infantry and seven thousand cavalry to go into the land of Judah and destroy it, as the king had commanded.
text.DRB: And he sent with them forty thousand men, and seven thousand horsemen: that they should come into the land of Juda, and should destroy it according to the word of the king.
And they went forth with all their power, and came, and joined near to Emmaum in the champion country.
text.KJV: So they went forth with all their power, and came and pitched by Emmaus in the plain country.
text.NABRE: Setting out with their whole force, they came and pitched their camp near Emmaus in the plain.
text.NRSV: So they set out with their entire force, and when they arrived they encamped near Emmaus in the plain.

text.DRB: And the merchants of the countries heard the name of them: and they took silver and gold exceeding much, and servants: and they came into the camp, to take the children of Israel for slaves, & there were added to them the army of Syria, & of the land of the strangers.
text.KJV: And the merchants of the country, hearing the fame of them, took silver and gold very much, with servants, and came into the camp to buy the children of Israel for slaves: a power also of Syria and of the land of the Philistines joined themselves unto them.
text.NABRE: When the merchants of the region heard of their prowess, they came to the camp, bringing a huge sum of silver and gold, along with fetters, to buy the Israelites as slaves. A force from Edom and from Philistia joined with them.
text.NRSV: When the traders of the region heard what was said to them, they took silver and gold in immense amounts, and fetters, and went to the camp to get the Israelites for slaves. And forces from Syria and the land of the Philistines joined with them.

text.DRB: And Judas saw, & his brethren, that the evils were multiplied, and the armies approached to their borders: and they knew the king's words, which he commanded to do to the people unto destruction and consummation:
text.KJV: Now when Judas and his brethren saw that miseries were multiplied, and that the forces did encamp themselves in their borders: for they knew how the king had given commandment to destroy the people, and utterly abolish them;
text.NABRE: Judas and his brothers saw that evils had multiplied and that armies were encamped within their territory. They learned of the orders which the king had given to destroy and utterly wipe out the people.
text.NRSV: Now Judas and his brothers saw that misfortunes had increased and that the forces were encamped in their territory. They also learned what the king had commanded to be done to the people to cause their final destruction.

text.DRB: and they said every one to his neighbour: Let us set up the abasing of our people, & let us fight for our people, and our holies.
text.KJV: They said one to another, Let us restore the decayed fortune of our people, and let us fight for our people and the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: So they said to one another, “Let us raise our people from their ruin and fight for them and for our sanctuary!”
text.NRSV: But they said to one another, ‘Let us restore the ruins of our people, and fight for our people and the sanctuary.’

text.DRB: And an assembly was gathered, that they should be ready unto battle: & that they should pray and desire mercy & miserations.
text.KJV: Then was the congregation gathered together, that they might be ready for battle, and that they might pray, and ask mercy and compassion.
text.NABRE: The assembly gathered together to prepare for battle and to pray and ask for mercy and compassion.
text.NRSV: So the congregation assembled to be ready for battle, and to pray and ask for mercy and compassion.

text.DRB: And Jerusalem was not inhabited, but was a desert: there was none that came in and went out of her children: and the holy place was conculcated: and the children of strangers were in the castle, there was the inhabitation of the Gentiles, and pleasure was taken away from Jacob, & there failed their pipe and harp.
text.KJV: Now Jerusalem lay void as a wilderness, there was none of her children that went in or out: the sanctuary also was trodden down, and aliens kept the strong hold; the heathen had their habitation in that place; and joy was taken from Jacob, and the pipe with the harp ceased.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem was uninhabited, like a wilderness; not one of her children came in or went out. The sanctuary was trampled on, and foreigners were in the citadel; it was a habitation for Gentiles. Joy had disappeared from Jacob, and the flute and the harp were silent.
text.NRSV: Jerusalem was uninhabited like a wilderness; not one of her children went in or out. The sanctuary was trampled down, and aliens held the citadel; it was a lodging-place for the Gentiles. Joy was taken from Jacob; the flute and the harp ceased to play.

text.DRB: And they gathered together, and came into Maspha against Jerusalem: because the place of prayer in Israel was in Maspha before.
text.KJV: Wherefore the Israelites assembled themselves together, and came to Maspha, over against Jerusalem; for in Maspha was the place where they prayed aforetime in Israel.
text.NABRE: Thus they assembled and went to Mizpah near Jerusalem, because formerly at Mizpah there was a place of prayer for Israel.
text.NRSV: Then they gathered together and went to Mizpah, opposite Jerusalem, because Israel formerly had a place of prayer in Mizpah.

text.DRB: And they fasted that day, and clothed themselves with haircloths, and put ashes on their head: and they rent their garments:
text.KJV: Then they fasted that day, and put on sackcloth, and cast ashes upon their heads, and rent their clothes,
text.NABRE: That day they fasted and wore sackcloth; they sprinkled ashes on their heads and tore their garments.
text.NRSV: They fasted that day, put on sackcloth and sprinkled ashes on their heads, and tore their clothes.

text.DRB: and they laid open the books of the law, out of which the Gentiles searched the similitude of their idols:
text.KJV: And laid open the book of the law, wherein the heathen had sought to paint the likeness of their images.
text.NABRE: They unrolled the scroll of the law, to learn about the things for which the Gentiles consulted the images of their idols.
text.NRSV: And they opened the book of the law to inquire into those matters about which the Gentiles consulted the images of their gods.

text.DRB: and they brought the ornaments of Priests, and first-fruits, & tithes, & raised up Nazareites, that had fulfilled their days:
text.KJV: They brought also the priests’ garments, and the firstfruits, and the tithes: and the Nazarites they stirred up, who had accomplished their days.
text.NABRE: They brought with them the priestly garments, the first fruits, and the tithes; and they brought forward the nazirites who had completed the time of their vows.
text.NRSV: They also brought the vestments of the priesthood and the first fruits and the tithes, and they stirred up the nazirites who had completed their days;

text.DRB: and they cried with a loud voice to heaven, saying: What shall we do with these, & whither shall we carry them?
text.KJV: Then cried they with a loud voice toward heaven, saying, What shall we do with these, and whither shall we carry them away?
text.NABRE: And they cried aloud to Heaven: “What shall we do with these, and where shall we take them?
text.NRSV: and they cried aloud to Heaven, saying, ‘What shall we do with these? Where shall we take them?

text.DRB: And thy holies are conculcated, and they are contaminated, and thy Priests are brought into mourning, & into humiliation.
text.KJV: For thy sanctuary is trodden down and profaned, and thy priests are in heaviness, and brought low.
text.NABRE: For your sanctuary has been trampled on and profaned, and your priests are in mourning and humbled.
text.NRSV: Your sanctuary is trampled down and profaned, and your priests mourn in humiliation.

text.DRB: And behold the Nations are come together against us to destroy us: thou knowest what things they intend against us.
text.KJV: And lo, the heathen are assembled together against us to destroy us: what things they imagine against us, thou knowest.
text.NABRE: Now the Gentiles are gathered together against us to destroy us. You know what they plot against us.
text.NRSV: Here the Gentiles are assembled against us to destroy us; you know what they plot against us.

text.DRB: How shall we be able to stand before their face, unless thou o God do help us?
text.KJV: How shall we be able to stand against them, except thou, O God, be our help?
text.NABRE: How shall we be able to resist them unless you help us?”
text.NRSV: How will we be able to withstand them, if you do not help us?’

text.DRB: And with trumpets they cried out with a loud voice.
text.KJV: Then sounded they with trumpets, and cried with a loud voice.
text.NABRE: Then they blew the trumpets and cried out loudly.
text.NRSV: Then they sounded the trumpets and gave a loud shout.

text.DRB: And after these things Judas appointed captains of the people, tribunes, and centurions, and sergeants, and decurions.
text.KJV: And after this Judas ordained captains over the people, even captains over thousands, and over hundreds, and over fifties, and over tens.
text.NABRE: After this Judas appointed officers for the people, over thousands, over hundreds, over fifties, and over tens.
text.NRSV: After this Judas appointed leaders of the people, in charge of thousands and hundreds and fifties and tens.

text.DRB: And he said to them, that built houses, and despoused wives, and planted vineyards, and to the fearful, that every one should return into his house, according to the law.
text.KJV: But as for such as were building houses, or had betrothed wives, or were planting vineyards, or were fearful, those he commanded that they should return, every man to his own house, according to the law.
text.NABRE: He proclaimed that those who were building houses, or were just married, or were planting vineyards, and those who were afraid, could each return home, according to the law.
text.NRSV: Those who were building houses, or were about to be married, or were planting a vineyard, or were faint-hearted, he told to go home again, in accordance with the law.

text.DRB: And they removed the camp, and pitched at the South of Emmaum.
text.KJV: So the camp removed, and pitched upon the south side of Emmaus.
text.NABRE: Then the army moved off, and they camped to the south of Emmaus.
text.NRSV: Then the army marched out and encamped to the south of Emmaus.

text.DRB: And Judas said: Gird up yourselves, and be mighty sons, and be ready against the morning, that you may fight against these nations, which are assembled against us to destroy us, and our holies,
text.KJV: And Judas said, arm yourselves, and be valiant men, and see that ye be in readiness against the morning, that ye may fight with these nations, that are assembled together against us to destroy us and our sanctuary:
text.NABRE: Judas said: “Arm yourselves and be brave; in the morning be ready to fight these Gentiles who have assembled against us to destroy us and our sanctuary.
text.NRSV: And Judas said, ‘Arm yourselves and be courageous. Be ready early in the morning to fight with these Gentiles who have assembled against us to destroy us and our sanctuary.

text.DRB: because it is better for us to die in battle, than to see the evils of our nation, and of the holies:
text.KJV: For it is better for us to die in battle, than to behold the calamities of our people and our sanctuary.
text.NABRE: It is better for us to die in battle than to witness the evils befalling our nation and our sanctuary.
text.NRSV: It is better for us to die in battle than to see the misfortunes of our nation and of the sanctuary.

text.DRB: but as it shall be the will in heaven, so be it done.
text.KJV: Nevertheless, as the will of God is in heaven, so let him do.
text.NABRE: Whatever is willed in heaven will be done.”
text.NRSV: But as his will in heaven may be, so shall he do.’

text.KJV: Then took Gorgias five thousand footmen, and a thousand of the best horsemen, and removed out of the camp by night;
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Victory over Gorgias. Now Gorgias took five thousand infantry and a thousand picked cavalry, and this detachment set out at night
text.NRSV: The Battle at Emmaus 4
text.DRB: And Gorgias took five thousand men, & a thousand chosen horsemen: & they removed the camp by night,
that they might approach to the camp of the Jews, and might strike them suddenly: & the children that were of the castle, were their guides.
text.KJV: To the end he might rush in upon the camp of the Jews, and smite them suddenly. And the men of the fortress were his guides.
text.NABRE: in order to fall upon the camp of the Jews in a surprise attack. Some from the citadel were his guides.
text.NRSV: to fall upon the camp of the Jews and attack them suddenly. Men from the citadel were his guides.

text.DRB: And Judas heard, & arose, he and the mighties to strike the power of the king's army, that were in Emmaum.
text.KJV: Now when Judas heard thereof he himself removed, and the valiant men with him, that he might smite the king’s army which was at Emmaus,
text.NABRE: Judas heard of it and himself set out with his soldiers to attack the king’s army at Emmaus
text.NRSV: But Judas heard of it, and he and his warriors moved out to attack the king’s force in Emmaus

text.DRB: For as yet the army was dispersed from the camp.
text.KJV: While as yet the forces were dispersed from the camp.
text.NABRE: while these forces were still scattered away from the camp.
text.NRSV: while the division was still absent from the camp.

text.DRB: And Gorgias came into the camp of Judas by night, and found no man, & he sought them in the mountains: because he said: These fly from us.
text.KJV: In the mean season came Gorgias by night into the camp of Judas: and when he found no man there, he sought them in the mountains: for said he, These fellows flee from us
text.NABRE: During the night Gorgias came into the camp of Judas, and found no one there; so he sought them in the mountains, saying, “They are fleeing from us.”
text.NRSV: When Gorgias entered the camp of Judas by night, he found no one there, so he looked for them in the hills, because he said, ‘These men are running away from us.’

text.DRB: And when the day was come, Judas appeared in the field with three thousand men only, which had not harness and swords as they would:
text.KJV: But as soon as it was day, Judas shewed himself in the plain with three thousand men, who nevertheless had neither armour nor swords to their minds.
text.NABRE: But at daybreak Judas appeared in the plain with three thousand men; furthermore they lacked the helmets and swords they wanted.
text.NRSV: At daybreak Judas appeared in the plain with three thousand men, but they did not have armour and swords such as they desired.

text.DRB: and they saw the camp of the Gentiles strong, and the men in brigantines, and horsemen round about them, and these were skillful to battle.
text.KJV: And they saw the camp of the heathen, that it was strong and well harnessed, and compassed round about with horsemen; and these were expert of war.
text.NABRE: They saw the army of the Gentiles, strong, breastplated, and flanked with cavalry, and made up of experienced soldiers.
text.NRSV: And they saw the camp of the Gentiles, strong and fortified, with cavalry all around it; and these men were trained in war.

text.DRB: And Judas said to the men that were with him: Fear not the multitude of them, & of their assault be not afraid.
text.KJV: Then said Judas to the men that were with him, Fear ye not their multitude, neither be ye afraid of their assault.
text.NABRE: Judas said to the men with him: “Do not fear their numbers or dread their attack.
text.NRSV: But Judas said to those who were with him, ‘Do not fear their numbers or be afraid when they charge.

text.DRB: Remember in what sort our fathers were saved in the red sea, when Pharao with a great army followed them.
text.KJV: Remember how our fathers were delivered in the Red sea, when Pharaoh pursued them with an army.
text.NABRE: Remember how our ancestors were saved in the Red Sea, when Pharaoh pursued them with an army.
text.NRSV: Remember how our ancestors were saved at the Red Sea, when Pharaoh with his forces pursued them.

text.DRB: And now let us cry towards heaven: and our Lord will have mercy on us, and will be mindful of the testament of our fathers, and will destroy this army, before our face this day:
text.KJV: Now therefore let us cry unto heaven, if peradventure the Lord will have mercy upon us, and remember the covenant of our fathers, and destroy this host before our face this day:
text.NABRE: So now let us cry to Heaven in the hope that he will favor us, remember the covenant with our ancestors, and destroy this army before us today.
text.NRSV: And now, let us cry to Heaven, to see whether he will favour us and remember his covenant with our ancestors and crush this army before us today.

text.DRB: and all Nations shall know that there is one that redeemeth and delivereth Israel.
text.KJV: That so all the heathen may know that there is one who delivereth and saveth Israel.
text.NABRE: All the Gentiles shall know that there is One who redeems and delivers Israel.”
text.NRSV: Then all the Gentiles will know that there is one who redeems and saves Israel.’

text.KJV: Then the strangers lifted up their eyes, and saw them coming over against them.
text.NABRE: When the foreigners looked up and saw them marching toward them,
text.NRSV: When the foreigners looked up and saw them coming against them,
text.DRB: And the aliens lifted up their eyes, and saw them coming against them.
And they went out of the camp into battle, and these that were with Judas, sounded with the trumpet.
text.KJV: Wherefore they went out of the camp to battle; but they that were with Judas sounded their trumpets.
text.NABRE: they came out of their camp for battle. The men with Judas blew the trumpet, and
text.NRSV: they went out from their camp to battle. Then the men with Judas blew their trumpets

text.KJV: So they joined battle, and the heathen being discomfited fled into the plain.
text.NABRE: joined the battle. They crushed the Gentiles, who fled toward the plain.
text.NRSV: and engaged in battle. The Gentiles were crushed, and fled into the plain,
text.DRB: And they met together: and the Gentiles were discomfited, and fled into the plain.
But all the hindmost fell by the sword, and they pursued them as far as Gezeron, and even to the plains of Idumea, and of Azotus, and of Jamnia: and there fell of them even to three thousand men.
text.KJV: Howbeit all the hindmost of them were slain with the sword: for they pursued them unto Gazera, and unto the plains of Idumea, and Azotus, and Jamnia, so that there were slain of them upon a three thousand men.
text.NABRE: Their whole rear guard fell by the sword, and they were pursued as far as Gazara and the plains of Idumaea, to Azotus and Jamnia. About three thousand of their men fell.
text.NRSV: and all those in the rear fell by the sword. They pursued them to Gazara, and to the plains of Idumea, and to Azotus and Jamnia; and three thousand of them fell.

text.DRB: And Judas returned, and his army following him.
text.KJV: This done, Judas returned again with his host from pursuing them,
text.NABRE: When Judas and the army returned from the pursuit,
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his force turned back from pursuing them,

text.DRB: And he said to the people: Covet not the spoils: because there is battle against us,
text.KJV: And said to the people, Be not greedy of the spoil inasmuch as there is a battle before us,
text.NABRE: he said to the people: “Do not be greedy for plunder; for there is a fight ahead of us,
text.NRSV: and he said to the people, ‘Do not be greedy for plunder, for there is a battle before us;

text.DRB: and Gorgias & his army are near us in the mount: but stand ye now against our enemies, and overthrow them, and you shall take the spoils afterwards secure.
text.KJV: And Gorgias and his host are here by us in the mountain: but stand ye now against our enemies, and overcome them, and after this ye may boldly take the spoils.
text.NABRE: and Gorgias and his army are near us on the mountain. But now stand firm against our enemies and fight them. Afterward you can freely take the plunder.”
text.NRSV: Gorgias and his force are near us in the hills. But stand now against our enemies and fight them, and afterwards seize the plunder boldly.’

text.DRB: And as Judas was speaking these words, lo a certain part appeared looking forth from the mountain.
text.KJV: As Judas was yet speaking these words, there appeared a part of them looking out of the mountain:
text.NABRE: As Judas was finishing this speech, a detachment appeared, looking down from the mountain.
text.NRSV: Just as Judas was finishing this speech, a detachment appeared, coming out of the hills.

text.DRB: And Gorgias saw that his men were turned to flight, & that they burnt the camp: for the smoke that was seen declared what was done.
text.KJV: Who when they perceived that the Jews had put their host to flight and were burning the tents; for the smoke that was seen declared what was done:
text.NABRE: They saw that their army had been put to flight and their camp was burning. The smoke they saw revealed what had happened.
text.NRSV: They saw that their army had been put to flight, and that the Jews were burning the camp, for the smoke that was seen showed what had happened.

text.DRB: Which things seen they feared exceedingly, beholding withal both Judas, and the army in the plain ready to battle.
text.KJV: When therefore they perceived these things, they were sore afraid, and seeing also the host of Judas in the plain ready to fight,
text.NABRE: When they realized this, they completely lost heart; and when they also saw the army of Judas in the plain ready to attack,
text.NRSV: When they perceived this, they were greatly frightened, and when they also saw the army of Judas drawn up in the plain for battle,

text.DRB: And they did all fly into the plain of the aliens:
text.KJV: They fled every one into the land of strangers.
text.NABRE: they all fled to the land of the foreigners.
text.NRSV: they all fled into the land of the Philistines.

text.DRB: & Judas returned to the spoils of the camp, & they took much gold, and silver, and hyacinth, and purple of the sea, and great riches.
text.KJV: Then Judas returned to spoil the tents, where they got much gold, and silver, and blue silk, and purple of the sea, and great riches.
text.NABRE: Then Judas went back to plunder the camp, and they took much gold and silver, cloth dyed blue and marine purple, and great treasure.
text.NRSV: Then Judas returned to plunder the camp, and they seized a great amount of gold and silver, and cloth dyed blue and sea purple, and great riches.

text.DRB: And turning they sung an hymn, & blessed God towards heaven, because he is good, because his mercy is for ever.
text.KJV: After this they went home, and sung a song of thanksgiving, and praised the Lord in heaven: because it is good, because his mercy endureth forever.
text.NABRE: As they returned, they were singing hymns and glorifying Heaven, “who is good, whose mercy endures forever.”
text.NRSV: On their return they sang hymns and praises to Heaven—‘For he is good, for his mercy endures for ever.’

text.DRB: And great salvation was made in Israel in that day.
text.KJV: Thus Israel had a great deliverance that day.
text.NABRE: Thus Israel experienced a great deliverance that day.
text.NRSV: Thus Israel had a great deliverance that day.

text.DRB: And whosoever escaped of the aliens, they came and told Lysias all things that had chanced.
text.KJV: Now all the strangers that had escaped came and told Lysias what had happened:
text.NABRE: Victory over Lysias. But those of the foreigners who had escaped went and told Lysias all that had occurred.
text.NRSV: First Campaign of Lysias Those of the foreigners who escaped went and reported to Lysias all that had happened.

text.DRB: Which when he heard being amazed he fainted in mind: that such things chanced not in Israel, as he would, & such as the king commanded.
text.KJV: Who, when he heard thereof, was confounded and discouraged, because neither such things as he would were done unto Israel, nor such things as the king commanded him were come to pass.
text.NABRE: When he heard it he was disturbed and discouraged, because things had not turned out in Israel as he intended and as the king had ordered.
text.NRSV: When he heard it, he was perplexed and discouraged, for things had not happened to Israel as he had intended, nor had they turned out as the king had ordered.

text.DRB: And the year following Lysias gathered of chosen men three score thousand, & of horsemen five thousand, to overthrow them.
text.KJV: The next year therefore following Lysias gathered together threescore thousand choice men of foot, and five thousand horsemen, that he might subdue them.
text.NABRE: So the following year he gathered together sixty thousand picked men and five thousand cavalry, to fight them.
text.NRSV: But the next year he mustered sixty thousand picked infantry and five thousand cavalry to subdue them.

text.DRB: And they came into Jewry, and pitched the camp in Bethoron, and Judas met them with ten thousand men.
text.KJV: So they came into Idumea, and pitched their tents at Bethsura, and Judas met them with ten thousand men.
text.NABRE: They came into Idumea and camped at Beth-zur, and Judas met them with ten thousand men.
text.NRSV: They came into Idumea and encamped at Beth-zur, and Judas met them with ten thousand men.

text.DRB: And they saw the army strong, and he prayed, and said: Blessed art thou o saviour of Israel, which brakest the assault of the mighty by the hand of thy servant David, & didst deliver the camp of the aliens into the hand of Jonathas Saul's son, and of his esquire.
text.KJV: And when he saw that mighty army, he prayed and said, Blessed art thou, O Saviour of Israel, who didst quell the violence of the mighty man by the hand of thy servant David, and gavest the host of strangers into the hands of Jonathan the son of Saul, and his armourbearer;
text.NABRE: Seeing that the army was strong, he prayed thus: “Blessed are you, Savior of Israel, who crushed the attack of the mighty one by the hand of your servant David and delivered the foreign camp into the hand of Jonathan, the son of Saul, and his armor-bearer.
text.NRSV: When he saw that their army was strong, he prayed, saying, ‘Blessed are you, O Saviour of Israel, who crushed the attack of the mighty warrior by the hand of your servant David, and gave the camp of the Philistines into the hands of Jonathan son of Saul, and of the man who carried his armour.

text.DRB: shut up this army in the hand of thy people Israel, and let them be confounded in their army and horsemen.
text.KJV: Shut up this army in the hand of thy people Israel, and let them be confounded in their power and horsemen:
text.NABRE: Give this army into the hands of your people Israel; make them ashamed of their troops and their cavalry.
text.NRSV: Hem in this army by the hand of your people Israel, and let them be ashamed of their troops and their cavalry.

text.DRB: Give them fear, and consume the boldness of their strength, and let them be shaken with their contrition,
text.KJV: Make them to be of no courage, and cause the boldness of their strength to fall away, and let them quake at their destruction:
text.NABRE: Strike them with cowardice, weaken the boldness of their strength, and let them tremble at their own destruction.
text.NRSV: Fill them with cowardice; melt the boldness of their strength; let them tremble in their destruction.

text.DRB: cast them down with the sword of them that love thee: & let all that know thy name, praise thee in hymns.
text.KJV: Cast them down with the sword of them that love thee, and let all those that know thy name praise thee with thanksgiving.
text.NABRE: Strike them down by the sword of those who love you, that all who know your name may sing your praise.”
text.NRSV: Strike them down with the sword of those who love you, and let all who know your name praise you with hymns.’

text.DRB: And they joined battle: and there fell of the army of Lysias five thousand men.
text.KJV: So they joined battle; and there were slain of the host of Lysias about five thousand men, even before them were they slain.
text.NABRE: Then they engaged in battle, and about five thousand of Lysias’ army fell in hand-to-hand fighting.
text.NRSV: Then both sides attacked, and there fell of the army of Lysias five thousand men; they fell in action.

text.DRB: And Lysias seeing the flight of his men, and the boldness of the Jews, & that they are ready either to live or to die manfully, he went to Antioch, and chose soldiers, that being multiplied they might come again into Jewry.
text.KJV: Now when Lysias saw his army put to flight, and the manliness of Judas’ soldiers, and how they were ready either to live or die valiantly, he went into Antiochia, and gathered together a company of strangers, and having made his army greater than it was, he purposed to come again into Judea.
text.NABRE: When Lysias saw the tide of the battle turning, and the increased boldness of Judas, whose men were ready either to live or to die nobly, he withdrew to Antioch and began to recruit mercenaries so as to return to Judea with greater numbers.
text.NRSV: When Lysias saw the rout of his troops and observed the boldness that inspired those of Judas, and how ready they were either to live or to die nobly, he withdrew to Antioch and enlisted mercenaries in order to invade Judea again with an even larger army.

text.KJV: Then said Judas and his brethren, Behold, our enemies are discomfited: let us go up to cleanse and dedicate the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: Purification and Rededication of the Temple. Then Judas and his brothers said, “Now that our enemies have been crushed, let us go up to purify the sanctuary and rededicate it.”
text.NRSV: Cleansing and Dedication of the Temple Then Judas and his brothers said, ‘See, our enemies are crushed; let us go up to cleanse the sanctuary and dedicate it.’
text.DRB: But Judas, and his brethren said: Behold our enemies are discomfited: let us go up now to cleanse the holy places, & to renew them.

text.KJV: Upon this all the host assembled themselves together, and went up into mount Sion.
text.NABRE: So the whole army assembled, and went up to Mount Zion.
text.NRSV: So all the army assembled and went up to Mount Zion.
text.DRB: And all the army assembled together, and they went up into mount Sion.
And they saw the sanctification desert, and the altar profaned, and the gates burnt, and in the courts shrubs grown up as in a forest, or on the mountains, and the chambers thrown down.
text.KJV: And when they saw the sanctuary desolate, and the altar profaned, and the gates burned up, and shrubs growing in the courts as in a forest, or in one of the mountains, yea, and the priests’ chambers pulled down;
text.NABRE: They found the sanctuary desolate, the altar desecrated, the gates burnt, weeds growing in the courts as in a thicket or on some mountain, and the priests’ chambers demolished.
text.NRSV: There they saw the sanctuary desolate, the altar profaned, and the gates burned. In the courts they saw bushes sprung up as in a thicket, or as on one of the mountains. They saw also the chambers of the priests in ruins.

text.DRB: And they rent their garments, and lamented with a great Lamentation, and laid ashes upon their head:
text.KJV: They rent their clothes, and made great lamentation, and cast ashes upon their heads,
text.NABRE: Then they tore their garments and made great lamentation; they sprinkled their heads with ashes
text.NRSV: Then they tore their clothes and mourned with great lamentation; they sprinkled themselves with ashes

text.DRB: and they fell on their face upon the earth, & cried out with trumpets of significations, & cried towards heaven.
text.KJV: And fell down flat to the ground upon their faces, and blew an alarm with the trumpets, and cried toward heaven.
text.NABRE: and prostrated themselves. And when the signal was given with trumpets, they cried out to Heaven.
text.NRSV: and fell face down on the ground. And when the signal was given with the trumpets, they cried out to Heaven.

text.DRB: Then Judas ordained men to fight against them that were in the castle, till they cleansed the holy places.
text.KJV: Then Judas appointed certain men to fight against those that were in the fortress, until he had cleansed the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: Judas appointed men to attack those in the citadel, while he purified the sanctuary.
text.NRSV: Then Judas detailed men to fight against those in the citadel until he had cleansed the sanctuary.

text.DRB: And he chose Priests without spot, having their will in the law of God:
text.KJV: So he chose priests of blameless conversation, such as had pleasure in the law:
text.NABRE: He chose blameless priests, devoted to the law;
text.NRSV: He chose blameless priests devoted to the law,

text.DRB: and they cleansed the holy places, and took away the stones of contamination into an unclean place.
text.KJV: Who cleansed the sanctuary, and bare out the defiled stones into an unclean place.
text.NABRE: these purified the sanctuary and carried away the stones of the defilement to an unclean place.
text.NRSV: and they cleansed the sanctuary and removed the defiled stones to an unclean place.

text.DRB: And he considered of the altar of holocausts, that was profaned, what he should do with it.
text.KJV: And when as they consulted what to do with the altar of burnt offerings, which was profaned;
text.NABRE: They deliberated what ought to be done with the altar for burnt offerings that had been desecrated.
text.NRSV: They deliberated what to do about the altar of burnt-offering, which had been profaned.

text.DRB: And good counsel came to their minds, to destroy it: lest perhaps it might be a reproach to them, because the Gentiles contaminated it, and they threw it down.
text.KJV: They thought it best to pull it down, lest it should be a reproach to them, because the heathen had defiled it: wherefore they pulled it down,
text.NABRE: They decided it best to tear it down, lest it be a lasting shame to them that the Gentiles had defiled it; so they tore down the altar.
text.NRSV: And they thought it best to tear it down, so that it would not be a lasting shame to them that the Gentiles had defiled it. So they tore down the altar,

text.DRB: And they laid up the stones in the mount of the house in a place convenient, till their should come a Prophet, & give answer concerning them.
text.KJV: And laid up the stones in the mountain of the temple in a convenient place, until there should come a prophet to shew what should be done with them.
text.NABRE: They stored the stones in a suitable place on the temple mount, until the coming of a prophet who could determine what to do with them.
text.NRSV: and stored the stones in a convenient place on the temple hill until a prophet should come to tell what to do with them.

text.DRB: And they took whole stones according to the law, and builded a new altar according to that which was before:
text.KJV: Then they took whole stones according to the law, and built a new altar according to the former;
text.NABRE: Then they took uncut stones, according to the law, and built a new altar like the former one.
text.NRSV: Then they took unhewn stones, as the law directs, and built a new altar like the former one.

text.DRB: and they built the holy places, and the things that were within the temple inward: and the temple, and the courts they sanctified.
text.KJV: And made up the sanctuary, and the things that were within the temple, and hallowed the courts.
text.NABRE: They also repaired the sanctuary and the interior of the temple and consecrated the courts.
text.NRSV: They also rebuilt the sanctuary and the interior of the temple, and consecrated the courts.

text.DRB: And they made the holy vessels new, and brought in the candlestick, and the altar of incenses, and the table into the temple.
text.KJV: They made also new holy vessels, and into the temple they brought the candlestick, and the altar of burnt offerings, and of incense, and the table.
text.NABRE: They made new sacred vessels and brought the lampstand, the altar of incense, and the table into the temple.
text.NRSV: They made new holy vessels, and brought the lampstand, the altar of incense, and the table into the temple.

text.DRB: And they put incense upon the altar, and lighted the lamps, that were upon the candlestick, and they gave light in the temple.
text.KJV: And upon the altar they burned incense, and the lamps that were upon the candlestick they lighted, that they might give light in the temple.
text.NABRE: Then they burned incense on the altar and lighted the lamps on the lampstand, and these illuminated the temple.
text.NRSV: Then they offered incense on the altar and lit the lamps on the lampstand, and these gave light in the temple.

text.DRB: And they set loaves upon the table, and hung up the veils, and finished all the works, that they had made.
text.KJV: Furthermore they set the loaves upon the table, and spread out the veils, and finished all the works which they had begun to make.
text.NABRE: They also put loaves on the table and hung up the curtains. Thus they finished all the work they had undertaken.
text.NRSV: They placed the bread on the table and hung up the curtains. Thus they finished all the work they had undertaken.

text.KJV: Now on the five and twentieth day of the ninth month, which is called the month Casleu, in the hundred forty and eighth year, they rose up betimes in the morning,
text.NABRE: They rose early on the morning of the twenty-fifth day of the ninth month, that is, the month of Kislev, in the year one hundred and forty-eight,
text.NRSV: Early in the morning on the twenty-fifth day of the ninth month, which is the month of Chislev, in the one hundred and forty-eighth year,
text.DRB: And before the morning they arose the five and twentieth day of the ninth month (this is the month of Casleu) of the hundreth & eight and fortieth year:

text.KJV: And offered sacrifice according to the law upon the new altar of burnt offerings, which they had made.
text.NABRE: and offered sacrifice according to the law on the new altar for burnt offerings that they had made.
text.NRSV: they rose and offered sacrifice, as the law directs, on the new altar of burnt-offering that they had built.
text.DRB: and they offered sacrifice according to the law upon the new altar of holocausts, which they made.
According to the time and according to the day, wherein the heathen contaminated it, in the same was it renewed in canticles, & harps, and lutes, and in cymbals.
text.KJV: Look, at what time and what day the heathen had profaned it, even in that was it dedicated with songs, and citherns, and harps, and cymbals.
text.NABRE: On the anniversary of the day on which the Gentiles had desecrated it, on that very day it was rededicated with songs, harps, lyres, and cymbals.
text.NRSV: At the very season and on the very day that the Gentiles had profaned it, it was dedicated with songs and harps and lutes and cymbals.

text.DRB: And all the people fell on their face, and they adored toward heaven, and blessed him that prospered them.
text.KJV: Then all the people fell upon their faces, worshipping and praising the God of heaven, who had given them good success.
text.NABRE: All the people prostrated themselves and adored and praised Heaven, who had given them success.
text.NRSV: All the people fell on their faces and worshipped and blessed Heaven, who had prospered them.

text.KJV: And so they kept the dedication of the altar eight days and offered burnt offerings with gladness, and sacrificed the sacrifice of deliverance and praise.
text.NABRE: For eight days they celebrated the dedication of the altar and joyfully offered burnt offerings and sacrifices of deliverance and praise.
text.NRSV: So they celebrated the dedication of the altar for eight days, and joyfully offered burnt-offerings; they offered a sacrifice of well-being and a thanksgiving-offering.
text.DRB: And they made the dedication of the altar eight days, and they offered holocausts with joy, and sacrifice of salvation, and of praise.
And they adorned the face of the temple with gold crowns, and little shields: & they dedicated the gates, and the chambers, and hanged doors upon them.
text.KJV: They decked also the forefront of the temple with crowns of gold, and with shields; and the gates and the chambers they renewed, and hanged doors upon them.
text.NABRE: They ornamented the facade of the temple with gold crowns and shields; they repaired the gates and the priests’ chambers and furnished them with doors.
text.NRSV: They decorated the front of the temple with golden crowns and small shields; they restored the gates and the chambers for the priests, and fitted them with doors.

text.DRB: And there was made exceeding great joy in the people, and the reproach of the Gentiles was turned away.
text.KJV: Thus was there very great gladness among the people, for that the reproach of the heathen was put away.
text.NABRE: There was great joy among the people now that the disgrace brought by the Gentiles was removed.
text.NRSV: There was very great joy among the people, and the disgrace brought by the Gentiles was removed.

text.KJV: Moreover Judas and his brethren with the whole congregation of Israel ordained, that the days of the dedication of the altar should be kept in their season from year to year by the space of eight days, from the five and twentieth day of the month Casleu, with mirth and gladness.
text.NABRE: Then Judas and his brothers and the entire assembly of Israel decreed that every year for eight days, from the twenty-fifth day of the month Kislev, the days of the dedication of the altar should be observed with joy and gladness on the anniversary.
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his brothers and all the assembly of Israel determined that every year at that season the days of dedication of the altar should be observed with joy and gladness for eight days, beginning with the twenty-fifth day of the month of Chislev.
text.DRB: And Judas decreed, and his brethren, and all the Church of Israel, that the day of the dedication of the altar be kept in the times thereof from year to year for eight days, from the five & twentieth day of the month Casleu, with joy and gladness.
And they builded at that time mount Sion, and round about high walls, and strong towers, lest sometime the Gentiles should come, and conculcate it as they did before.
text.KJV: At that time also they builded up the mount Sion with high walls and strong towers round about, lest the Gentiles should come and tread it down as they had done before.
text.NABRE: At that time they built high walls and strong towers around Mount Zion, to prevent the Gentiles from coming and trampling it as they had done before.
text.NRSV: At that time they fortified Mount Zion with high walls and strong towers all round, to keep the Gentiles from coming and trampling them down as they had done before.

text.DRB: And he placed an army there, to keep it, & he fenced it to keep Bethsura, that the people might have a munition against the face of Idumea.
text.KJV: And they set there a garrison to keep it, and fortified Bethsura to preserve it; that the people might have a defence against Idumea.
text.NABRE: Judas also placed a garrison there to protect it, and likewise fortified Beth-zur, that the people might have a stronghold facing Idumea.
text.NRSV: Judas stationed a garrison there to guard it; he also fortified Beth-zur to guard it, so that the people might have a stronghold that faced Idumea.

text.KJV: Now when the nations round about heard that the altar was built and the sanctuary renewed as before, it displeased them very much.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Victories over Hostile Neighbors. When the nations round about heard that the altar had been rebuilt and the sanctuary restored as before, they were enraged.
text.NRSV: Wars with Neighbouring Peoples 5
text.DRB: And it came to pass, as the Gentiles round about heard that the altar was builded up, and the sanctuary as before, they were exceeding angry:

text.KJV: Wherefore they thought to destroy the generation of Jacob that was among them, and thereupon they began to slay and destroy the people.
text.NABRE: So they decided to destroy the descendants of Jacob who were among them, and they began to kill and eradicate the people.
text.NRSV: and they determined to destroy the descendants of Jacob who lived among them. So they began to kill and destroy among the people.
text.DRB: and they thought to take away the stock of Jacob, that were among them, and they began to kill of the people, and to persecute.
And Judas fought against the children of Esau in Idumea, & them that were in Acrabathane: because they beset the Israelites round about, and he struck them with a great plague.
text.KJV: Then Judas fought against the children of Esau in Idumea at Arabattine, because they besieged Gael: and he gave them a great overthrow, and abated their courage, and took their spoils.
text.NABRE: Then Judas attacked the Edomites at Akrabattene in Idumea, because they were blockading Israel; he dealt them a heavy blow, humbled and despoiled them.
text.NRSV: But Judas made war on the descendants of Esau in Idumea, at Akrabattene, because they kept lying in wait for Israel. He dealt them a heavy blow and humbled them and despoiled them.

text.DRB: And he remembered the malice of the children of Bean, which were to the people a snare and a scandal, lying in wait for them in the way.
text.KJV: Also he remembered the injury of the children of Bean, who had been a snare and an offence unto the people, in that they lay in wait for them in the ways.
text.NABRE: He also remembered the malice of the Baeanites, who had become a snare and a stumbling block to the people by ambushing them along the roads.
text.NRSV: He also remembered the wickedness of the sons of Baean, who were a trap and a snare to the people and ambushed them on the highways.

text.DRB: And they were shut up by him in towers, and he came near to them, and anathematized them, and burnt their towers with fire, with all that were in them.
text.KJV: He shut them up therefore in the towers, and encamped against them, and destroyed them utterly, and burned the towers of that place with fire, and all that were therein.
text.NABRE: He forced them to take refuge in towers, which he besieged; he put them under the ban and burned down their towers along with all who were in them.
text.NRSV: They were shut up by him in their towers; and he encamped against them, vowed their complete destruction, and burned with fire their towers and all who were in them.

text.DRB: And he passed to the children of Ammon, and found a strong band, and a copious people, and Timothee their captain:
text.KJV: Afterward he passed over to the children of Ammon, where he found a mighty power, and much people, with Timotheus their captain.
text.NABRE: Then he crossed over to the Ammonites, where he found a strong army and a large body of people with Timothy as their leader.
text.NRSV: Then he crossed over to attack the Ammonites, where he found a strong band and many people, with Timothy as their leader.

text.DRB: and he made many battles with them, and they were discomfited in their sight, & he struck them:
text.KJV: So he fought many battles with them, till at length they were discomfited before him; and he smote them.
text.NABRE: He fought many battles with them, routed them, and struck them down.
text.NRSV: He engaged in many battles with them, and they were crushed before him; he struck them down.

text.DRB: and he took Gazer the city, and her daughters, and returned into Jewry.
text.KJV: And when he had taken Jazar, with the towns belonging thereto, he returned into Judea.
text.NABRE: After seizing Jazer and its villages, he returned to Judea.
text.NRSV: He also took Jazer and its villages; then he returned to Judea.

text.DRB: And the Gentiles that are in Galaad, gathered together against the Israelites, that were in their coasts to dispatch them: and they fled into the fortress of Datheman.
text.KJV: Then the heathen that were at Galaad assembled themselves together against the Israelites that were in their quarters, to destroy them; but they fled to the fortress of Dathema.
text.NABRE: Liberation of Jews in Galilee and Gilead. The Gentiles in Gilead assembled to destroy the Israelites who were in their territory; these then fled to the stronghold of Dathema.
text.NRSV: Liberation of Galilean Jews Now the Gentiles in Gilead gathered together against the Israelites who lived in their territory, and planned to destroy them. But they fled to the stronghold of Dathema,

text.DRB: And they sent letters to Judas, and his brethren, saying: The heathen round about are gathered together against us, to dispatch us:
text.KJV: And sent letters unto Judas and his brethren, The heathen that are round about us are assembled together against us to destroy us:
text.NABRE: They sent a letter to Judas and his brothers saying: “The Gentiles around us have assembled against us to destroy us,
text.NRSV: and sent to Judas and his brothers a letter that said, ‘The Gentiles around us have gathered together to destroy us.

text.DRB: and they prepare to come, and to occupy the fortress into which we are fled: and Timothee is the captain of their host.
text.KJV: And they are preparing to come and take the fortress whereunto we are fled, Timotheus being captain of their host.
text.NABRE: and they are preparing to come and seize this stronghold to which we have fled. Timothy is the leader of their army.
text.NRSV: They are preparing to come and capture the stronghold to which we have fled, and Timothy is leading their forces.

text.DRB: Now therefore come, & deliver us, out of their hands, because a multitude of us is fallen.
text.KJV: Come now therefore, and deliver us from their hands, for many of us are slain:
text.NABRE: Come at once to rescue us from them, for many of us have fallen.
text.NRSV: Now then, come and rescue us from their hands, for many of us have fallen,

text.DRB: And all our brethren, that were in the places of Tubin, are slain: and they led away their wives captives, and the children, and the spoils, and they have slain there almost a thousand men.
text.KJV: Yea, all our brethren that were in the places of Tobie are put to death: their wives and their children also they have carried away captives, and borne away their stuff; and they have destroyed there about a thousand men.
text.NABRE: All our kindred who were in the territory of the Tobiads have been killed; the Gentiles have captured their wives, their children and their goods, and they have slain there about a thousand men.”
text.NRSV: and all our kindred who were in the land of Tob have been killed; the enemy have captured their wives and children and goods, and have destroyed about a thousand people there.’

text.DRB: And the epistles were yet in reading, and lo other messengers came out of Galilee their coats rent, reporting according to these words:
text.KJV: While these letters were yet reading, behold, there came other messengers from Galilee with their clothes rent, who reported on this wise,
text.NABRE: While they were reading this letter, suddenly other messengers, with garments torn, arrived from Galilee to deliver a similar message:
text.NRSV: While the letter was still being read, other messengers, with their garments torn, came from Galilee and made a similar report;

text.DRB: saying that there were assembled against them from Ptolemais, and Tyre, and Sidon, and all Galilee is replenished with aliens, to consume us.
text.KJV: And said, They of Ptolemais, and of Tyrus, and Sidon, and all Galilee of the Gentiles, are assembled together against us to consume us.
text.NABRE: that “the inhabitants of Ptolemais, Tyre, and Sidon, and the whole of Gentile Galilee have joined forces to destroy us.”
text.NRSV: they said that the people of Ptolemais and Tyre and Sidon, and all Galilee of the Gentiles, had gathered together against them ‘to annihilate us.’

text.DRB: And as Judas and the people heard these words, a great assembly assembled together to consider what they should do for their brethren, that were in tribulation, and were expugned of them.
text.KJV: Now when Judas and the people heard these words, there assembled a great congregation together, to consult what they should do for their brethren, that were in trouble, and assaulted of them.
text.NABRE: When Judas and the people heard this, a great assembly convened to consider what they should do for their kindred who were in distress and being attacked by enemies.
text.NRSV: When Judas and the people heard these messages, a great assembly was called to determine what they should do for their kindred who were in distress and were being attacked by enemies.

text.KJV: Then said Judas unto Simon his brother, Choose thee out men, and go and deliver thy brethren that are in Galilee, for I and Jonathan my brother will go into the country of Galaad.
text.NABRE: Judas said to his brother Simon: “Choose men for yourself, and go, rescue your kindred in Galilee; my brother Jonathan and I will go to Gilead.”
text.NRSV: Then Judas said to his brother Simon, ‘Choose your men and go and rescue your kindred in Galilee; Jonathan my brother and I will go to Gilead.’
text.DRB: And Judas said to Simon his brother: Choose thee men, and go, and deliver thy brethren in Galilee: and I, and my brother Jonathas will go into the country of Galaad.
And he left Joseph the son of Zacharie, and Azarias, captains of the people with the residue of the army in Jurie to keep it:
text.KJV: So he left Joseph the son of Zacharias, and Azarias, captains of the people, with the remnant of the host in Judea to keep it.
text.NABRE: He left Joseph, son of Zechariah, and Azariah, leader of the people, with the rest of the army in Judea to guard it.
text.NRSV: But he left Joseph, son of Zechariah, and Azariah, a leader of the people, with the rest of the forces, in Judea to guard it;

text.DRB: and he commanded them, saying: Be ye over this people: & make no battle against the heathen, till we return.
text.KJV: Unto whom he gave commandment, saying, Take ye the charge of this people, and see that ye make not war against the heathen until the time that we come again.
text.NABRE: He commanded them, “Take charge of these people, but do not join battle against the Gentiles until we return.”
text.NRSV: and he gave them this command, ‘Take charge of this people, but do not engage in battle with the Gentiles until we return.’

text.DRB: And there were parted to Simon three thousand men, to go into Galilee: but to Judas eight thousand to go into the country of Galaad.
text.KJV: Now unto Simon were given three thousand men to go into Galilee, and unto Judas eight thousand men for the country of Galaad.
text.NABRE: Three thousand men were allotted to Simon to go into Galilee, and eight thousand men to Judas, for Gilead.
text.NRSV: Then three thousand men were assigned to Simon to go to Galilee, and eight thousand to Judas for Gilead.

text.DRB: And Simon went into Galilee, & made many battles with the heathen: and the heathen were discomfited before his face, and he pursued them even to the gate of Ptolemais.
text.KJV: Then went Simon into Galilee, where he fought many battles with the heathen, so that the heathen were discomfited by him.
text.NABRE: Simon went into Galilee and fought many battles with the Gentiles. They were crushed before him,
text.NRSV: So Simon went to Galilee and fought many battles against the Gentiles, and the Gentiles were crushed before him.

text.DRB: And there fell of the heathen almost three thousand men, & he took the spoils of them,
text.KJV: And he pursued them unto the gate of Ptolemais; and there were slain of the heathen about three thousand men, whose spoils he took.
text.NABRE: and he pursued them to the very gate of Ptolemais. About three thousand of the Gentiles fell, and he gathered their spoils.
text.NRSV: He pursued them to the gate of Ptolemais; as many as three thousand of the Gentiles fell, and he despoiled them.

text.DRB: and he took them that were in Galilee & in Arbatis with their wives, and children, & all things that they had, and he brought them into Jurie with great joy.
text.KJV: And those that were in Galilee, and in Arbattis, with their wives and their children, and all that they had, took he away with him, and brought them into Judea with great joy.
text.NABRE: He took with him the Jews who were in Galilee and in Arbatta, with their wives and children and all that they had, and brought them to Judea with great rejoicing.
text.NRSV: Then he took the Jews of Galilee and Arbatta, with their wives and children, and all they possessed, and led them to Judea with great rejoicing.

text.DRB: And Judas Machabeus, and Jonathas his brother passed over Jordan, and went three days' journey through the desert.
text.KJV: Judas Maccabeus also and his brother Jonathan went over Jordan, and travelled three days’ journey in the wilderness,
text.NABRE: Judas Maccabeus and his brother Jonathan crossed the Jordan and marched for three days through the wilderness.
text.NRSV: Judas and Jonathan in Gilead Judas Maccabeus and his brother Jonathan crossed the Jordan and made three days’ journey into the wilderness.

text.DRB: And the Nabutheians met them, & received them peaceably, & told them all things, that had happened to their brethren in the country of Galaad,
text.KJV: Where they met with the Nabathites, who came unto them in a peaceable manner, and told them every thing that had happened to their brethren in the land of Galaad:
text.NABRE: There they met some Nabateans, who received them peaceably and told them all that had happened to their kindred in Gilead:
text.NRSV: They encountered the Nabateans, who met them peaceably and told them all that had happened to their kindred in Gilead:

text.DRB: and that many of them were comprehended in Barasa, and Bosor, and in Alimis, and in Casphor, & Mageth, & Carnaim, all these cities fenced, & great.
text.KJV: And how that many of them were shut up in Bosora, and Bosor, and Alema, Casphor, Maked, and Carnaim; all these cities are strong and great:
text.NABRE: “Many of them are shut up in Bozrah, in Bosor near Alema, in Chaspho, Maked, and Carnaim”—all of these are large, fortified cities—
text.NRSV: ‘Many of them have been shut up in Bozrah and Bosor, in Alema and Chaspho, Maked and Carnaim’—all these towns were strong and large—

text.DRB: Yea and in the rest of the cities of Galaad they are held captive, & on the morrow they appointed to remove the army near to these cities, and to take them, & to dispatch them in one day.
text.KJV: And that they were shut up in the rest of the cities of the country of Galaad, and that against to morrow they had appointed to bring their host against the forts, and to take them, and to destroy them all in one day.
text.NABRE: “and some are shut up in other cities of Gilead. Tomorrow their enemies plan to attack the strongholds and to seize and destroy all these people in one day.”
text.NRSV: ‘and some have been shut up in the other towns of Gilead; the enemy are getting ready to attack the strongholds tomorrow and capture and destroy all these people in a single day.’

text.DRB: And Judas turned & his army the way into Bosor suddenly, and took the city: and he slew every male in the edge of the sword, & took all their spoils, & burnt it with fire.
text.KJV: Hereupon Judas and his host turned suddenly by the way of the wilderness unto Bosora; and when he had won the city, he slew all the males with the edge of the sword, and took all their spoils, and burned the city with fire,
text.NABRE: Thereupon Judas suddenly changed direction with his army, marched across the wilderness to Bozrah, and captured the city. He put every male to the sword, took all their spoils, and set fire to the city.
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his army quickly turned back by the wilderness road to Bozrah; and he took the town, and killed every male by the edge of the sword; then he seized all its spoils and burned it with fire.

text.DRB: And they arose thence by night, & went even to the fortress.
text.KJV: From whence he removed by night, and went till he came to the fortress.
text.NABRE: He led his army from that place by night, and they marched toward the stronghold.
text.NRSV: He left the place at night, and they went all the way to the stronghold of Dathema.

text.DRB: And it came to pass early in the morning, when they had lifted up their eyes, & behold much people, whereof there was no number, carrying ladders, & engines to take the fortress, and they expugned them.
text.KJV: And betimes in the morning they looked up, and, behold, there was an innumerable people bearing ladders and other engines of war, to take the fortress: for they assaulted them.
text.NABRE: When morning came, they looked ahead and saw a countless multitude, with ladders and machines for capturing the stronghold, beginning to attack.
text.NRSV: At dawn they looked out and saw a large company, which could not be counted, carrying ladders and engines of war to capture the stronghold, and attacking the Jews within.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that the battle began, and the cry of the battle ascended to heaven as a trumpet, and a great cry out of the city:
text.KJV: When Judas therefore saw that the battle was begun, and that the cry of the city went up to heaven, with trumpets, and a great sound,
text.NABRE: When Judas perceived that the struggle had begun and that the noise of the battle was resounding to heaven with trumpet blasts and loud shouting,
text.NRSV: So Judas saw that the battle had begun and that the cry of the town went up to Heaven, with trumpets and loud shouts,

text.DRB: & he said to his host: Fight ye today for your brethren.
text.KJV: He said unto his host, Fight this day for your brethren.
text.NABRE: he said to the men of his army, “Fight for our kindred today.”
text.NRSV: and he said to the men of his forces, ‘Fight today for your kindred!’

text.DRB: And he came with three ranks behind them & they cried out with trumpets, & cried in prayer.
text.KJV: So he went forth behind them in three companies, who sounded their trumpets, and cried with prayer.
text.NABRE: He came up behind them with three columns blowing their trumpets and crying out in prayer.
text.NRSV: Then he came up behind them in three companies, who sounded their trumpets and cried aloud in prayer.

text.DRB: And the camp of Timothee understood that it was Machabeus, and they fled back from his presence: and they struck them with a great plague: and there fell of them in that day almost eight thousand men.
text.KJV: Then the host of Timotheus, knowing that it was Maccabeus, fled from him: wherefore he smote them with a great slaughter; so that there were killed of them that day about eight thousand men.
text.NABRE: When the army of Timothy realized that it was Maccabeus, they fled before him, and he inflicted on them a great defeat. About eight thousand of their men fell that day.
text.NRSV: And when the army of Timothy realized that it was Maccabeus, they fled before him, and he dealt them a heavy blow. As many as eight thousand of them fell that day.

text.DRB: And Judas turned aside into Maspha, and expugned, & took it, and he slew every male thereof, & he took the spoils thereof, & burnt it with fire.
text.KJV: This done, Judas turned aside to Maspha; and after he had assaulted it he took and slew all the males therein, and received the spoils thereof and and burnt it with fire.
text.NABRE: Then he turned toward Alema and attacked and captured it; he killed every male, took spoils, and burned it down.
text.NRSV: Next he turned aside to Maapha, and fought against it and took it; and he killed every male in it, plundered it, and burned it with fire.

text.DRB: From thence he marched, and took Casbon, and Mageth, and Bosor, & the rest of the cities of Galaad.
text.KJV: From thence went he, and took Casphon, Maged, Bosor, and the other cities of the country of Galaad.
text.NABRE: From there he moved on and took Chaspho, Maked, Bosor, and the other cities of Gilead.
text.NRSV: From there he marched on and took Chaspho, Maked, and Bosor, and the other towns of Gilead.

text.DRB: But after these words Timothee gathered another army, and camped against Raphon beyond the torrent.
text.KJV: After these things gathered Timotheus another host and encamped against Raphon beyond the brook.
text.NABRE: After these events Timothy assembled another army and camped opposite Raphon, on the other side of the wadi.
text.NRSV: After these things Timothy gathered another army and encamped opposite Raphon, on the other side of the stream.

text.DRB: And Judas send to view the army: and they reported unto him, saying: That all nations, that are round about us, are assembled against us, an army exceeding great:
text.KJV: So Judas sent men to espy the host, who brought him word, saying, All the heathen that be round about us are assembled unto them, even a very great host.
text.NABRE: Judas sent men to spy on the camp, and they reported to him: “All the Gentiles around us have rallied to him, making a very large force;
text.NRSV: Judas sent men to spy out the camp, and they reported to him, ‘All the Gentiles around us have gathered to him; it is a very large force.

text.DRB: and they have hired the Arabians to help them, & they have camped beyond the torrent, being ready to come unto thee into battle. And Judas went to meet them.
text.KJV: He hath also hired the Arabians to help them and they have pitched their tents beyond the brook, ready to come and fight against thee. Upon this Judas went to meet them.
text.NABRE: they have also hired Arabians to help them, and have camped beyond the wadi, ready to attack you.” So Judas went forward to meet them.
text.NRSV: They also have hired Arabs to help them, and they are encamped across the stream, ready to come and fight against you.’ And Judas went to meet them.

text.DRB: And Timothee said to the princes of his army: When Judas shall approach, & his army to the torrent of water: if he pass over unto us first, we shall not be able to sustain him: because prevailing he will prevail against us.
text.KJV: Then Timotheus said unto the captains of his host, When Judas and his host come near the brook, if he pass over first unto us, we shall not be able to withstand him; for he will mightily prevail against us:
text.NABRE: As Judas and his army were approaching the flowing wadi, Timothy said to the officers of his army: “If he crosses over to us first, we shall not be able to resist him; he will certainly defeat us.
text.NRSV: Now as Judas and his army drew near to the stream of water, Timothy said to the officers of his forces, ‘If he crosses over to us first, we will not be able to resist him, for he will surely defeat us.

text.DRB: But if he be afraid to pass over, and camp without the river, let us pass over to them, and we shall prevail against them.
text.KJV: But if he be afraid, and camp beyond the river, we shall go over unto him, and prevail against him.
text.NABRE: But if he is hesitant and camps on the other side of the river, we will cross over to him and defeat him.”
text.NRSV: But if he shows fear and camps on the other side of the river, we will cross over to him and defeat him.’

text.DRB: But as Judas approached to the torrent of water, he set the scribes of the people by the torrent, & commanded them, saying: Leave not a man: but let all come into the battle.
text.KJV: Now when Judas came near the brook, he caused the scribes of the people to remain by the brook: unto whom he gave commandment, saying, Suffer no man to remain in the camp, but let all come to the battle.
text.NABRE: But when Judas reached the flowing wadi, he stationed the officers of the people beside it and gave them this order: “Do not allow anyone to encamp; all must go into battle.”
text.NRSV: When Judas approached the stream of water, he stationed the officers of the army at the stream and gave them this command, ‘Permit no one to encamp, but make them all enter the battle.’

text.DRB: And he passed over to them first, & all the people after him, & all the heathen were discomfited at their presence, & they threw away their weapons, & fled to the temple, that was in Carnaim.
text.KJV: So he went first over unto them, and all the people after him: then all the heathen, being discomfited before him, cast away their weapons, and fled unto the temple that was at Carnaim.
text.NABRE: He was the first to cross to the attack, with all the people behind him, and all the Gentiles were crushed before them. They threw away their arms and fled to the temple enclosure at Carnaim.
text.NRSV: Then he crossed over against them first, and the whole army followed him. All the Gentiles were defeated before him, and they threw away their arms and fled into the sacred precincts at Carnaim.

text.DRB: And he took the city it-self, & the temple he burnt with fire, with all things that were therein: & Carnaim was subdued, & could not stand against the face of Judas.
text.KJV: But they took the city, and burned the temple with all that were therein. Thus was Carnaim subdued, neither could they stand any longer before Judas.
text.NABRE: But Judas’ troops captured the city and burnt the temple enclosure with all who were in it. So Carnaim was subdued, and Judas met with no more resistance.
text.NRSV: But he took the town and burned the sacred precincts with fire, together with all who were in them. Thus Carnaim was conquered; they could stand before Judas no longer.

text.DRB: And Judas gathered together all the Israelites, that were in the country of Galaad, from the least even to the greatest, and their wives, and children, and an army exceeding great, to come into the land of Juda.
text.KJV: Then Judas gathered together all the Israelites that were in the country of Galaad, from the least unto the greatest, even their wives, and their children, and their stuff, a very great host, to the end they might come into the land of Judea.
text.NABRE: Return to Jerusalem. Then Judas assembled all the Israelites, great and small, who were in Gilead, with their wives and children and their goods, a very large company, to go into the land of Judah.
text.NRSV: The Return to Jerusalem Then Judas gathered together all the Israelites in Gilead, the small and the great, with their wives and children and goods, a very large company, to go to the land of Judah.

text.DRB: And they came as far as Ephron: and this city was great, situate in the entrance, fenced exceedingly, and there was no means to decline from it on the right hand or on the left, but the way was through the midst.
text.KJV: Now when they came unto Ephron, (this was a great city in the way as they should go, very well fortified) they could not turn from it, either on the right hand or the left, but must needs pass through the midst of it.
text.NABRE: When they reached Ephron, a large and strongly fortified city along the way, they found it impossible to go around it on either the right or the left; they would have to march right through it.
text.NRSV: So they came to Ephron. This was a large and very strong town on the road, and they could not go around it to the right or to the left; they had to go through it.

text.DRB: And they that were in the city, shut in themselves, and stopped the gates with stones: & Judas sent to them in peaceable words,
text.KJV: Then they of the city shut them out, and stopped up the gates with stones.
text.NABRE: But the people in the city shut them out and blocked up the gates with stones.
text.NRSV: But the people of the town shut them out and blocked up the gates with stones.

text.DRB: saying: Let us pass through your land, and go into our country: and no man shall hurt you, only on foot we will pass. And they would not open unto them.
text.KJV: Whereupon Judas sent unto them in peaceable manner, saying, Let us pass through your land to go into our own country, and none shall do you any hurt; we will only pass through on foot: howbeit they would not open unto him.
text.NABRE: Then Judas sent them this peaceful message: “Let us cross your territory in order to reach our own; no one will harm you; we will only march through.” But they would not open to him.
text.NRSV: Judas sent them this friendly message, ‘Let us pass through your land to get to our land. No one will do you harm; we will simply pass by on foot.’ But they refused to open to him.

text.DRB: And Judas commanded proclamation to be made in the camp, that they should approach every man in the place, wherein he was.
