text.DRB: and that they took him alive, and appointed him that himself and they that should reign after him, should give a great tribute, and he should give hostages, and the thing appointed him.
text.KJV: And how they took him alive, and covenanted that he and such as reigned after him should pay a great tribute, and give hostages, and that which was agreed upon,
text.NABRE: They took him alive and obliged him and the kings who succeeded him to pay a heavy tribute, to give hostages and to cede
text.NRSV: they took him alive and decreed that he and those who would reign after him should pay a heavy tribute and give hostages and surrender some of their best provinces,

text.DRB: and the region of the Indes, and the Medes, and the Lydians of their best countries: and the same being taken of them, they gave to Eumenes the king.
text.KJV: And the country of India, and Media and Lydia and of the goodliest countries, which they took of him, and gave to king Eumenes:
text.NABRE: Lycia, Mysia, and Lydia from among their best provinces. The Romans took these from him and gave them to King Eumenes.
text.NRSV: the countries of India, Media, and Lydia. These they took from him and gave to King Eumenes.

text.DRB: And that they which were in Hellada, would have gone to dispatch them: and the word was known to them,
text.KJV: Moreover how the Grecians had determined to come and destroy them;
text.NABRE: When the Greeks planned to come and destroy them,
text.NRSV: The Greeks planned to come and destroy them,

text.DRB: and they sent unto them one captain, and they fought against them, and many of them were slain, and they led away their wives captive, and their children, and spoiled them, and possessed their land, and destroyed their walls, and brought them into bondage even until this day:
text.KJV: And that they, having knowledge thereof sent against them a certain captain, and fighting with them slew many of them, and carried away captives their wives and their children, and spoiled them, and took possession of their lands, and pulled down their strong holds, and brought them to be their servants unto this day:
text.NABRE: the Romans discovered it, and sent against the Greeks a single general who made war on them. Many were wounded and fell, and the Romans took their wives and children captive. They plundered them, took possession of their land, tore down their strongholds and reduced them to slavery even to this day.
text.NRSV: but this became known to them, and they sent a general against the Greeks and attacked them. Many of them were wounded and fell, and the Romans took captive their wives and children; they plundered them, conquered the land, tore down their strongholds, and enslaved them to this day.

text.DRB: and the residue of kingdoms, and isles that some time had resisted them, they spoiled, and brought under their power.
text.KJV: It was told him besides, how they destroyed and brought under their dominion all other kingdoms and isles that at any time resisted them;
text.NABRE: All the other kingdoms and islands that had ever opposed them they destroyed and enslaved; with their friends, however, and those who relied on them, they maintained friendship.
text.NRSV: The remaining kingdoms and islands, as many as ever opposed them, they destroyed and enslaved;

text.DRB: But with their friends, and those that rested in them, they kept amity, and obtained the kingdoms, that were next, and that were far off: that whosoever heard their name, feared them.
text.KJV: But with their friends and such as relied upon them they kept amity: and that they had conquered kingdoms both far and nigh, insomuch as all that heard of their name were afraid of them:
text.NABRE: They subjugated kings both near and far, and all who heard of their fame were afraid of them.
text.NRSV: but with their friends and those who rely on them they have kept friendship. They have subdued kings far and near, and as many as have heard of their fame have feared them.

text.DRB: But such as they would help to reign, those reigned: and whom they would, they deposed from the kingdom: and they were exalted exceedingly.
text.KJV: Also that, whom they would help to a kingdom, those reign; and whom again they would, they displace: finally, that they were greatly exalted:
text.NABRE: Those whom they wish to help and to make kings, they make kings; and those whom they wish, they depose; and they were greatly exalted.
text.NRSV: Those whom they wish to help and to make kings, they make kings, and those whom they wish they depose; and they have been greatly exalted.

text.DRB: And in all these none bare a crown, nor was clothed with purple, to be magnified therein.
text.KJV: Yet for all this none of them wore a crown or was clothed in purple, to be magnified thereby:
text.NABRE: Yet with all this, none of them put on a diadem or wore purple as a display of grandeur.
text.NRSV: Yet for all this not one of them has put on a crown or worn purple as a mark of pride,

text.DRB: And that they made themselves a court, and consulted daily three hundred and twenty, that sat in counsel always for the multitude, that they might do the things that were convenient:
text.KJV: Moreover how they had made for themselves a senate house, wherein three hundred and twenty men sat in council daily, consulting alway for the people, to the end they might be well ordered:
text.NABRE: But they made for themselves a senate chamber, and every day three hundred and twenty men took counsel, deliberating on all that concerned the people and their well-being.
text.NRSV: but they have built for themselves a senate chamber, and every day three hundred and twenty senators constantly deliberate concerning the people, to govern them well.

text.DRB: and they commit their government to one man every year to rule over all their land, and all they obey one, and there is no envy, nor emulation amongst them.
text.KJV: And that they committed their government to one man every year, who ruled over all their country, and that all were obedient to that one, and that there was neither envy nor emulation among them.
text.NABRE: They entrust their government to one man every year, to rule over their entire land, and they all obey that one, and there is no envy or jealousy among them.
text.NRSV: They trust one man each year to rule over them and to control all their land; they all heed the one man, and there is no envy or jealousy among them.

text.KJV: In consideration of these things, Judas chose Eupolemus the son of John, the son of Accos, and Jason the son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome, to make a league of amity and confederacy with them,
text.NABRE: Treaty with the Romans. So Judas chose Eupolemus, son of John, son of Accos, and Jason, son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance with them.
text.NRSV: An Alliance with Rome So Judas chose Eupolemus son of John son of Accos, and Jason son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance,
text.DRB: And Judas chose Eupolemus the son of John, the son of Jacob, and Jason the son of Eleazar, & he sent them to Rome to make amity and society with them:

text.KJV: And to intreat them that they would take the yoke from them; for they saw that the kingdom of the Grecians did oppress Israel with servitude.
text.NABRE: He did this to lift the yoke from Israel, for it was obvious that the kingdom of the Greeks was subjecting them to slavery.
text.NRSV: and to free themselves from the yoke; for they saw that the kingdom of the Greeks was enslaving Israel completely.
text.DRB: and that they should take from them the yoke of the Greeks, because they saw that they pressed the kingdom of Israel unto bondage.
And they went to Rome, a way exceeding great, and they entered into the court, and said:
text.KJV: They went therefore to Rome, which was a very great journey, and came into the senate, where they spake and said.
text.NABRE: After making a very long journey to Rome, the envoys entered the senate chamber and spoke as follows:
text.NRSV: They went to Rome, a very long journey; and they entered the senate chamber and spoke as follows:

text.DRB: Judas Machabeus, and his brethren, and the people of the Jews have sent us unto you to make society and peace with you, and to write us your fellows and friends.
text.KJV: Judas Maccabeus with his brethren, and the people of the Jews, have sent us unto you, to make a confederacy and peace with you, and that we might be registered your confederates and friends.
text.NABRE: “Judas, called Maccabeus, and his brothers, with the Jewish people, have sent us to you to establish alliance and peace with you, and to be enrolled among your allies and friends.”
text.NRSV: ‘Judas, who is also called Maccabeus, and his brothers and the people of the Jews have sent us to you to establish alliance and peace with you, so that we may be enrolled as your allies and friends.’

text.DRB: And the word was liked in their sight.
text.KJV: So that matter pleased the Romans well.
text.NABRE: The proposal pleased the Romans,
text.NRSV: The proposal pleased them,

text.KJV: And this is the copy of the epistle which the senate wrote back again in tables of brass, and sent to Jerusalem, that there they might have by them a memorial of peace and confederacy:
text.NABRE: and this is a copy of the reply they inscribed on bronze tablets and sent to Jerusalem, to remain there with the Jews as a record of peace and alliance:
text.NRSV: and this is a copy of the letter that they wrote in reply, on bronze tablets, and sent to Jerusalem to remain with them there as a memorial of peace and alliance:
text.DRB: And this is the rescript that they wrote again in tables of brass, and sent into Jerusalem, that it might be with them there a memorial of peace and society.
BE IT Well to The Romans, and to the nation of the Jews by sea, and by land for ever: and sword and enemy be far from them.
text.KJV: Good success be to the Romans, and to the people of the Jews, by sea and by land for ever: the sword also and enemy be far from them,
text.NABRE: “May it be well with the Romans and the Jewish nation at sea and on land forever; may sword and enemy be far from them.
text.NRSV: ‘May all go well with the Romans and with the nation of the Jews at sea and on land for ever, and may sword and enemy be far from them.

text.DRB: But and if war be toward the Romans first, or all their fellows in all their dominion:
text.KJV: If there come first any war upon the Romans or any of their confederates throughout all their dominion,
text.NABRE: But if war is first made on Rome, or any of its allies in any of their dominions,
text.NRSV: If war comes first to Rome or to any of their allies in all their dominion,

text.DRB: the nation of the Jews shall give aid, according as the time shall appoint, with full heart:
text.KJV: The people of the Jews shall help them, as the time shall be appointed, with all their heart:
text.NABRE: the Jewish nation will fight along with them wholeheartedly, as the occasion shall demand;
text.NRSV: the nation of the Jews shall act as their allies wholeheartedly, as the occasion may indicate to them.

text.DRB: and to them fighting, they shall not give nor allow wheat, armour, money, ships, as it hath pleased the Romans: & they shall keep their charge, taking nothing of them.
text.KJV: Neither shall they give any thing unto them that make war upon them, or aid them with victuals, weapons, money, or ships, as it hath seemed good unto the Romans; but they shall keep their covenants without taking any thing therefore.
text.NABRE: and to those who wage war they shall not give or provide grain, weapons, money, or ships, as seems best to Rome. They shall fulfill their obligations without receiving any recompense.
text.NRSV: To the enemy that makes war they shall not give or supply grain, arms, money, or ships, just as Rome has decided; and they shall keep their obligations without receiving any return.

text.DRB: In like manner also & if war shall happen first to the nation of the Jews, the Romans shall help with all their heart, according as the time shall permit them:
text.KJV: In the same manner also, if war come first upon the nation of the Jews, the Romans shall help them with all their heart, according as the time shall be appointed them:
text.NABRE: In the same way, if war is made first on the Jewish nation, the Romans will fight along with them willingly, as the occasion shall demand,
text.NRSV: In the same way, if war comes first to the nation of the Jews, the Romans shall willingly act as their allies, as the occasion may indicate to them.

text.DRB: & to them helping shall not be given wheat, armour, money, ships, as it hath pleased the Romans: and they shall keep their charge without guile.
text.KJV: Neither shall victuals be given to them that take part against them, or weapons, or money, or ships, as it hath seemed good to the Romans; but they shall keep their covenants, and that without deceit.
text.NABRE: and to those who attack them there shall not be given grain, weapons, money, or ships, as seems best to Rome. They shall fulfill their obligations without deception.
text.NRSV: And to their enemies there shall not be given grain, arms, money, or ships, just as Rome has decided; and they shall keep these obligations and do so without deceit.

text.DRB: According to these words did the Romans agree to the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: According to these articles did the Romans make a covenant with the people of the Jews.
text.NABRE: On these terms the Romans have made an agreement with the Jewish people.
text.NRSV: Thus on these terms the Romans make a treaty with the Jewish people.

text.DRB: And if after these words these or they will add to these, or take away any thing, they shall do it at their pleasure: and whatsoever they shall add, or take away, shall be ratified.
text.KJV: Howbeit if hereafter the one party or the other shall think to meet to add or diminish any thing, they may do it at their pleasures, and whatsoever they shall add or take away shall be ratified.
text.NABRE: But if both parties hereafter agree to add or take away anything, they shall do as they choose, and whatever they shall add or take away shall be valid.
text.NRSV: If after these terms are in effect both parties shall determine to add or delete anything, they shall do so at their discretion, and any addition or deletion that they may make shall be valid.

text.DRB: Yea and concerning the evils, that Demetrius the king hath done against them, we have written to him, saying: Why hast thou aggravated the yoke upon our friends and fellows the Jews?
text.KJV: And as touching the evils that Demetrius doeth to the Jews, we have written unto him, saying, Wherefore thou made thy yoke heavy upon our friends and confederates the Jews?
text.NABRE: “Moreover, concerning the wrongs that King Demetrius is doing to them, we have written to him thus: ‘Why have you made your yoke heavy upon our friends and allies the Jews?
text.NRSV: ‘Concerning the wrongs that King Demetrius is doing to them, we have written to him as follows, “Why have you made your yoke heavy on our friends and allies the Jews?

text.DRB: If therefore they come again unto us against thee, we will do judgement for them, and will fight with thee by sea and land.
text.KJV: If therefore they complain any more against thee, we will do them justice, and fight with thee by sea and by land.
text.NABRE: If they petition against you again, we will enforce justice and make war on you by sea and land.’”
text.NRSV: If now they appeal again for help against you, we will defend their rights and fight you on sea and on land.”’

text.KJV: Furthermore, when Demetrius heard the Nicanor and his host were slain in battle, he sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judea the second time, and with them the chief strength of his host:
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Death of Judas. When Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his army had fallen in battle, he again sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judah, along with the right wing of his army.
text.NRSV: Bacchides Returns to Judea 9
text.DRB: In the mean time as Demetrius heard that Nicanor was fallen and his army in the battle, he added to send Bacchides and Alcimus again into Jurie, and the right wing with them.
And they went the way, that leadeth into Galgal, and camped in Masaloth, which is in Arbellis: and they won it, and slew of men many souls.
text.KJV: Who went forth by the way that leadeth to Galgala, and pitched their tents before Masaloth, which is in Arbela, and after they had won it, they slew much people.
text.NABRE: They took the road to Galilee, and camping opposite the ascent at Arbela, they captured it and killed many people.
text.NRSV: They went by the road that leads to Gilgal and encamped against Mesaloth in Arbela, and they took it and killed many people.

text.DRB: In the first month of the year an hundreth and fifty two they brought the army near to Jerusalem:
text.KJV: Also the first month of the hundred fifty and second year they encamped before Jerusalem:
text.NABRE: In the first month of the one hundred and fifty-second year, they encamped against Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: In the first month of the one hundred and fifty-second year they encamped against Jerusalem;

text.DRB: and they arose, and went into Beræa twenty thousand men, and two thousand horsemen.
text.KJV: From whence they removed, and went to Berea, with twenty thousand footmen and two thousand horsemen.
text.NABRE: Then they set out for Berea with twenty thousand men and two thousand cavalry.
text.NRSV: then they marched off and went to Berea with twenty thousand foot-soldiers and two thousand cavalry.

text.DRB: And Judas had camped in Laisa, and three thousand chosen men with him:
text.KJV: Now Judas had pitched his tents at Eleasa, and three thousand chosen men with him:
text.NABRE: Judas, with three thousand picked men, had camped at Elasa.
text.NRSV: Now Judas was encamped in Elasa, and with him were three thousand picked men.

text.DRB: and they saw the multitude of the army that they were many, and they feared exceedingly: and many withdrew themselves out of the camp, and there remained of them but eight hundred men.
text.KJV: Who seeing the multitude of the other army to he so great were sore afraid; whereupon many conveyed themselves out of the host, insomuch as abode of them no more but eight hundred men.
text.NABRE: When they saw the great number of the troops, they were very much afraid, and many slipped away from the camp, until only eight hundred of them remained.
text.NRSV: When they saw the huge number of the enemy forces, they were greatly frightened, and many slipped away from the camp, until no more than eight hundred of them were left.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that his army shrunk away, and the battle pressed upon him, and his heart was broken: because he had not time to gather them together, and he was discouraged.
text.KJV: When Judas therefore saw that his host slipt away, and that the battle pressed upon him, he was sore troubled in mind, and much distressed, for that he had no time to gather them together.
text.NABRE: When Judas saw that his army was melting away just as the battle was imminent, he was brokenhearted, because he had no time to gather them together.
text.NRSV: When Judas saw that his army had slipped away and the battle was imminent, he was crushed in spirit, for he had no time to assemble them.

text.DRB: And he said to them that were remaining: Let us rise, and go to our adversaries, if we may be able to fight against them.
text.KJV: Nevertheless unto them that remained he said, Let us arise and go up against our enemies, if peradventure we may be able to fight with them.
text.NABRE: In spite of his discouragement he said to those who remained: “Let us go forward to meet our enemies; perhaps we can put up a good fight against them.”
text.NRSV: He became faint, but he said to those who were left, ‘Let us get up and go against our enemies. We may have the strength to fight them.’

text.DRB: And they dissuaded him saying: We are not able, but let us save our lives now, and return to our brethren, and then we will fight against them: and we are few.
text.KJV: But they dehorted him, saying, We shall never be able: let us now rather save our lives, and hereafter we will return with our brethren, and fight against them: for we are but few.
text.NABRE: They tried to dissuade him, saying: “We certainly cannot. Let us save our own lives now, and come back with our kindred, and then fight against them. Now we are too few.”
text.NRSV: But they tried to dissuade him, saying, ‘We do not have the strength. Let us rather save our own lives now, and let us come back with our kindred and fight them; we are too few.’

text.DRB: And Judas said: God forbid we should do this thing, to fly from them: & if our time be near, let us die manfully for our brethren, and let us not stain our glory.
text.KJV: Then Judas said, God forbid that I should do this thing, and flee away from them: if our time be come, let us die manfully for our brethren, and let us not stain our honour.
text.NABRE: But Judas said: “Far be it from me to do such a thing as to flee from them! If our time has come, let us die bravely for our kindred and not leave a stain upon our honor!”
text.NRSV: But Judas said, ‘Far be it from us to do such a thing as to flee from them. If our time has come, let us die bravely for our kindred, and leave no cause to question our honour.’

text.KJV: With that the host of Bacchides removed out of their tents, and stood over against them, their horsemen being divided into two troops, and their slingers and archers going before the host and they that marched in the foreward were all mighty men.
text.NABRE: Then the army of Bacchides moved out of camp and took its position for combat. The cavalry were divided into two squadrons, and the slingers and the archers came on ahead of the army, and in the front line were all the best warriors. Bacchides was on the right wing.
text.NRSV: The Last Battle of Judas Then the army of Bacchides marched out from the camp and took its stand for the encounter. The cavalry was divided into two companies, and the slingers and the archers went ahead of the army, as did all the chief warriors.
text.DRB: And the army moved out of the camp, and they stood to meet them: and the horsemen were divided into two parts, and the slingers, and the archers went before the army, & of the forward all mighty.
And Bacchides was in the right wing, and the legion approached on two sides, & they sounded with trumpets:
text.KJV: As for Bacchides, he was in the right wing: so the host drew near on the two parts, and sounded their trumpets.
text.NABRE: Flanked by the two squadrons, the phalanx attacked as they blew their trumpets. Those who were on Judas’ side also blew their trumpets.
text.NRSV: Bacchides was on the right wing. Flanked by the two companies, the phalanx advanced to the sound of the trumpets; and the men with Judas also blew their trumpets.

text.DRB: and they also cried out that were on Judas' side, even they also, & the earth was moved at the voice of the armies: and the battle was fought from morning even until the evening.
text.KJV: They also of Judas’ side, even they sounded their trumpets also, so that the earth shook at the noise of the armies, and the battle continued from morning till night.
text.NABRE: The earth shook with the noise of the armies, and the battle raged from morning until evening.
text.NRSV: The earth was shaken by the noise of the armies, and the battle raged from morning until evening.

text.DRB: And Judas saw that the stronger part of the army was on the right hand, and all the stout of heart came together with him:
text.KJV: Now when Judas perceived that Bacchides and the strength of his army were on the right side, he took with him all the hardy men,
text.NABRE: When Judas saw that Bacchides was on the right, with the main force of his army, all the most stouthearted rallied to him,
text.NRSV: Judas saw that Bacchides and the strength of his army were on the right; then all the stout-hearted men went with him,

text.DRB: and the right side was discomfited of them, & he pursued them even to the mount of Azotus.
text.KJV: Who discomfited the right wing, and pursued them unto the mount Azotus.
text.NABRE: and the right wing was crushed; Judas pursued them as far as the mountain slopes.
text.NRSV: and they crushed the right wing, and he pursued them as far as Mount Azotus.

text.DRB: And they that were in the left wing, saw that the ring wing was discomfited, and they followed after Judas, and them that were with him at the back:
text.KJV: But when they of the left wing saw that they of the right wing were discomfited, they followed upon Judas and those that were with him hard at the heels from behind:
text.NABRE: But when those on the left wing saw that the right wing was crushed, they closed in behind Judas and those with him.
text.NRSV: When those on the left wing saw that the right wing was crushed, they turned and followed close behind Judas and his men.

text.DRB: and the battle grew sore, and there fell many wounded of these and of them.
text.KJV: Whereupon there was a sore battle, insomuch as many were slain on both parts.
text.NABRE: The battle became intense, and many on both sides fell wounded.
text.NRSV: The battle became desperate, and many on both sides were wounded and fell.

text.KJV: Judas also was killed, and the remnant fled.
text.NABRE: Then Judas fell, and the rest fled.
text.NRSV: Judas also fell, and the rest fled.
text.DRB: And Judas was slain, and the rest fled.

text.KJV: Then Jonathan and Simon took Judas their brother, and buried him in the sepulchre of his fathers in Modin.
text.NABRE: Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein,
text.DRB: And Jonathas and Simon took Judas their brother, and buried him in the sepulchre of their fathers in the city Modin.

text.KJV: Moreover they bewailed him, and all Israel made great lamentation for him, and mourned many days, saying,
text.NABRE: All Israel wept for him with great lamentation. They mourned for him many days, and they said,
text.NRSV: and wept for him. All Israel made great lamentation for him; they mourned for many days and said,
text.DRB: And all the people of Israel lamented him with great lamentation, and they mourned many days,
and said: How is the mighty fallen, that saved the people of Israel!
text.KJV: How is the valiant man fallen, that delivered Israel!
text.NABRE: “How the mighty one has fallen, the savior of Israel!”
text.NRSV: ‘How is the mighty fallen, the saviour of Israel!’

text.DRB: And the rest of the words of Judas' battles, and of the valiant acts that he did, and of his greatness are not described: for they were exceeding many.
text.KJV: As for the other things concerning Judas and his wars, and the noble acts which he did, and his greatness, they are not written: for they were very many.
text.NABRE: The other acts of Judas, his battles, the brave deeds he performed, and his greatness have not been recorded; but they were very many.
text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Judas, and his wars and the brave deeds that he did, and his greatness, have not been recorded, but they were very many.

text.DRB: And it came to pass: after the death of Judas, there came forth the wicked men in all the coasts of Israel, & there arose all that wrought iniquity.
text.KJV: Now after the death of Judas the wicked began to put forth their heads in all the coasts of Israel, and there arose up all such as wrought iniquity.
text.NABRE: III. Leadership of Jonathan Jonathan Succeeds Judas. After the death of Judas, the lawless raised their heads in every part of Israel, and all kinds of evildoers appeared.
text.NRSV: Jonathan Succeeds Judas After the death of Judas, the renegades emerged in all parts of Israel; all the wrongdoers reappeared.

text.DRB: In those days was made an exceeding great famine, and all their country with themselves yielded to Bacchides.
text.KJV: In those days also was there a very great famine, by reason whereof the country revolted, and went with them.
text.NABRE: In those days there was a very great famine, and the country deserted to them.
text.NRSV: In those days a very great famine occurred, and the country went over to their side.

text.DRB: And Bacchides chose the impious men, & appointed them lords of the country:
text.KJV: Then Bacchides chose the wicked men, and made them lords of the country.
text.NABRE: Bacchides chose renegades and made them masters of the country.
text.NRSV: Bacchides chose the godless and put them in charge of the country.

text.DRB: and they sought out, & searched for the friends of Judas, & brought them to Bacchides, and he took revenge on them, and scorned them.
text.KJV: And they made enquiry and search for Judas’ friends, and brought them unto Bacchides, who took vengeance of them, and used them despitefully.
text.NABRE: These sought out and hunted down the friends of Judas and brought them to Bacchides, who punished and derided them.
text.NRSV: They made inquiry and searched for the friends of Judas, and brought them to Bacchides, who took vengeance on them and made sport of them.

text.DRB: And there was made great tribulation in Israel, such as was not since the day, that there was no Prophet seen in Israel.
text.KJV: So was there a great affliction in Israel, the like whereof was not since the time that a prophet was not seen among them.
text.NABRE: There was great tribulation in Israel, the like of which had not been since the time prophets ceased to appear among them.
text.NRSV: So there was great distress in Israel, such as had not been since the time that prophets ceased to appear among them.

text.KJV: For this cause all Judas’ friends came together, and said unto Jonathan,
text.NABRE: Then all the friends of Judas came together and said to Jonathan:
text.NRSV: Then all the friends of Judas assembled and said to Jonathan,
text.DRB: And all the friends of Judas gathered, & said to Jonathas:
Since thy brother Judas died, there is not a man like unto him, to go forth against the enemies, Bacchides, & them that are the enemies of our nation.
text.KJV: Since thy brother Judas died, we have no man like him to go forth against our enemies, and Bacchides, and against them of our nation that are adversaries to us.
text.NABRE: “Ever since your brother Judas died, there has been no one like him to lead us against our enemies, both Bacchides and those of our nation who are hostile to us.
text.NRSV: ‘Since the death of your brother Judas there has been no one like him to go against our enemies and Bacchides, and to deal with those of our nation who hate us.

text.DRB: Now therefore thee have we chosen this day to be for him our prince, and captain to wage our battle.
text.KJV: Now therefore we have chosen thee this day to be our prince and captain in his stead, that thou mayest fight our battles.
text.NABRE: Now therefore we have chosen you today to be our ruler and leader in his place, to fight our battle.”
text.NRSV: Now therefore we have chosen you today to take his place as our ruler and leader, to fight our battle.’

text.KJV: Upon this Jonathan took the governance upon him at that time, and rose up instead of his brother Judas.
text.NABRE: From that moment Jonathan accepted the leadership, and took the place of Judas his brother.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan accepted the leadership at that time in place of his brother Judas.
text.DRB: And Jonathas took upon him at that time the princedom, & arose in the place of Judas his brother.
And Bacchides understood it, and sought to kill him.
text.KJV: But when Bacchides gat knowledge thereof, he sought for to slay him
text.NABRE: Bacchides Pursues Jonathan. When Bacchides learned of it, he sought to kill him.
text.NRSV: The Campaigns of Jonathan When Bacchides learned of this, he tried to kill him.

text.DRB: And Jonathas understood it, and Simon his brother, and all that were with them: and they fled into the desert of Thecua, and they pitched by the water of the lake Asphar.
text.KJV: Then Jonathan, and Simon his brother, and all that were with him, perceiving that, fled into the wilderness of Thecoe, and pitched their tents by the water of the pool Asphar.
text.NABRE: But Jonathan and his brother Simon and all who were with him discovered this, and they fled to the wilderness of Tekoa and camped by the waters of the pool of Asphar.
text.NRSV: But Jonathan and his brother Simon and all who were with him heard of it, and they fled into the wilderness of Tekoa and camped by the water of the pool of Asphar.

text.DRB: And Bacchides understood it, and in the day of the Sabbaths came himself, and all his army over Jordan.
text.KJV: Which when Bacchides understood, he came near to Jordan with all his host upon the sabbath day.
text.NRSV: Bacchides found this out on the sabbath day, and he with all his army crossed the Jordan.

text.DRB: And Jonathas sent his brother captain of the people, to desire the Nabutheians his friends, that they would lend him their provision, which was copious.
text.KJV: Now Jonathan had sent his brother John, a captain of the people, to pray his friends the Nabathites, that they might leave with them their carriage, which was much.
text.NABRE: Jonathan sent his brother as leader of the convoy to implore his friends, the Nabateans, to let them deposit with them their great quantity of baggage.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan sent his brother as leader of the multitude and begged the Nabateans, who were his friends, for permission to store with them the great amount of baggage that they had.

text.DRB: And the children of Jambri issued forth of Madaba, and took John, & all things that he had, and went away having them.
text.KJV: But the children of Jambri came out of Medaba, and took John, and all that he had, and went their way with it.
text.NABRE: But the tribe of Jambri from Medaba made a raid and seized and carried off John and everything he had.
text.NRSV: But the family of Jambri from Medeba came out and seized John and all that he had, and left with it.

text.DRB: After these words, it was told Jonathas and Simon his brother, that the children of Jambri make a great marriage, & bring the bride out of Madaba, the daughter of one of the great princes of Chanaan with great pomp.
text.KJV: After this came word to Jonathan and Simon his brother, that the children of Jambri made a great marriage, and were bringing the bride from Nadabatha with a great train, as being the daughter of one of the great princes of Chanaan.
text.NABRE: After this, word was brought to Jonathan and his brother Simon: “The tribe of Jambri are celebrating a great wedding, and with a large escort they are bringing the bride, the daughter of one of the great princes of Canaan, from Nadabath.”
text.NRSV: After these things it was reported to Jonathan and his brother Simon, ‘The family of Jambri are celebrating a great wedding, and are conducting the bride, a daughter of one of the great nobles of Canaan, from Nadabath with a large escort.’

text.DRB: And they remembered the blood of John their brother: and they went up, and hid themselves under the covert of the mountain.
text.KJV: Therefore they remembered John their brother, and went up, and hid themselves under the covert of the mountain:
text.NABRE: Remembering the blood of John their brother, they went up and hid themselves under cover of the mountain.
text.NRSV: Remembering how their brother John had been killed, they went up and hid under cover of the mountain.

text.DRB: And they lifted up their eyes, and saw: and lo a tumult, a great preparation: and the bridegroom came forth, and his friends, and his brethren to meet them with timbrels, and musical instruments, and many weapons.
text.KJV: Where they lifted up their eyes, and looked, and, behold, there was much ado and great carriage: and the bridegroom came forth, and his friends and brethren, to meet them with drums, and instruments of musick, and many weapons.
text.NABRE: As they watched there appeared a noisy throng with much baggage; then the bridegroom and his friends and kinsmen had come out to meet them with tambourines and musicians with their instruments.
text.NRSV: They looked out and saw a tumultuous procession with a great amount of baggage; and the bridegroom came out with his friends and his brothers to meet them with tambourines and musicians and many weapons.

text.DRB: And they rose upon them out of the embushment, and slew them, and there fell many wounded, & the rest fled into the mountains, and they took all the spoils of them:
text.KJV: Then Jonathan and they that were with him rose up against them from the place where they lay in ambush, and made a slaughter of them in such sort, as many fell down dead, and the remnant fled into the mountain, and they took all their spoils.
text.NABRE: Jonathan and his party rose up against them from their ambush and killed them. Many fell wounded; the rest fled toward the mountain; all their spoils were taken.
text.NRSV: Then they rushed on them from the ambush and began killing them. Many were wounded and fell, and the rest fled to the mountain; and the Jews took all their goods.

text.DRB: and the marriage was turned into mourning, and the voice of their musical instruments into lamentation.
text.KJV: Thus was the marriage turned into mourning, and the noise of their melody into lamentation.
text.NABRE: Thus the wedding was turned into mourning, and the sound of their music into lamentation.
text.NRSV: So the wedding was turned into mourning and the voice of their musicians into a funeral dirge.

text.DRB: And they took revenge of the blood of their brother: and they returned to the bank of Jordan.
text.KJV: So when they had avenged fully the blood of their brother, they turned again to the marsh of Jordan.
text.NABRE: Having taken their revenge for the blood of their brother, they returned to the marshes of the Jordan.
text.NRSV: After they had fully avenged the blood of their brother, they returned to the marshes of the Jordan.

text.DRB: And Bacchides heard it, and he came on the day of the Sabbaths even to the brink of Jordan with a great power:
text.KJV: Now when Bacchides heard hereof, he came on the sabbath day unto the banks of Jordan with a great power.
text.NABRE: When Bacchides heard of it, he came on the sabbath to the banks of the Jordan with a large force.
text.NRSV: When Bacchides heard of this, he came with a large force on the sabbath day to the banks of the Jordan.

text.DRB: And Jonathas said to his company: Let us arise, and fight against our enemies: for it is not today as yesterday, and the day before.
text.KJV: Then Jonathan said to his company, Let us go up now and fight for our lives, for it standeth not with us to day, as in time past:
text.NABRE: Then Jonathan said to his companions, “Let us rise up now and fight for our lives, for today is not like yesterday and the day before.
text.NRSV: And Jonathan said to those with him, ‘Let us get up now and fight for our lives, for today things are not as they were before.

text.DRB: For lo battle directly against us, and the water of Jordan on this side and on that side, and banks, and marishes, & forests: and there is no place to turn aside.
text.KJV: For, behold, the battle is before us and behind us, and the water of Jordan on this side and that side, the marsh likewise and wood, neither is there place for us to turn aside.
text.NABRE: The battle is before us, behind us are the waters of the Jordan, on either side of us, marsh and thickets; there is no way of escape.
text.NRSV: For look! the battle is in front of us and behind us; the water of the Jordan is on this side and on that, with marsh and thicket; there is no place to turn.

text.DRB: Now therefore cry ye unto heaven, that you may be delivered out of the hand of your enemies. And battle was joined.
text.KJV: Wherefore cry ye now unto heaven, that ye may be delivered from the hand of your enemies.
text.NABRE: Cry out now to Heaven so that you may be delivered from the hand of our enemies.”
text.NRSV: Cry out now to Heaven that you may be delivered from the hands of our enemies.’

text.DRB: And Jonathas stretched forth his hand to strike Bacchides, & he turned aside from him backwards.
text.KJV: With that they joined battle, and Jonathan stretched forth his hand to smite Bacchides, but he turned back from him.
text.NABRE: When they joined battle, Jonathan raised his hand to strike Bacchides, but Bacchides backed away from him.
text.NRSV: So the battle began, and Jonathan stretched out his hand to strike Bacchides, but he eluded him and went to the rear.

text.DRB: And Jonathas lept aside, and they that were with him into Jordan, & they swam over Jordan to them:
text.KJV: Then Jonathan and they that were with him leapt into Jordan, and swam over unto the other bank: howbeit the other passed not over Jordan unto them.
text.NABRE: Jonathan and those with him jumped into the Jordan and swam across to the other side, but the enemy did not pursue them across the Jordan.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan and the men with him leapt into the Jordan and swam across to the other side, and the enemy did not cross the Jordan to attack them.

text.DRB: and there fell of Bacchides' part that day a thousand men: and they returned into Jerusalem,
text.KJV: So there were slain of Bacchides’ side that day about a thousand men.
text.NABRE: About a thousand men on Bacchides’ side fell that day.
text.NRSV: And about one thousand of Bacchides’ men fell that day.

text.DRB: and built fenced cities in Jurie, the fortress that was in Jericho, and in Ammaum, and in Bethoron, and in Bethel, and Thamnata, and Phara, and Thopo with high walls, and gates, & locks.
text.KJV: Afterward returned Bacchides to Jerusalem and repaired the strong cites in Judea; the fort in Jericho, and Emmaus, and Bethhoron, and Bethel, and Thamnatha, Pharathoni, and Taphon, these did he strengthen with high walls, with gates and with bars.
text.NABRE: On returning to Jerusalem, Bacchides built strongholds in Judea: the Jericho fortress, as well as Emmaus, Beth-horon, Bethel, Timnath, Pharathon, and Tephon, with high walls and gates and bars.
text.NRSV: Bacchides Builds Fortifications Then Bacchides returned to Jerusalem and built strong cities in Judea: the fortress in Jericho, and Emmaus, and Beth-horon, and Bethel, and Timnath, and Pharathon, and Tephon, with high walls and gates and bars.

text.DRB: And he placed a garrison in them, that they might exercise enmities against Israel:
text.KJV: And in them he set a garrison, that they might work malice upon Israel.
text.NABRE: In each he put a garrison to harass Israel.
text.NRSV: And he placed garrisons in them to harass Israel.

text.DRB: and he fenced the city Bethsura, and Gazara, and the castle, and set garrisons in them, & provision of victuals:
text.KJV: He fortified also the city Bethsura, and Gazera, and the tower, and put forces in them, and provision of victuals.
text.NABRE: He fortified the city of Beth-zur, Gazara and the citadel, and put troops in them and stores of provisions.
text.NRSV: He also fortified the town of Beth-zur, and Gazara, and the citadel, and in them he put troops and stores of food.

text.DRB: & he took the sons of the princes of the country for hostages, and put them in the castle in Jerusalem into ward.
text.KJV: Besides, he took the chief men’s sons in the country for hostages, and put them into the tower at Jerusalem to be kept.
text.NABRE: He took as hostages the sons of the leading people of the country and put them in custody in the citadel at Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: And he took the sons of the leading men of the land as hostages and put them under guard in the citadel at Jerusalem.

text.KJV: Moreover in the hundred fifty and third year, in the second month, Alcimus commanded that the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary should be pulled down; he pulled down also the works of the prophets
text.NABRE: In the one hundred and fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus ordered the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary to be torn down, thus destroying the work of the prophets. But he only began to tear it down.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred and fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus gave orders to tear down the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary. He tore down the work of the prophets!
text.DRB: And in the year an hundred fifty three, the second month, Alcimus commanded the walls of the inner holy house to be destroyed, and the works of the Prophets to be destroyed: & he began to destroy.

In that time was Alcimus strucken: and his works were stayed, and his mouth was stopped, and he was dissolved with the palsy, neither could he speak a word any more, and give commandment touching his house.
text.KJV: And as he began to pull down, even at that time was Alcimus plagued, and his enterprizes hindered: for his mouth was stopped, and he was taken with a palsy, so that he could no more speak any thing, nor give order concerning his house.
text.NABRE: Just at that time Alcimus was stricken, and his work was interrupted; his mouth was closed and he was paralyzed, so that he could no longer utter a word or give orders concerning his household.
text.NRSV: But he only began to tear it down, for at that time Alcimus was stricken and his work was hindered; his mouth was stopped and he was paralysed, so that he could no longer say a word or give commands concerning his house.

text.KJV: So Alcimus died at that time with great torment.
text.NABRE: Alcimus died in great agony at that time.
text.NRSV: And Alcimus died at that time in great agony.
text.DRB: And Alcimus died at that time with great torment.
And Bacchides saw that Alcimus was dead: and he returned to the king, & the land was quiet for two years.
text.KJV: Now when Bacchides saw that Alcimus was dead, he returned to the king: whereupon the land of Judea was in rest two years.
text.NABRE: Seeing that Alcimus was dead, Bacchides returned to the king, and the land of Judah was at rest for two years.
text.NRSV: When Bacchides saw that Alcimus was dead, he returned to the king, and the land of Judah had rest for two years.

text.DRB: And all the wicked thought saying: Behold Jonathas, and they that are with him, dwell in silence securely: now therefore let us bring Bacchides, and he shall take them all in one night.
text.KJV: Then all the ungodly men held a council, saying, Behold, Jonathan and his company are at ease, and dwell without care: now therefore we will bring Bacchides hither, who shall take them all in one night.
text.NABRE: Then all the lawless took counsel and said: “Jonathan and those with him are living in peace and security. Now then, let us have Bacchides return, and he will capture all of them in a single night.”
text.NRSV: The End of the War Then all the lawless plotted and said, ‘See! Jonathan and his men are living in quiet and confidence. So now let us bring Bacchides back, and he will capture them all in one night.’

text.DRB: And they went, and gave him counsel.
text.KJV: So they went and consulted with him.
text.NABRE: So they went and took counsel with him.
text.NRSV: And they went and consulted with him.

text.DRB: And he arose to come with a great army: and he sent secretly epistles to his fellows that were in Jurie, to take Jonathas, and them that were with him: but they could not, because their counsel was known to them.
text.KJV: Then removed he, and came with a great host, and sent letters privily to his adherents in Judea, that they should take Jonathan and those that were with him: howbeit they could not, because their counsel was known unto them.
text.NABRE: When Bacchides was setting out with a large force, he sent letters secretly to all his allies in Judea, telling them to seize Jonathan and his companions. They were not able to do this, however, because their plan became known.
text.NRSV: He started to come with a large force, and secretly sent letters to all his allies in Judea, telling them to seize Jonathan and his men; but they were unable to do it, because their plan became known.

text.DRB: And he apprehended of the men of the country, that were the principal of the mischief, fifty men, and he slew them.
text.KJV: Wherefore they took of the men of the country, that were authors of that mischief, about fifty persons, and slew them.
text.NABRE: In fact, Jonathan’s men seized about fifty of the men of the country who were leaders in the conspiracy and put them to death.
text.NRSV: And Jonathan’s men seized about fifty of the men of the country who were leaders in this treachery, and killed them.

text.DRB: And Jonathas retired aside, and Simon, and they that were with him into Bethbessen, which is in the desert: and he built up the ruins thereof, and they fortified it.
text.KJV: Afterward Jonathan, and Simon, and they that were with him, got them away to Bethbasi, which is in the wilderness, and they repaired the decays thereof, and made it strong.
text.NABRE: Then Jonathan and those with him, along with Simon, withdrew to Bethbasi in the wilderness; he rebuilt its ruins and fortified it.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan with his men, and Simon, withdrew to Bethbasi in the wilderness; he rebuilt the parts of it that had been demolished, and they fortified it.

text.DRB: And Bacchides understood it, and gathered together all his multitude, and sent word to them that were of Jurie.
text.KJV: Which thing when Bacchides knew, he gathered together all his host, and sent word to them that were of Judea.
text.NABRE: When Bacchides learned of this, he gathered together his whole force and sent word to those who were in Judea.
text.NRSV: When Bacchides learned of this, he assembled all his forces, and sent orders to the men of Judea.

text.DRB: And he came, and camped above Bethbessen, and assaulted it many days, and made engines.
text.KJV: Then went he and laid siege against Bethbasi; and they fought against it a long season and made engines of war.
text.NABRE: He came and camped before Bethbasi, and constructing siege engines, he fought against it for many days.
text.NRSV: Then he came and encamped against Bethbasi; he fought against it for many days and made machines of war.

text.DRB: And Jonathas left Simon his brother in the city, and went forth into the country, & came with a number,
text.KJV: But Jonathan left his brother Simon in the city, and went forth himself into the country, and with a certain number went he forth.
text.NABRE: Leaving his brother Simon in the city, Jonathan, accompanied by a small group of men, went out into the countryside.
text.NRSV: But Jonathan left his brother Simon in the town, while he went out into the country; and he went with only a few men.

text.DRB: and struck Odares, and his brethren, and the children of Phaseron in their tabernacles, and he began to slay, and to grow in forces.
text.KJV: And he smote Odonarkes and his brethren, and the children of Phasiron in their tent.
text.NABRE: He struck down Odomera and his kindred and the tribe of Phasiron in their encampment; these men had begun to attack and they were going up with their forces.
text.NRSV: He struck down Odomera and his kindred and the people of Phasiron in their tents.

text.DRB: But Simon and they that were with him, went out of the city, and burnt the engines,
text.KJV: And when he began to smite them, and came up with his forces, Simon and his company went out of the city, and burned up the engines of war,
text.NABRE: Simon and those with him then sallied forth from the city and set fire to the siege engines.
text.NRSV: Then he began to attack and went into battle with his forces; and Simon and his men sallied out from the town and set fire to the machines of war.

text.DRB: and they fought against Bacchides, & he was discomfited by them: and they afflicted him exceedingly, because his counsel, and his conflict was in vain.
text.KJV: And fought against Bacchides, who was discomfited by them, and they afflicted him sore: for his counsel and travail was in vain.
text.NABRE: They fought against Bacchides, and he was crushed. They caused him great distress, because the enterprise he had planned was in vain.
text.NRSV: They fought with Bacchides, and he was crushed by them. They pressed him very hard, for his plan and his expedition had been in vain.

text.DRB: And being angry against the wicked men, that had given him counsel to come into their country, he slew many of them: but himself thought with the rest to depart into their country.
text.KJV: Wherefore he was very wroth at the wicked men that gave him counsel to come into the country, inasmuch as he slew many of them, and purposed to return into his own country.
text.NABRE: He was enraged with the lawless men who had advised him to invade the province. He killed many of them and resolved to return to his own country.
text.NRSV: So he was very angry at the renegades who had counselled him to come into the country, and he killed many of them. Then he decided to go back to his own land.

text.DRB: And Jonathas understood it, and he sent unto him legates to make peace with him, and to render unto him the captives.
text.KJV: Whereof when Jonathan had knowledge, he sent ambassadors unto him, to the end he should make peace with him, and deliver them the prisoners.
text.NABRE: Jonathan learned of this and sent ambassadors to agree on peace with him and to obtain the release of the prisoners.
text.NRSV: When Jonathan learned of this, he sent ambassadors to him to make peace with him and obtain release of the captives.

text.DRB: And he took it gladly, and did according to his words, and sware that he would do him no harm all the days of his life.
text.KJV: Which thing he accepted, and did according to his demands, and sware unto him that he would never do him harm all the days of his life.
text.NABRE: He agreed to do as Jonathan asked. He swore an oath to him that he would never try to do him any harm for the rest of his life;
text.NRSV: He agreed, and did as he said; and he swore to Jonathan that he would not try to harm him as long as he lived.

text.DRB: And he rendered unto him the captives, which he before had taken for a prey, out of the land of Juda: & returning he departed into his own country, and he added no more to come into their coasts.
text.KJV: When therefore he had restored unto him the prisoners that he had taken aforetime out of the land of Judea, he returned and went his way into his own land, neither came he any more into their borders.
text.NABRE: and he released to him the prisoners he had previously taken from the land of Judah. Thereupon he returned to his own land and never came into their territory again.
text.NRSV: He restored to him the captives whom he had taken previously from the land of Judah; then he turned and went back to his own land, and did not come again into their territory.

text.DRB: And the sword ceased out of Israel: and Jonathas dwelt in Machmas, and Jonathas began there to judge the people, and he destroyed the impious out of Israel.
text.KJV: Thus the sword ceased from Israel: but Jonathan dwelt at Machmas, and began to govern the people; and he destroyed the ungodly men out of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then the sword ceased from Israel. Jonathan settled in Michmash; he began to judge the people and he eliminated the renegades from Israel.
text.NRSV: Thus the sword ceased from Israel. Jonathan settled in Michmash and began to judge the people; and he destroyed the godless out of Israel.

text.KJV: In the hundred and sixtieth year Alexander, the son of Antiochus surnamed Epiphanes, went up and took Ptolemais: for the people had received him, by means whereof he reigned there,
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Jonathan Becomes High Priest. In the one hundred and sixtieth year, Alexander Epiphanes, son of Antiochus, came up and took Ptolemais. They accepted him as king and he began to reign there.
text.NRSV: Revolt of Alexander Epiphanes 10
text.DRB: And in the year an hundreth threescore came up Alexander the son of Antiochus, that was surnamed Noble: and he took Ptolemais, and they received him, and he reigned there.
And Demetrius the king heard it, and he gathered together an exceeding great army, and went forth to meet him into battle.
text.KJV: Now when king Demetrius heard thereof, he gathered together an exceeding great host, and went forth against him to fight.
text.NABRE: When King Demetrius heard of it, he mustered a very large army and marched out to engage him in battle.
text.NRSV: When King Demetrius heard of it, he assembled a very large army and marched out to meet him in battle.

text.DRB: And Demetrius sent an epistle to Jonathas in peaceable words, to magnify him.
text.KJV: Moreover Demetrius sent letters unto Jonathan with loving words, so as he magnified him.
text.NABRE: Demetrius sent a letter to Jonathan written in peaceful terms, to exalt him;
text.NRSV: Demetrius sent Jonathan a letter in peaceable words to honour him;

text.DRB: For he said: Let us prevent to make peace with him, before he make with Alexander against us:
text.KJV: For said he, Let us first make peace with him, before he join with Alexander against us:
text.NABRE: for he said: “Let us be the first to make peace with him, before he makes peace with Alexander against us,
text.NRSV: for he said to himself, ‘Let us act first to make peace with him before he makes peace with Alexander against us,

text.DRB: for he will remember all the evils, that we have done against him, and against his brother, and against his nation.
text.KJV: Else he will remember all the evils that we have done against him, and against his brethren and his people.
text.NABRE: since he will remember all the wrongs we have done to him, his brothers, and his nation.”
text.NRSV: for he will remember all the wrongs that we did to him and to his brothers and his nation.’

text.DRB: And he gave him authority to gather an army, and to make weapons, and to be himself his fellow: and the hostages that were in the castle, he commanded to be delivered to him.
text.KJV: Wherefore he gave him authority to gather together an host, and to provide weapons, that he might aid him in battle: he commanded also that the hostages that were in the tower should be delivered him.
text.NABRE: So Demetrius authorized him to gather an army and procure arms as his ally; and he ordered that the hostages in the citadel be released to him.
text.NRSV: So Demetrius gave him authority to recruit troops, to equip them with arms, and to become his ally; and he commanded that the hostages in the citadel should be released to him.

text.DRB: And Jonathas came into Jerusalem, and read the epistles in the hearing of all the people, and of them that were in the castle.
text.KJV: Then came Jonathan to Jerusalem, and read the letters in the audience of all the people, and of them that were in the tower:
text.NABRE: Accordingly Jonathan went to Jerusalem and read the letter to all the people and to those who were in the citadel.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan came to Jerusalem and read the letter in the hearing of all the people and of those in the citadel.

text.DRB: And they feared with great fear, because they heard that the king gave him authority to gather an army.
text.KJV: Who were sore afraid, when they heard that the king had given him authority to gather together an host.
text.NABRE: They were struck with fear when they heard that the king had given him authority to gather an army.
text.NRSV: They were greatly alarmed when they heard that the king had given him authority to recruit troops.

text.DRB: And the hostages were delivered to Jonathas, and he rendered them to their parents.
text.KJV: Whereupon they of the tower delivered their hostages unto Jonathan, and he delivered them unto their parents.
text.NABRE: Those in the citadel released the hostages to Jonathan, and he gave them back to their parents.
text.NRSV: But those in the citadel released the hostages to Jonathan, and he returned them to their parents.

text.DRB: And Jonathas dwelt in Jerusalem, and began to build, & to renew the city.
text.KJV: This done, Jonathan settled himself in Jerusalem, and began to build and repair the city.
text.NABRE: Thereafter Jonathan dwelt in Jerusalem, and began to build and restore the city.
text.NRSV: And Jonathan took up residence in Jerusalem and began to rebuild and restore the city.

text.DRB: And he spake to them that made the works, that they should build the walls, and mount Sion round about with square stones for munition: and so they did.
text.KJV: And he commanded the workmen to build the walls and the mount Sion and about with square stones for fortification; and they did so.
text.NABRE: He ordered those doing the work to build the walls and to encircle Mount Zion with square stones for its fortification, and they did so.
text.NRSV: He directed those who were doing the work to build the walls and encircle Mount Zion with squared stones, for better fortification; and they did so.

text.DRB: And the Aliens fled that were in the holds, which Bacchides had built:
text.KJV: Then the strangers, that were in the fortresses which Bacchides had built, fled away;
text.NABRE: The foreigners in the strongholds that Bacchides had built took flight;
text.NRSV: Then the foreigners who were in the strongholds that Bacchides had built fled;

text.DRB: and every man left his place, & departed into his own country:
text.KJV: Insomuch as every man left his place, and went into his own country.
text.NABRE: all of them left their places and returned to their own lands.
text.NRSV: all of them left their places and went back to their own lands.

text.DRB: only in Bethsura there remained some of them, that had forsaken the law, and the precepts of God: for this was for a refuge to them.
text.KJV: Only at Bethsura certain of those that had forsaken the law and the commandments remained still: for it was their place of refuge.
text.NABRE: Only in Beth-zur did some remain of those who had abandoned the law and the commandments, for it was a place of refuge.
text.NRSV: Only in Beth-zur did some remain who had forsaken the law and the commandments, for it served as a place of refuge.

text.DRB: And Alexander the king heard of the promises, that Demetrius had promised to Jonathas: and they told him the battles, & the worthy acts that he did, and his brethren, and the labours, that they endured:
text.KJV: Now when king Alexander had heard what promises Demetrius had sent unto Jonathan: when also it was told him of the battles and noble acts which he and his brethren had done, and of the pains that they had endured,
text.NABRE: King Alexander heard of the promises that Demetrius had made to Jonathan; he was also told of the battles and brave deeds of Jonathan and his brothers and of the troubles that they had endured.
text.NRSV: Now King Alexander heard of all the promises that Demetrius had sent to Jonathan, and he heard of the battles that Jonathan and his brothers had fought, of the brave deeds that they had done, and of the troubles that they had endured.

text.DRB: and he said: Why, shall we find any such man? And now we will make him a friend, and our fellow.
text.KJV: He said, Shall we find such another man? now therefore we will make him our friend and confederate.
text.NABRE: He said, “Shall we ever find another man like him? Let us now make him our friend and ally.”
text.NRSV: So he said, ‘Shall we find another such man? Come now, we will make him our friend and ally.’

text.DRB: And he wrote an epistle, and sent it to him according to these words, saying:
text.KJV: Upon this he wrote a letter, and sent it unto him, according to these words, saying,
text.NABRE: So he sent Jonathan a letter written in these terms:
text.NRSV: And he wrote a letter and sent it to him, in the following words:

text.KJV: King Alexander to his brother Jonathan sendeth greeting:
text.NABRE: “King Alexander sends greetings to his brother Jonathan.
text.NRSV: Jonathan Becomes High Priest ‘King Alexander to his brother Jonathan, greetings.
text.DRB: King Alexander to his brother Jonathas greeting.
We have heard of thee, that thou art mighty of power, and art meet to be our friend:
text.KJV: We have heard of thee, that thou art a man of great power, and meet to be our friend.
text.NABRE: We have heard of you, that you are a mighty warrior and worthy to be our friend.
text.NRSV: We have heard about you, that you are a mighty warrior and worthy to be our friend.

text.KJV: Wherefore now this day we ordain thee to be the high priest of thy nation, and to be called the king’s friend; (and therewithal he sent him a purple robe and a crown of gold:) and require thee to take our part, and keep friendship with us.
text.NABRE: We have therefore appointed you today to be high priest of your nation; you are to be called the King’s Friend, and you are to look after our interests and preserve friendship with us.” He also sent him a purple robe and a crown of gold.
text.NRSV: And so we have appointed you today to be the high priest of your nation; you are to be called the king’s Friend and you are to take our side and keep friendship with us.’ He also sent him a purple robe and a golden crown.
text.DRB: and now we make thee this day high Priest of thy nation, that thou be called the king's friend (& he sent him purple, and a crown of gold) & in our affairs thou be of one mind with us, and keep amity towards us.

text.KJV: So in the seventh month of the hundred and sixtieth year, at the feast of the tabernacles, Jonathan put on the holy robe, and gathered together forces, and provided much armour.
text.NABRE: Jonathan put on the sacred vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred and sixtieth year at the feast of Booths, and he gathered an army and procured many weapons.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan put on the sacred vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred and sixtieth year, at the festival of booths, and he recruited troops and equipped them with arms in abundance.
text.DRB: And Jonathas put on him the holy stole the seventh month, in the year an hundreth threescore in the solemn day of Scenopegia: and he gathered an army, and made very many weapons.
And Demetrius heard these words, and was exceeding sorry, and said:
text.KJV: Whereof when Demetrius heard, he was very sorry, and said,
text.NABRE: A Letter from Demetrius to Jonathan. When Demetrius heard of these things, he was distressed and said:
text.NRSV: When Demetrius heard of these things he was distressed and said,

text.DRB: What is this that we have done, that Alexander hath prevented us to take the friendship of the Jews for his strength?
text.KJV: What have we done, that Alexander hath prevented us in making amity with the Jews to strengthen himself?
text.NABRE: “Why have we allowed Alexander to get ahead of us by gaining the friendship of the Jews and thus strengthening himself?
text.NRSV: ‘What is this that we have done? Alexander has overtaken us in forming a friendship with the Jews to strengthen himself.

text.DRB: I also will write to them words of request, and dignities, and gifts: that they may be with me to aid me.
text.KJV: I also will write unto them words of encouragement, and promise them dignities and gifts, that I may have their aid.
text.NABRE: I too will write them encouraging words and offer honors and gifts, so that they may support me.”
text.NRSV: I also will write them words of encouragement and promise them honour and gifts, so that I may have their help.’

text.DRB: And he wrote to them in these words: King Demetrius to the nation of the Jews greeting.
text.KJV: He sent unto them therefore to this effect: King Demetrius unto the people of the Jews sendeth greeting:
text.NABRE: So he sent them this message: “King Demetrius sends greetings to the Jewish nation.
text.NRSV: So he sent a message to them in the following words: A Letter from Demetrius to Jonathan ‘King Demetrius to the nation of the Jews, greetings.

text.DRB: Because you have kept covenant towards us, and have continued in our amity, and have not joined to our enemies, we have heard of it, and are glad.
text.KJV: Whereas ye have kept covenants with us, and continued in our friendship, not joining yourselves with our enemies, we have heard hereof, and are glad.
text.NABRE: We have heard how you have kept the treaty with us and continued in our friendship and not gone over to our enemies, and we are glad.
text.NRSV: Since you have kept your agreement with us and have continued your friendship with us, and have not sided with our enemies, we have heard of it and rejoiced.

text.DRB: And now persevere still to keep fidelity towards us, & we will reward you with good things, for that you have done with us.
text.KJV: Wherefore now continue ye still to be faithful unto us, and we will well recompense you for the things ye do in our behalf,
text.NABRE: Continue, therefore, to keep faith with us, and we will reward you with favors in return for what you do in our behalf.
text.NRSV: Now continue still to keep faith with us, and we will repay you with good for what you do for us.

text.DRB: And we will remit you many charges, and will give you many gifts.
text.KJV: And will grant you many immunities, and give you rewards.
text.NABRE: We will grant you many exemptions and will bestow gifts on you.
text.NRSV: We will grant you many immunities and give you gifts.

text.DRB: And now I release you, and all the Jews from tributes, & I pardon you the prices of salt, & remit the crowns, & the thirds of the seed:
text.KJV: And now do I free you, and for your sake I release all the Jews, from tributes, and from the customs of salt, and from crown taxes,
text.NABRE: “I now free you and exempt all the Jews from the tribute, the salt tax, and the crown levies.
text.NRSV: ‘I now free you and exempt all the Jews from payment of tribute and salt tax and crown levies,

text.DRB: and the half of the fruit of trees, which is my portion, I leave to you from this day forward, that it be not taken of the land of Juda, and of the three cities that are added thereto of Samaria, & Galilee from this day & for ever:
text.KJV: And from that which appertaineth unto me to receive for the third part or the seed, and the half of the fruit of the trees, I release it from this day forth, so that they shall not be taken of the land of Judea, nor of the three governments which are added thereunto out of the country of Samaria and Galilee, from this day forth for evermore.
text.NABRE: Instead of collecting the third of the grain and the half of the fruit of the trees that should be my share, I renounce the right from this day forward. Neither now nor in the future will I collect them from the land of Judah or from the three districts annexed from Samaria.
text.NRSV: and instead of collecting the third of the grain and the half of the fruit of the trees that I should receive, I release them from this day and henceforth. I will not collect them from the land of Judah or from the three districts added to it from Samaria and Galilee, from this day and for all time.

text.DRB: & let Jerusalem be holy, and free with the coasts thereof: & the tithes, & tributes be they to it.
text.KJV: Let Jerusalem also be holy and free, with the borders thereof, both from tenths and tributes.
text.NABRE: Let Jerusalem and her territory, her tithes and her tolls, be sacred and free from tax.
text.NRSV: Jerusalem and its environs, its tithes and its revenues, shall be holy and free from tax.

text.DRB: I release also the authority of the castle, that is in Jerusalem, and I give it to the high priest, to place therein men whomsoever he shall choose, that may keep it.
text.KJV: And as for the tower which is at Jerusalem, I yield up authority over it, and give the high priest, that he may set in it such men as he shall choose to keep it.
text.NABRE: I also yield my authority over the citadel in Jerusalem, and I transfer it to the high priest, that he may put in it such men as he shall choose to guard it.
text.NRSV: I release also my control of the citadel in Jerusalem and give it to the high priest, so that he may station in it men of his own choice to guard it.

text.DRB: And every soul of the Jews, that is captive from the land of Juda in all my kingdom, I dismiss free for nothing, that all may be discharged also from tributes of their cattle.
text.KJV: Moreover I freely set at liberty every one of the Jews, that were carried captives out of the land of Judea into any part of my kingdom, and I will that all my officers remit the tributes even of their cattle.
text.NABRE: Every Jew who has been carried into captivity from the land of Judah into any part of my kingdom I set at liberty without ransom; and let all their taxes, even those on their cattle, be canceled.
text.NRSV: And everyone of the Jews taken as a captive from the land of Judah into any part of my kingdom, I set free without payment; and let all officials cancel also the taxes on their livestock.

text.DRB: And all the solemn days, & the sabbaths, & the new moons, & the days appointed, and three days before the solemn day, and three days after the solemn day let them all be of immunity & remission, to all the Jews that are in my kingdom:
text.KJV: Furthermore I will that all the feasts, and sabbaths, and new moons, and solemn days, and the three days before the feast, and the three days after the feast shall be all of immunity and freedom for all the Jews in my realm.
text.NABRE: Let all feast days, sabbaths, new moon festivals, appointed days, and the three days that precede each feast day, and the three days that follow, be days of immunity and exemption for all Jews in my kingdom.
text.NRSV: ‘All the festivals and sabbaths and new moons and appointed days, and the three days before a festival and the three after a festival—let them all be days of immunity and release for all the Jews who are in my kingdom.

text.DRB: & no man shall have power to do any thing, & to make businesses against any of them in any cause.
text.KJV: Also no man shall have authority to meddle with or to molest any of them in any matter.
text.NABRE: No one will have authority to exact payment from them or to harass any of them in any matter.
text.NRSV: No one shall have authority to exact anything from them or annoy any of them about any matter.

text.DRB: And let there be enrolled of the Jews in the king's army to the number of thirty thousand men: & allowance shall be given them as behoveth to all the king's bands, & of them there shall be ordained certain, that shall be in the fortresses of the great king:
text.KJV: I will further, that there be enrolled among the king’s forces about thirty thousand men of the Jews, unto whom pay shall be given, as belongeth to all king’s forces.
text.NABRE: “Let thirty thousand Jews be enrolled in the king’s army and allowances be given them, as is due to all the king’s soldiers.
text.NRSV: ‘Let Jews be enrolled in the king’s forces to the number of thirty thousand men, and let the maintenance be given them that is due to all the forces of the king.

text.DRB: and of these there shall be appointed over the affairs of the kingdom, that are done of trust, and let there be princes of them, and let them walk in their own laws, as the king hath commanded in the land of Juda.
text.KJV: And of them some shall be placed in the king’s strong holds, of whom also some shall be set over the affairs of the kingdom, which are of trust: and I will that their overseers and governors be of themselves, and that they live after their own laws, even as the king hath commanded in the land of Judea.
text.NABRE: Let some of them be stationed in the king’s principal strongholds, and of these let some be given positions of trust in the affairs of the kingdom. Let their superiors and their rulers be chosen from among them, and let them follow their own laws, as the king has commanded in the land of Judah.
text.NRSV: Let some of them be stationed in the great strongholds of the king, and let some of them be put in positions of trust in the kingdom. Let their officers and leaders be of their own number, and let them live by their own laws, just as the king has commanded in the land of Judah.

text.DRB: And the three cities that are added to Jury of the country of Samaria, let them be accounted with Jury: that they may be under one, and obey no other power, but the high priest's:
text.KJV: And concerning the three governments that are added to Judea from the country of Samaria, let them be joined with Judea, that they may be reckoned to be under one, nor bound to obey other authority than the high priest’s.
text.NABRE: “Let the three districts that have been added to Judea from the province of Samaria be annexed to Judea so that they may be under one rule and obey no other authority than the high priest.
text.NRSV: ‘As for the three districts that have been added to Judea from the country of Samaria, let them be annexed to Judea so that they may be considered to be under one ruler and obey no other authority than the high priest.

text.DRB: Ptolomais, and the confines thereof, which I have given for a gift to the holies, that are in Jerusalem, to the necessary charges of the holy things.
text.KJV: As for Ptolemais, and the land pertaining thereto, I give it as a free gift to the sanctuary at Jerusalem for the necessary expences of the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: Ptolemais and its confines I give as a present to the sanctuary in Jerusalem for the necessary expenses of the sanctuary.
text.NRSV: Ptolemais and the land adjoining it I have given as a gift to the sanctuary in Jerusalem, to meet the necessary expenses of the sanctuary.

text.DRB: And I give every year fifteen thousand sicles of silver of the king's accounts, that belong to me:
text.KJV: Moreover I give every year fifteen thousand shekels of silver out of the king’s accounts from the places appertaining.
text.NABRE: I make a yearly personal grant of fifteen thousand silver shekels out of the royal revenues, taken from appropriate places.
text.NRSV: I also grant fifteen thousand shekels of silver yearly out of the king’s revenues from appropriate places.

text.DRB: & all that is above which they had not rendered, that were over the affairs the years before, from this time they shall give it to the works of the house.
text.KJV: And all the overplus, which the officers payed not in as in former time, from henceforth shall be given toward the works of the temple.
text.NABRE: All the additional funds that the officials did not hand over as they had done in the first years shall henceforth be handed over for the services of the temple.
text.NRSV: And all the additional funds that the government officials have not paid as they did in the first years, they shall give from now on for the service of the temple.

text.DRB: And beside these five thousand sicles of silver, which they received of the account of the holies every year: & let these pertain to the priests, that execute the ministry.
text.KJV: And beside this, the five thousand shekels of silver, which they took from the uses of the temple out of the accounts year by year, even those things shall be released, because they appertain to the priests that minister.
