text.KJV: And whosoever should do otherwise, or break any of these things, he should be punished.
text.NABRE: Whoever acts otherwise or violates any of these prescriptions shall be liable to punishment.
text.NRSV: Whoever acts contrary to these decisions or rejects any of them shall be liable to punishment.’

text.DRB: And it pleased all the people to appoint Simon, and to do according to these words.
text.KJV: Thus it liked all the people to deal with Simon, and to do as hath been said.
text.NABRE: “‘Thus all the people approved of granting Simon the right to act in accord with these decisions,
text.NRSV: All the people agreed to grant Simon the right to act in accordance with these decisions.

text.DRB: And Simon took it upon him, and it pleased him to execute high Priest-hood, and to be duke & prince of the nation of the Jews, and of the Priests, and to be chief over all.
text.KJV: Then Simon accepted hereof, and was well pleased to be high priest, and captain and governor of the Jews and priests, and to defend them all.
text.NABRE: and Simon accepted and agreed to be high priest, governor, and ethnarch of the Jewish people and priests, and to have authority over all.’”
text.NRSV: So Simon accepted and agreed to be high priest, to be commander and ethnarch of the Jews and priests, and to be protector of them all.

text.DRB: And this writing they bade them put in tables of brass, and to set them in the circuit of the holies, in a famous place:
text.KJV: So they commanded that this writing should be put in tables of brass, and that they should be set up within the compass of the sanctuary in a conspicuous place;
text.NABRE: It was decreed that this inscription should be engraved on bronze tablets, to be set up in a conspicuous place in the precincts of the sanctuary,
text.NRSV: And they gave orders to inscribe this decree on bronze tablets, to put them up in a conspicuous place in the precincts of the sanctuary,

text.DRB: and to put a copy thereof in the treasury, that Simon may have it, and his children.
text.KJV: Also that the copies thereof should be laid up in the treasury, to the end that Simon and his sons might have them.
text.NABRE: and that copies of it should be deposited in the treasury, where they would be available to Simon and his sons.
text.NRSV: and to deposit copies of them in the treasury, so that Simon and his sons might have them.

text.KJV: Moreover Antiochus son of Demetrius the king sent letters from the isles of the sea unto Simon the priest and prince of the Jews, and to all the people;
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Letter of Antiochus VII. Antiochus, son of King Demetrius, sent a letter from the islands of the sea to Simon, the priest and ethnarch of the Jews, and to all the nation,
text.NRSV: Letter of Antiochus VII 15
text.DRB: And king Antiochus the son of Demetrius sent epistles from the isles of the sea to Simon the priest, and prince of the nation of the Jews, and to all the nation:
and they contained this tenure: King Antiochus to Simon the grand-Priest, and to the nation of the Jews greeting.
text.KJV: The contents whereof were these: King Antiochus to Simon the high priest and prince of his nation, and to the people of the Jews, greeting:
text.NABRE: which read as follows: “King Antiochus sends greetings to Simon, the high priest and ethnarch, and to the Jewish nation.
text.NRSV: its contents were as follows: ‘King Antiochus to Simon the high priest and ethnarch and to the nation of the Jews, greetings.

text.DRB: Because certain pestilent men have obtained the kingdom of our fathers, and I mean to challenge the kingdom, and to restore it as it was before: and I have chosen a great army, and have made ships of war.
text.KJV: Forasmuch as certain pestilent men have usurped the kingdom of our fathers, and my purpose is to challenge it again, that I may restore it to the old estate, and to that end have gathered a multitude of foreign soldiers together, and prepared ships of war;
text.NABRE: Whereas certain villains have gained control of the kingdom of our ancestors, I intend to reclaim it, that I may restore it to its former state. I have recruited a large number of mercenary troops and equipped warships.
text.NRSV: Whereas certain scoundrels have gained control of the kingdom of our ancestors, and I intend to lay claim to the kingdom so that I may restore it as it formerly was, and have recruited a host of mercenary troops and have equipped warships,

text.DRB: And I will march through the country, that I may take revenge of them, that have destroyed our country, and that have made many cities desolate in my realm.
text.KJV: My meaning also being to go through the country, that I may be avenged of them that have destroyed it, and made many cities in the kingdom desolate:
text.NABRE: I intend to make a landing in the country so that I may take revenge on those who have ruined our country and laid waste many cities in my kingdom.
text.NRSV: and intend to make a landing in the country so that I may proceed against those who have destroyed our country and those who have devastated many cities in my kingdom,

text.DRB: Now therefore I establish unto thee all the oblations, which all the kings before me remitted unto thee, & what other gifts soever they remitted thee:
text.KJV: Now therefore I confirm unto thee all the oblations which the kings before me granted thee, and whatsoever gifts besides they granted.
text.NABRE: “Now, therefore, I confirm to you all the tax exemptions that the kings before me granted you and whatever other privileges they conceded to you.
text.NRSV: now therefore I confirm to you all the tax remissions that the kings before me have granted you, and a release from all the other payments from which they have released you.

text.DRB: and I permit thee to make a coin of thy own money in thy country:
text.KJV: I give thee leave also to coin money for thy country with thine own stamp.
text.NABRE: I authorize you to coin your own money, as legal tender in your country.
text.NRSV: I permit you to mint your own coinage as money for your country,

text.DRB: and Jerusalem to be holy and free, and all the armour that is made, and the fortresses which thou hast built, and which thou dost hold, let them remain to thee.
text.KJV: And as concerning Jerusalem and the sanctuary, let them be free; and all the armour that thou hast made, and fortresses that thou hast built, and keepest in thine hands, let them remain unto thee.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem and its sanctuary shall be free. All the weapons you have prepared and all the strongholds you have built and now occupy shall remain in your possession.
text.NRSV: and I grant freedom to Jerusalem and the sanctuary. All the weapons that you have prepared and the strongholds that you have built and now hold shall remain yours.

text.DRB: And all that is due to the king, and the things that are to be the king's hereafter, from this present and for all time, they are remitted to thee.
text.KJV: And if anything be, or shall be, owing to the king, let it be forgiven thee from this time forth for evermore.
text.NABRE: All debts, present or future, due to the royal treasury shall be canceled for you, now and for all time.
text.NRSV: Every debt you owe to the royal treasury and any such future debts shall be cancelled for you from henceforth and for all time.

text.DRB: And when we shall have obtained our kingdom, we will glorify thee, and thy nation, & the temple with great glory, so that your glory shall be made manifest in all the earth.
text.KJV: Furthermore, when we have obtained our kingdom, we will honour thee, and thy nation, and thy temple, with great honour, so that your honour shall be known throughout the world.
text.NABRE: When we establish our kingdom, we will greatly honor you and your nation and the temple, so that your glory will be manifest in all the earth.”
text.NRSV: When we gain control of our kingdom, we will bestow great honour on you and your nation and the temple, so that your glory will become manifest in all the earth.’

text.KJV: In the hundred threescore and fourteenth year went Antiochus into the land of his fathers: at which time all the forces came together unto him, so that few were left with Tryphon.
text.NABRE: In the one hundred and seventy-fourth year Antiochus invaded the land of his ancestors, and all the troops rallied to him, so that few were left with Trypho.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred and seventy-fourth year Antiochus set out and invaded the land of his ancestors. All the troops rallied to him, so that there were only a few with Trypho.
text.DRB: In the year an hundreth seventy four went forth Antiochus into the land of his fathers, and all the hosts assembled unto him, so that there were few left with Tryphon.
And Antiochus the king pursued him, and he came to Dora flying by the sea coast.
text.KJV: Wherefore being pursued by king Antiochus, he fled unto Dora, which lieth by the sea side:
text.NABRE: Pursued by Antiochus, Trypho fled to Dor, by the sea,
text.NRSV: Antiochus pursued him, and Trypho came in his flight to Dor, which is by the sea;

text.DRB: For he knew that evils were heaped upon him, and the army forsook him.
text.KJV: For he saw that troubles came upon him all at once, and that his forces had forsaken him.
text.NABRE: realizing what troubles had come upon him now that his soldiers had deserted him.
text.NRSV: for he knew that troubles had converged on him, and his troops had deserted him.

text.DRB: And Antiochus camped upon Dora with an hundred twenty thousand men of war, & eight thousand horsemen:
text.KJV: Then camped Antiochus against Dora, having with him an hundred and twenty thousand men of war, and eight thousand horsemen.
text.NABRE: Antiochus encamped before Dor with a hundred and twenty thousand infantry and eight thousand cavalry.
text.NRSV: So Antiochus encamped against Dor, and with him were one hundred and twenty thousand warriors and eight thousand cavalry.

text.DRB: and he compassed the city, and the ships approached to the sea: & they vexed the city by land & by sea, and suffered none to come in, or to go out.
text.KJV: And when he had compassed the city round about, and joined ships close to the town on the sea side, he vexed the city by land and by sea, neither suffered he any to go out or in.
text.NABRE: While he surrounded the city, his ships closed from the sea, so that he pressed it hard by land and sea and let no one go in or out.
text.NRSV: He surrounded the town, and the ships joined battle from the sea; he pressed the town hard from land and sea, and permitted no one to leave or enter it.

text.DRB: And Numenius came, and they that had been with him, from the city of Rome, having epistles written to kings, and countries, wherein were contained these words:
text.KJV: In the mean season came Numenius and his company from Rome, having letters to the kings and countries; wherein were written these things:
text.NABRE: Roman Alliance Renewed. Meanwhile, Numenius and his companions came from Rome with letters containing this message to various kings and countries:
text.NRSV: Rome Supports the Jews Then Numenius and his companions arrived from Rome, with letters to the kings and countries, in which the following was written:

text.DRB: Lucius the Consul of the Romans, to Ptolemee the king greeting.
text.KJV: Lucius, consul of the Romans unto king Ptolemee, greeting:
text.NABRE: “Lucius, Consul of the Romans, sends greetings to King Ptolemy.
text.NRSV: ‘Lucius, consul of the Romans, to King Ptolemy, greetings.

text.DRB: The Ambassadors of the Jews our friends came to us, renewing the old amity, and society, being sent from Simon the prince of the priests, and the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: The Jews’ ambassadors, our friends and confederates, came unto us to renew the old friendship and league, being sent from Simon the high priest, and from the people of the Jews:
text.NABRE: Ambassadors of the Jews, our friends and allies, have come to us to renew their earlier friendship and alliance. They had been sent by Simon the high priest and the Jewish people,
text.NRSV: The envoys of the Jews have come to us as our friends and allies to renew our ancient friendship and alliance. They had been sent by the high priest Simon and by the Jewish people

text.DRB: And they brought also a buckler of gold of a thousand pounds.
text.KJV: And they brought a shield of gold of a thousand pound.
text.NABRE: and they brought with them a gold shield of a thousand minas.
text.NRSV: and have brought a gold shield weighing one thousand minas.

text.DRB: It hath pleased us therefore to write to the kings, & countries, that they do them no harm, nor impugn them, & their cities, and their countries: and that they give no aid to them that fight against them.
text.KJV: We thought it good therefore to write unto the kings and countries, that they should do them no harm, nor fight against them, their cities, or countries, nor yet aid their enemies against them.
text.NABRE: Therefore we have decided to write to various kings and countries, that they are not to venture to harm them, or wage war against them or their cities or their country, and are not to assist those who fight against them.
text.NRSV: We therefore have decided to write to the kings and countries that they should not seek their harm or make war against them and their cities and their country, or make alliance with those who war against them.

text.DRB: And it hath seemed good unto us to take the buckler of them.
text.KJV: It seemed also good to us to receive the shield of them.
text.NABRE: We have also decided to accept the shield from them.
text.NRSV: And it has seemed good to us to accept the shield from them.

text.DRB: If therefore any pestilent men are fled out of their country to you, deliver them to Simon the Prince of the Priests, that he may punish them according to their law.
text.KJV: If therefore there be any pestilent fellows, that have fled from their country unto you, deliver them unto Simon the high priest, that he may punish them according to their own law.
text.NABRE: If, then, any troublemakers from their country take refuge with you, hand them over to Simon the high priest, so that he may punish them according to their law.”
text.NRSV: Therefore if any scoundrels have fled to you from their country, hand them over to the high priest Simon, so that he may punish them according to their law.’

text.DRB: These self same things were written to Demetrius the king, and to Attalus, and to Ariarathes, and to Arsaces,
text.KJV: The same things wrote he likewise unto Demetrius the king, and Attalus, to Ariarathes, and Arsaces,
text.NABRE: The consul sent identical letters to Kings Demetrius, Attalus, Ariarthes and Arsaces;
text.NRSV: The consul wrote the same thing to King Demetrius and to Attalus and Ariarathes and Arsaces,

text.DRB: and into all countries: and to Lampsaces, & to the Spartiates, and to Delus, and to Myndus, and to Sicyon, & to Caria, and to Samus, & Pamphilia, and Lycia, and Alicarnassus, and Coo, & Siden, & Aradon, and Rhodes, and Phaselis, & Gortyna, and Gnidus, and Cypres, & Cyrenee.
text.KJV: And to all the countries and to Sampsames, and the Lacedemonians, and to Delus, and Myndus, and Sicyon, and Caria, and Samos, and Pamphylia, and Lycia, and Halicarnassus, and Rhodus, and Aradus, and Cos, and Side, and Aradus, and Gortyna, and Cnidus, and Cyprus, and Cyrene.
text.NABRE: to all the countries—Sampsames, the Spartans, Delos, Myndos, Sicyon, Caria, Samos, Pamphylia, Lycia, Halicarnassus, Rhodes, Phaselis, Cos, Side, Aradus, Gortyna, Cnidus, Cyprus, and Cyrene.
text.NRSV: and to all the countries, and to Sampsames, and to the Spartans, and to Delos, and to Myndos, and to Sicyon, and to Caria, and to Samos, and to Pamphylia, and to Lycia, and to Halicarnassus, and to Rhodes, and to Phaselis, and to Cos, and to Side, and to Aradus and Gortyna and Cnidus and Cyprus and Cyrene.

text.DRB: And a copy thereof they wrote to Simon the prince of the priests and people of the Jews.
text.KJV: And the copy hereof they wrote to Simon the high priest.
text.NABRE: A copy of the letter was also sent to Simon the high priest.
text.NRSV: They also sent a copy of these things to the high priest Simon.

text.DRB: But king Antiochus moved his camp unto Dora the second time, setting hands always upon it, and making engines: & he shut up Tryphon, that he could not go forth.
text.KJV: So Antiochus the king camped against Dora the second day, assaulting it continually, and making engines, by which means he shut up Tryphon, that he could neither go out nor in.
text.NABRE: Hostility from Antiochus VII. When King Antiochus encamped before Dor, he assaulted it continuously both with troops and with the siege engines he had made. He blockaded Trypho by preventing anyone from going in or out.
text.NRSV: Antiochus VII Threatens Simon King Antiochus besieged Dor for the second time, continually throwing his forces against it and making engines of war; and he shut Trypho up and kept him from going out or in.

text.DRB: And Simon sent unto him two thousand chosen men for aid, and silver, & gold, and aboundance of furniture.
text.KJV: At that time Simon sent him two thousand chosen men to aid him; silver also, and gold, and much armour.
text.NABRE: Simon sent to Antiochus’ support two thousand elite troops, together with silver and gold and much equipment.
text.NRSV: And Simon sent to Antiochus two thousand picked troops, to fight for him, and silver and gold and a large amount of military equipment.

text.DRB: And he would not take them, but brake all things that he covenanted with him before, and alienated himself from him.
text.KJV: Nevertheless he would not receive them, but brake all the covenants which he had made with him afore, and became strange unto him.
text.NABRE: But he refused to accept the aid; in fact, he broke all the agreements he had previously made with Simon and became hostile toward him.
text.NRSV: But he refused to receive them, and broke all the agreements he formerly had made with Simon, and became estranged from him.

text.DRB: And he sent to him Athenobius one of his friends, to treat with him, saying: You hold Joppe, and Gazara, and the castle, that is in Jerusalem, cities of my kingdom:
text.KJV: Furthermore he sent unto him Athenobius, one of his friends, to commune with him, and say, Ye withhold Joppa and Gazera; with the tower that is in Jerusalem, which are cities of my realm.
text.NABRE: He sent Athenobius, one of his Friends, to confer with Simon and say: “You are occupying Joppa and Gazara and the citadel of Jerusalem; these are cities of my kingdom.
text.NRSV: He sent to him Athenobius, one of his Friends, to confer with him, saying, ‘You hold control of Joppa and Gazara and the citadel in Jerusalem; they are cities of my kingdom.

text.DRB: their borders you have made desolate, and you have made a great plague in the land, and have ruled in many places in my kingdom.
text.KJV: The borders thereof ye have wasted, and done great hurt in the land, and got the dominion of many places within my kingdom.
text.NABRE: You have laid waste their territories, done great harm to the land, and taken possession of many districts in my kingdom.
text.NRSV: You have devastated their territory, you have done great damage in the land, and you have taken possession of many places in my kingdom.

text.DRB: Now therefore deliver the cities, that you have taken, and the tributes of the places, wherein you have ruled without the borders of Jury.
text.KJV: Now therefore deliver the cities which ye have taken, and the tributes of the places, whereof ye have gotten dominion without the borders of Judea:
text.NABRE: Now, therefore, give up the cities you have seized and the tribute money of the districts you control outside the territory of Judea;
text.NRSV: Now then, hand over the cities that you have seized and the tribute money of the places that you have conquered outside the borders of Judea;

text.DRB: But if not, give you for them five hundred talents of silver, and for the destruction, that you have made, & the tributes of cities other five hundred talents: but if not, we will come and expugn you.
text.KJV: Or else give me for them five hundred talents of silver; and for the harm that ye have done, and the tributes of the cities, other five hundred talents: if not, we will come and fight against you
text.NABRE: or instead, pay me five hundred talents of silver for the devastation you have caused and five hundred talents more for the tribute money of the cities. If you do not do this, we will come and make war on you.”
text.NRSV: or else pay me five hundred talents of silver for the destruction that you have caused and five hundred talents more for the tribute money of the cities. Otherwise we will come and make war on you.’

text.DRB: And Athenobius the king's friend came into Jerusalem, and saw the glory of Simon, and his magnificence in gold, and silver, and furniture aboundant, and he was astonished, & told him the king's words.
text.KJV: So Athenobius the king’s friend came to Jerusalem: and when he saw the glory of Simon, and the cupboard of gold and silver plate, and his great attendance, he was astonished, and told him the king’s message.
text.NABRE: So Athenobius, the king’s Friend, came to Jerusalem and on seeing the splendor of Simon’s court, the gold and silver plate on the sideboard, and his rich display, he was amazed. When he gave him the king’s message,
text.NRSV: So Athenobius, the king’s Friend, came to Jerusalem, and when he saw the splendour of Simon, and the sideboard with its gold and silver plate, and his great magnificence, he was amazed. When he reported to him the king’s message,

text.DRB: And Simon answered him, and said to him: Neither have we taken other men's land, neither do we hold that is other men's: but the inheritance of our fathers, which was a certain season unjustly possessed of our enemies.
text.KJV: Then answered Simon, and said unto him, We have neither taken other men’s land, nor holden that which appertaineth to others, but the inheritance of our fathers, which our enemies had wrongfully in possession a certain time.
text.NABRE: Simon said to him in reply: “It is not foreign land we have taken nor have we seized the property of others, but only our ancestral heritage which for a time had been unjustly held by our enemies.
text.NRSV: Simon said to him in reply: ‘We have neither taken foreign land nor seized foreign property, but only the inheritance of our ancestors, which at one time had been unjustly taken by our enemies.

text.DRB: But we having opportunity challenge the inheritance of our fathers.
text.KJV: Wherefore we, having opportunity, hold the inheritance of our fathers.
text.NABRE: Now that we have the opportunity, we are holding on to the heritage of our ancestors.
text.NRSV: Now that we have the opportunity, we are firmly holding the inheritance of our ancestors.

text.DRB: For concerning Joppe, and Gazara, that thou complainest they made a great plague among the people, and in our country: for these we given an hundred talents. And Athenobius answered him not a word.
text.KJV: And whereas thou demandest Joppa and Gazera, albeit they did great harm unto the people in our country, yet will we give thee an hundred talents for them. Hereunto Athenobius answered him not a word;
text.NABRE: As for Joppa and Gazara, which you demand, those cities were doing great harm to our people and our country. For these we will give you a hundred talents.” Athenobius made no reply,
text.NRSV: As for Joppa and Gazara, which you demand, they were causing great damage among the people and to our land; for them we will give you one hundred talents.’ Athenobius did not answer him a word,

text.DRB: But with anger returning to the king, he reported unto him these words, and the glory of Simon, and all things that he saw, and the king was wrath with great anger.
text.KJV: But returned in a rage to the king, and made report unto him of these speeches, and of the glory of Simon, and of all that he had seen: whereupon the king was exceeding wroth.
text.NABRE: but returned to the king in anger. When he told him of Simon’s words, of his splendor, and of all he had seen, the king fell into a violent rage.
text.NRSV: but returned in wrath to the king and reported to him these words, and also the splendour of Simon and all that he had seen. And the king was very angry.

text.DRB: And Tryphon fled by ship to Orthosias
text.KJV: In the mean time fled Tryphon by ship unto Orthosias.
text.NABRE: Victory over Cendebeus. Trypho had boarded a ship and escaped to Orthosia.
text.NRSV: Victory over Cendebeus Meanwhile Trypho embarked on a ship and escaped to Orthosia.

text.DRB: And the king appointed Cendebæus captain by the sea coast, and gave him an army of foot men and horsemen.
text.KJV: Then the king made Cendebeus captain of the sea coast, and gave him an host of footmen and horsemen,
text.NABRE: Then the king appointed Cendebeus commander-in-chief of the seacoast, and gave him infantry and cavalry forces.
text.NRSV: Then the king made Cendebeus commander-in-chief of the coastal country, and gave him troops of infantry and cavalry.

text.DRB: And he commanded him to move the camp against the face of Jurie: and he commanded him to build Cedron, and to fortify the gates of the city, and to conquer the people. But the king pursued Tryphon.
text.KJV: And commanded him to remove his host toward Judea; also he commanded him to build up Cedron, and to fortify the gates, and to war against the people; but as for the king himself, he pursued Tryphon.
text.NABRE: He ordered him to encamp against Judea and to fortify Kedron and strengthen its gates, so that he could wage war on the people. Meanwhile the king went in pursuit of Trypho.
text.NRSV: He commanded him to encamp against Judea, to build up Kedron and fortify its gates, and to make war on the people; but the king pursued Trypho.

text.DRB: And Cendebæus came to Jamnia, and began to provoke the people, and to conculcate Jurie, & to take the people captive, and to kill, and to build Cedron.
text.KJV: So Cendebeus came to Jamnia and began to provoke the people and to invade Judea, and to take the people prisoners, and slay them.
text.NABRE: When Cendebeus came to Jamnia, he began to harass the people and to make incursions into Judea, where he took people captive and massacred them.
text.NRSV: So Cendebeus came to Jamnia and began to provoke the people and invade Judea and take the people captive and kill them.

text.DRB: And he placed there horsemen, and an host: that going forth they might walk by the way of Jurie, as the king appointed him.
text.KJV: And when he had built up Cedrou, he set horsemen there, and an host of footmen, to the end that issuing out they might make outroads upon the ways of Judea, as the king had commanded him.
text.NABRE: As the king ordered, he fortified Kedron and stationed cavalry and infantry there, so that they could go out and patrol the roads of Judea.
text.NRSV: He built up Kedron and stationed horsemen and troops there, so that they might go out and make raids along the highways of Judea, as the king had ordered him.

text.KJV: Then came up John from Gazera, and told Simon his father what Cendebeus had done.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 John then went up from Gazara and told his father Simon what Cendebeus was doing.
text.DRB: And John went up from Gazara, and told Simon his father what Cendebæus did among their people.

And Simon called his two elder sons, Judas and John, and he said to them: I and my brethren, and the house of my father, have expugned the enemies of Israel from our youth even to this day: and it hath prospered in our hands to deliver Israel oftentimes.
text.KJV: Wherefore Simon called his two eldest sons, Judas and John, and said unto them, I, and my brethren, and my father’s house, have ever from my youth unto this day fought against the enemies of Israel; and things have prospered so well in our hands, that we have delivered Israel oftentimes.
text.NABRE: Simon called his two oldest sons, Judas and John, and said to them: “I and my brothers and my father’s house have fought the wars of Israel from our youth until today, and many times we succeeded in saving Israel.
text.NRSV: And Simon called in his two eldest sons Judas and John, and said to them: ‘My brothers and I and my father’s house have fought the wars of Israel from our youth until this day, and things have prospered in our hands so that we have delivered Israel many times.

text.DRB: And now I am old, but be you in place of me, and my brethren, and going forth fight for our nation: and the help from heaven be with you.
text.KJV: But now I am old, and ye, by God’s mercy, are of a sufficient age: be ye instead of me and my brother, and go and fight for our nation, and the help from heaven be with you.
text.NABRE: I have now grown old, but you, by the mercy of Heaven, have come to maturity. Take my place and my brother’s, and go out and fight for our nation; and may the help of Heaven be with you!”
text.NRSV: But now I have grown old, and you by Heaven’s mercy are mature in years. Take my place and my brother’s, and go out and fight for our nation, and may the help that comes from Heaven be with you.’

text.DRB: And he chose out of the country twenty thousand fighting men and horsemen, and they went forth to Cendebæus: and they slept in Modin.
text.KJV: So he chose out of the country twenty thousand men of war with horsemen, who went out against Cendebeus, and rested that night at Modin.
text.NABRE: John then mustered in the land twenty thousand warriors and cavalry. Setting out against Cendebeus, they spent the night at Modein,
text.NRSV: So John chose out of the country twenty thousand warriors and cavalry, and they marched against Cendebeus and camped for the night in Modein.

text.DRB: And they arose in the morning, and went into the plain field: and lo a mighty great army coming to meet them of foot men, and horsemen, & there was a running river between them.
text.KJV: And when as they rose in the morning, and went into the plain, behold, a mighty great host both of footmen and horsemen came against them: howbeit there was a water brook betwixt them.
text.NABRE: rose early, and marched into the plain. There, facing them, was an immense army of foot soldiers and cavalry, and between the two armies was a wadi.
text.NRSV: Early in the morning they started out and marched into the plain, where a large force of infantry and cavalry was coming to meet them; and a stream lay between them.

text.DRB: And he camped against the face of them, himself and his people, and he saw the people fearful to pass through the torrent, and he passed through first: and the men saw him, and they passed over after him.
text.KJV: So he and his people pitched over against them: and when he saw that the people were afraid to go over the water brook, he went first over himself, and then the men seeing him passed through after him.
text.NABRE: John and his people took their position against the enemy. Seeing that his people were afraid to cross the wadi, John crossed first. When his men saw this, they crossed over after him.
text.NRSV: Then he and his army lined up against them. He saw that the soldiers were afraid to cross the stream, so he crossed over first; and when his troops saw him, they crossed over after him.

text.DRB: And he divided the people, and the horsemen in the midst of the footmen: and the horsemen of the adversaries were exceeding many.
text.KJV: That done, he divided his men, and set the horsemen in the midst of the footmen: for the enemies’ horsemen were very many.
text.NABRE: Then he divided his infantry and put his cavalry in the center, for the enemy’s cavalry were very numerous.
text.NRSV: Then he divided the army and placed the cavalry in the centre of the infantry, for the cavalry of the enemy were very numerous.

text.DRB: And they sounded with the holy trumpets: and Cendebæus was put to flight, and his camp: and there fell many of them wounded, & the rest fled into the hold.
text.KJV: Then sounded they with the holy trumpets: whereupon Cendebeus and his host were put to flight, so that many of them were slain, and the remnant gat them to the strong hold.
text.NABRE: They blew the trumpets, and Cendebeus and his army were routed; many of them fell wounded, and the rest fled toward the stronghold.
text.NRSV: They sounded the trumpets, and Cendebeus and his army were put to flight; many of them fell wounded and the rest fled into the stronghold.

text.DRB: Then was Judas John's brother wounded: But John pursued them, till he came to Cedron, which he built:
text.KJV: At that time was Judas John’s brother wounded; but John still followed after them, until he came to Cedron, which Cendebeus had built.
text.NABRE: It was then that John’s brother Judas fell wounded; but John pursued them until Cendebeus reached Kedron, which he had fortified.
text.NRSV: At that time Judas the brother of John was wounded, but John pursued them until Cendebeus reached Kedron, which he had built.

text.DRB: and they fled even to the towers, that were in the fields of Azotus, and he burnt them with fire. And there fell of them two thousand men, and he returned into Jurie in peace.
text.KJV: So they fled even unto the towers in the fields of Azotus; wherefore he burned it with fire: so that there were slain of them about two thousand men. Afterward he returned into the land of Judea in peace.
text.NABRE: Some took refuge in the towers on the plain of Azotus, but John set fire to these, and about two thousand of the enemy perished. He then returned to Judea in peace.
text.NRSV: They also fled into the towers that were in the fields of Azotus, and John burned it with fire, and about two thousand of them fell. He then returned to Judea safely.

text.DRB: And Ptolomee the son of Abobus was appointed captain in the plain of Jericho, and he had much silver, & gold.
text.KJV: Moreover in the plain of Jericho was Ptolemeus the son of Abubus made captain, and he had abundance of silver and gold:
text.NABRE: Murder of Simon and His Sons. Ptolemy, son of Abubus, had been appointed governor of the plain of Jericho, and he had much silver and gold,
text.NRSV: Murder of Simon and His Sons Now Ptolemy son of Abubus had been appointed governor over the plain of Jericho; he had a large store of silver and gold,

text.DRB: For he was the son in law of the high-Priest.
text.KJV: For he was the high priest’s son in law.
text.NABRE: being the son-in-law of the high priest.
text.NRSV: for he was son-in-law of the high priest.

text.DRB: And his heart was exalted, and he would gain the country, and he purposed treachery against Simon and his sons to dispatch them.
text.KJV: Wherefore his heart being lifted up, he thought to get the country to himself, and thereupon consulted deceitfully against Simon and his sons to destroy them.
text.NABRE: But his heart became proud and he was determined to get control of the country. So he made treacherous plans to do away with Simon and his sons.
text.NRSV: His heart was lifted up; he determined to get control of the country, and made treacherous plans against Simon and his sons, to do away with them.

text.KJV: Now Simon was visiting the cities that were in the country, and taking care for the good ordering of them; at which time he came down himself to Jericho with his sons, Mattathias and Judas, in the hundred threescore and seventeenth year, in the eleventh month, called Sabat:
text.NABRE: As Simon was inspecting the cities of the country and providing for their needs, he and his sons Mattathias and Judas went down to Jericho in the one hundred and seventy-seventh year, in the eleventh month (that is, the month Shebat).
text.NRSV: Now Simon was visiting the towns of the country and attending to their needs, and he went down to Jericho with his sons Mattathias and Judas, in the one hundred and seventy-seventh year, in the eleventh month, which is the month of Shebat.
text.DRB: And Simon walking through the cities, that were in the country of Jurie, and being careful of them, went down into Jericho, he and Mathathias his son, and Judas, the year an hundreth seventy seven, the eleventh month: this is the month Sabath.
And the son of Abobus received them with guile into a little fortress, that is called Doch, which he built, and he made them a great feast, and hid men there.
text.KJV: Where the son of Abubus receiving them deceitfully into a little hold, called Docus, which he had built, made them a great banquet: howbeit he had hid men there.
text.NABRE: The son of Abubus gave them a deceitful welcome in the little stronghold called Dok which he had built. He served them a sumptuous banquet, but he had his men hidden there.
text.NRSV: The son of Abubus received them treacherously in the little stronghold called Dok, which he had built; he gave them a great banquet, and hid men there.

text.KJV: So when Simon and his sons had drunk largely, Ptolemee and his men rose up, and took their weapons, and came upon Simon into the banqueting place, and slew him, and his two sons, and certain of his servants.
text.NABRE: Then, when Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men sprang up, weapons in hand, rushed upon Simon in the banquet hall, and killed him, his two sons, and some of his servants.
text.NRSV: When Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men rose up, took their weapons, rushed in against Simon in the banqueting-hall and killed him and his two sons, as well as some of his servants.
text.DRB: And when Simon was inebriated & his sons, Ptolomee arose with his company, and they took their weapons, and entered into the feast, and slew him, and his two sons, and certain of his servants.
And he did great deceit in Israel, and rendered evil for good.
text.KJV: In which doing he committed a great treachery, and recompensed evil for good.
text.NABRE: By this vicious act of treachery he repaid good with evil.
text.NRSV: So he committed an act of great treachery and returned evil for good.

text.DRB: And Ptolomee wrote these things, and sent to the king that he should send him an army for aid, and he would deliver him the country, and their cities, and tributes.
text.KJV: Then Ptolemee wrote these things, and sent to the king, that he should send him an host to aid him, and he would deliver him the country and cities.
text.NABRE: Then Ptolemy wrote a report and sent it to the king, asking him to send troops to help him and to turn over to him their country and its cities.
text.NRSV: John Succeeds Simon Then Ptolemy wrote a report about these things and sent it to the king, asking him to send troops to aid him and to turn over to him the towns and the country.

text.DRB: And he sent others into Gazara to dispatch John: and to the tribunes he sent epistles, that they should come to him, and he would give them silver, and gold, and gifts.
text.KJV: He sent others also to Gazera to kill John: and unto the tribunes he sent letters to come unto him, that he might give them silver, and gold, and rewards.
text.NABRE: He sent other men to Gazara to do away with John. To the army officers he sent letters inviting them to come to him so that he might present them with silver, gold, and gifts.
text.NRSV: He sent other troops to Gazara to do away with John; he sent letters to the captains asking them to come to him so that he might give them silver and gold and gifts;

text.DRB: And he sent others to take Jerusalem, and the mount of the temple.
text.KJV: And others he sent to take Jerusalem, and the mountain of the temple.
text.NABRE: He also sent others to seize Jerusalem and the temple mount.
text.NRSV: and he sent other troops to take possession of Jerusalem and the temple hill.

text.DRB: And one running before told John in Gazara, that his father was perished, and his brethren, and that he hath sent that thou also mayst be slain.
text.KJV: Now one had run afore to Gazera and told John that his father and brethren were slain, and, quoth he, Ptolemee hath sent to slay thee also.
text.NABRE: But someone ran ahead and brought word to John at Gazara that his father and his brothers had perished, and “Ptolemy has sent men to kill you also.”
text.NRSV: But someone ran ahead and reported to John at Gazara that his father and brothers had perished, and that ‘he has sent men to kill you also.’

text.DRB: But as he heard it, he was exceedingly afraid: and he apprehended the men, that came to destroy him, and he slew them: for he knew that they sought to destroy him.
text.KJV: Hereof when he heard, he was sore astonished: so he laid hands on them that were come to destroy him, and slew them; for he knew that they sought to make him away.
text.NABRE: On hearing this, John was utterly astounded. When the men came to kill him, he seized them and put them to death, for he knew that they sought to kill him.
text.NRSV: When he heard this, he was greatly shocked; he seized the men who came to destroy him and killed them, for he had found out that they were seeking to destroy him.

text.KJV: As concerning the rest of the acts of John, and his wars, and worthy deeds which he did, and the building of the walls which he made, and his doings,
text.NABRE: Now the rest of the acts of John, his wars and the brave deeds he performed, his rebuilding of the walls, and all his achievements—
text.NRSV: The rest of the acts of John and his wars, and the brave deeds that he did, and the building of the walls that he completed, and his achievements,
text.DRB: And the rest concerning the words of John, and his battles, and the valiant acts, which he did manfully, and the building of the walls, which he built, and the things that he did:
lo these are written in the book of the days of his priesthood, from the time that he was made prince of the priests after his father.
text.KJV: Behold, these are written in the chronicles of his priesthood, from the time he was made high priest after his father.
text.NABRE: these are recorded in the chronicle of his high priesthood, from the time that he succeeded his father as high priest.
text.NRSV: are written in the annals of his high-priesthood, from the time that he became high priest after his father.
